Test 7: CSG Union of Two Polyhedra (C++) Score: 1.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are implementing a CSG (Constructive Solid Geometry) Union operation in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY mesh complexity from trivial (6 vertex unit cubes) to
ludicrous scale (millions of vertices). The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: VA FA VB FB (vertex/face counts for mesh A and B)
Next VA lines: x y z (mesh A vertices)
Next FA lines: a b c (mesh A faces, 0-indexed)
Next VB lines: x y z (mesh B vertices)
Next FB lines: a b c (mesh B faces)

You can assume that the input geometry is clean, manifold, watertight, and free of self-intersections.

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: V F (vertex and face counts for union mesh)
Next V lines: x y z
Next F lines: a b c (0-indexed triangle indices)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on mesh complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for very complex meshes
3. **Correctness**: Must output correct union geometry. Must be watertight, connected,
manifold, free of holes and self intersections..

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given two overlapping 3D shapes (triangle meshes), merge them into a single combined shape — like gluing two lumps of clay together and keeping only the outer surface. The output mesh must be watertight with no self-intersections.

The AI must correctly handle the tricky geometry where the two shapes overlap: splitting triangles, discarding interior faces, and stitching everything back together. Subpasses use increasingly complex shape pairs.

 1.0000
[overlapping_boxes] Result vol: 15.00, Expected (MC): ~15.04, Inputs: 8.00 + 8.00, Time: 0.2s - excellent (< 1% error). Topology: clean

CSG Union - overlapping_boxes

Approach: CSG union solver for two watertight triangle meshes. Fast paths: disjoint / containment detection with BVH + ray casting. General path: classic BSP-based polygon boolean union.

View Code (28381 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <cmath>
#include <cstdint>
#include <initializer_list>
#include <iomanip>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <memory>
#include <numeric>
#include <random>
#include <stack>
#include <unordered_map>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using std::array;
using std::cout;
using std::size_t;
using std::vector;

struct Vec3 {
    double x = 0.0, y = 0.0, z = 0.0;

    Vec3() = default;
    Vec3(double xx, double yy, double zz) : x(xx), y(yy), z(zz) {}

    Vec3 operator+(const Vec3& o) const { return Vec3{x + o.x, y + o.y, z + o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator-(const Vec3& o) const { return Vec3{x - o.x, y - o.y, z - o.z}; }
    Vec3 operator*(double s) const { return Vec3{x * s, y * s, z * s}; }
    Vec3 operator/(double s) const { return Vec3{x / s, y / s, z / s}; }
    Vec3& operator+=(const Vec3& o) { x += o.x; y += o.y; z += o.z; return *this; }
    Vec3& operator-=(const Vec3& o) { x -= o.x; y -= o.y; z -= o.z; return *this; }
};

static inline Vec3 operator*(double s, const Vec3& v) { return v * s; }

static inline double dot(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z;
}

static inline Vec3 cross(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return Vec3{
        a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y,
        a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z,
        a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x
    };
}

static inline double length2(const Vec3& v) { return dot(v, v); }
static inline double length(const Vec3& v) { return std::sqrt(length2(v)); }

static inline Vec3 normalized(const Vec3& v) {
    double len = length(v);
    if (len <= 0.0) {
        return Vec3{0.0, 0.0, 0.0};
    }
    return v / len;
}

static inline Vec3 lerp(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, double t) {
    return a + (b - a) * t;
}

static inline double dist2(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return length2(a - b);
}

struct AABB {
    Vec3 mn, mx;

    AABB() {
        reset();
    }

    void reset() {
        mn = Vec3{
            std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
            std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
            std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity()
        };
        mx = Vec3{
            -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
            -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity(),
            -std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity()
        };
    }

    void expand(const Vec3& p) {
        mn.x = std::min(mn.x, p.x); mn.y = std::min(mn.y, p.y); mn.z = std::min(mn.z, p.z);
        mx.x = std::max(mx.x, p.x); mx.y = std::max(mx.y, p.y); mx.z = std::max(mx.z, p.z);
    }

    void expand(const AABB& b) {
        expand(b.mn);
        expand(b.mx);
    }

    bool valid() const {
        return mn.x <= mx.x && mn.y <= mx.y && mn.z <= mx.z;
    }
};

static inline bool boxesOverlap(const AABB& a, const AABB& b) {
    return !(a.mx.x < b.mn.x || a.mn.x > b.mx.x ||
             a.mx.y < b.mn.y || a.mn.y > b.mx.y ||
             a.mx.z < b.mn.z || a.mn.z > b.mx.z);
}

struct Face {
    int a = 0, b = 0, c = 0;
};

struct Triangle {
    int a = 0, b = 0, c = 0;
    Vec3 centroid;
    AABB box;
};

struct Polygon {
    vector<Vec3> v;
    Vec3 normal;

    void flip() {
        std::reverse(v.begin(), v.end());
        normal = Vec3{-normal.x, -normal.y, -normal.z};
    }
};

static double gClassEps = 1e-12;
static double gMergeEps = 1e-9;
static double gAreaEps2 = 1e-24;

static inline Vec3 polygonNormal(const vector<Vec3>& pts) {
    if (pts.size() < 3) {
        return Vec3{0.0, 0.0, 0.0};
    }
    Vec3 n = cross(pts[1] - pts[0], pts[2] - pts[0]);
    return normalized(n);
}

static inline bool pointClose(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b) {
    return dist2(a, b) <= gMergeEps * gMergeEps;
}

static void simplifyPolygon(Polygon& p) {
    if (p.v.size() < 3) {
        p.v.clear();
        return;
    }

    vector<Vec3> tmp;
    tmp.reserve(p.v.size());
    for (const Vec3& pt : p.v) {
        if (tmp.empty() || !pointClose(tmp.back(), pt)) {
            tmp.push_back(pt);
        }
    }
    if (tmp.size() > 1 && pointClose(tmp.front(), tmp.back())) {
        tmp.pop_back();
    }

    if (tmp.size() < 3) {
        p.v.clear();
        return;
    }

    vector<Vec3> out;
    out.reserve(tmp.size());
    for (size_t i = 0; i < tmp.size(); ++i) {
        const Vec3& a = tmp[(i + tmp.size() - 1) % tmp.size()];
        const Vec3& b = tmp[i];
        const Vec3& c = tmp[(i + 1) % tmp.size()];
        if (pointClose(a, b) || pointClose(b, c)) {
            continue;
        }
        Vec3 cr = cross(b - a, c - b);
        if (length2(cr) <= gAreaEps2) {
            continue;
        }
        out.push_back(b);
    }

    if (out.size() < 3) {
        p.v.clear();
        return;
    }
    p.v.swap(out);
}

static inline bool polygonHasArea(const Polygon& p) {
    if (p.v.size() < 3) {
        return false;
    }
    const Vec3& base = p.v[0];
    for (size_t i = 1; i + 1 < p.v.size(); ++i) {
        Vec3 cr = cross(p.v[i] - base, p.v[i + 1] - base);
        if (length2(cr) > gAreaEps2) {
            return true;
        }
    }
    return false;
}

static inline double triangleArea2(const Vec3& a, const Vec3& b, const Vec3& c) {
    return length2(cross(b - a, c - a));
}

struct Plane {
    Vec3 n;
    double d = 0.0;
    bool valid = false;

    static Plane fromPolygon(const Polygon& p) {
        Plane pl;
        pl.n = p.normal;
        pl.d = dot(pl.n, p.v[0]);
        pl.valid = true;
        return pl;
    }

    void flip() {
        n = Vec3{-n.x, -n.y, -n.z};
        d = -d;
    }

    double dist(const Vec3& p) const {
        return dot(n, p) - d;
    }

    enum { COPLANAR = 0, FRONT = 1, BACK = 2, SPANNING = 3 };

    void splitPolygon(
        const Polygon& poly,
        vector<Polygon>& coplanarFront,
        vector<Polygon>& coplanarBack,
        vector<Polygon>& front,
        vector<Polygon>& back
    ) const {
        const size_t nverts = poly.v.size();
        if (nverts < 3) {
            return;
        }

        vector<double> distv(nverts);
        vector<int> types(nverts);
        int polygonType = 0;

        for (size_t i = 0; i < nverts; ++i) {
            double dv = dist(poly.v[i]);
            distv[i] = dv;
            int t = (dv < -gClassEps) ? BACK : (dv > gClassEps ? FRONT : COPLANAR);
            types[i] = t;
            polygonType |= t;
        }

        if (polygonType == COPLANAR) {
            if (dot(n, poly.normal) >= 0.0) {
                coplanarFront.push_back(poly);
            } else {
                coplanarBack.push_back(poly);
            }
            return;
        }

        if (polygonType == FRONT) {
            front.push_back(poly);
            return;
        }

        if (polygonType == BACK) {
            back.push_back(poly);
            return;
        }

        Polygon f;
        Polygon b;
        f.normal = poly.normal;
        b.normal = poly.normal;
        f.v.reserve(nverts + 2);
        b.v.reserve(nverts + 2);

        for (size_t i = 0; i < nverts; ++i) {
            size_t j = (i + 1) % nverts;
            const Vec3& vi = poly.v[i];
            const Vec3& vj = poly.v[j];
            int ti = types[i];
            int tj = types[j];

            if (ti != BACK) {
                f.v.push_back(vi);
            }
            if (ti != FRONT) {
                b.v.push_back(vi);
            }

            if ((ti | tj) == SPANNING) {
                double t = distv[i] / (distv[i] - distv[j]);
                Vec3 v = lerp(vi, vj, t);
                f.v.push_back(v);
                b.v.push_back(v);
            }
        }

        simplifyPolygon(f);
        simplifyPolygon(b);

        if (f.v.size() >= 3 && polygonHasArea(f)) {
            front.push_back(std::move(f));
        }
        if (b.v.size() >= 3 && polygonHasArea(b)) {
            back.push_back(std::move(b));
        }
    }
};

struct BSPNode {
    Plane plane;
    vector<Polygon> polygons;
    std::unique_ptr<BSPNode> front;
    std::unique_ptr<BSPNode> back;

    void build(vector<Polygon> polys) {
        if (polys.empty()) {
            return;
        }
        if (!plane.valid) {
            plane = Plane::fromPolygon(polys[0]);
        }

        vector<Polygon> frontList;
        vector<Polygon> backList;
        frontList.reserve(polys.size());
        backList.reserve(polys.size());

        for (const Polygon& p : polys) {
            plane.splitPolygon(p, polygons, polygons, frontList, backList);
        }

        if (!frontList.empty()) {
            if (!front) {
                front = std::make_unique<BSPNode>();
            }
            front->build(std::move(frontList));
        }
        if (!backList.empty()) {
            if (!back) {
                back = std::make_unique<BSPNode>();
            }
            back->build(std::move(backList));
        }
    }

    vector<Polygon> clipPolygons(const vector<Polygon>& polys) const {
        if (!plane.valid) {
            return polys;
        }

        vector<Polygon> frontList;
        vector<Polygon> backList;
        frontList.reserve(polys.size());
        backList.reserve(polys.size());

        for (const Polygon& p : polys) {
            plane.splitPolygon(p, frontList, backList, frontList, backList);
        }

        if (front) {
            frontList = front->clipPolygons(frontList);
        }
        if (back) {
            backList = back->clipPolygons(backList);
        } else {
            backList.clear();
        }

        frontList.insert(frontList.end(), backList.begin(), backList.end());
        return frontList;
    }

    void clipTo(const BSPNode& bsp) {
        polygons = bsp.clipPolygons(polygons);
        if (front) {
            front->clipTo(bsp);
        }
        if (back) {
            back->clipTo(bsp);
        }
    }

    void invert() {
        if (!plane.valid) {
            return;
        }
        for (Polygon& p : polygons) {
            p.flip();
        }
        plane.flip();
        if (front) {
            front->invert();
        }
        if (back) {
            back->invert();
        }
        std::swap(front, back);
    }

    void allPolygons(vector<Polygon>& out) const {
        out.insert(out.end(), polygons.begin(), polygons.end());
        if (front) {
            front->allPolygons(out);
        }
        if (back) {
            back->allPolygons(out);
        }
    }

    vector<Polygon> allPolygons() const {
        vector<Polygon> out;
        allPolygons(out);
        return out;
    }
};

struct BVHNode {
    AABB box;
    int left = -1;
    int right = -1;
    int start = 0;
    int count = 0;

    bool leaf() const { return left < 0 && right < 0; }
};

struct BVH {
    const vector<Vec3>* verts = nullptr;
    const vector<Triangle>* tris = nullptr;
    vector<int> order;
    vector<BVHNode> nodes;
    int root = -1;
    int leafSize = 8;

    void build(const vector<Vec3>* vertsPtr, const vector<Triangle>* trisPtr) {
        verts = vertsPtr;
        tris = trisPtr;
        nodes.clear();
        order.clear();
        root = -1;

        if (!tris || tris->empty()) {
            return;
        }

        const int n = static_cast<int>(tris->size());
        order.resize(n);
        std::iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
        nodes.reserve(static_cast<size_t>(n) * 2u);
        root = buildRec(0, n);
    }

    int buildRec(int l, int r) {
        int idx = static_cast<int>(nodes.size());
        nodes.emplace_back();

        AABB box;
        AABB centroidBox;
        box.reset();
        centroidBox.reset();
        for (int i = l; i < r; ++i) {
            const Triangle& t = (*tris)[order[i]];
            box.expand(t.box);
            centroidBox.expand(t.centroid);
        }

        BVHNode node;
        node.box = box;
        node.start = l;
        node.count = r - l;

        if (r - l <= leafSize) {
            nodes[idx] = node;
            return idx;
        }

        Vec3 ext = centroidBox.mx - centroidBox.mn;
        int axis = 0;
        if (ext.y > ext.x && ext.y >= ext.z) {
            axis = 1;
        } else if (ext.z > ext.x && ext.z > ext.y) {
            axis = 2;
        }

        int m = (l + r) / 2;
        auto cmp = [&](int ia, int ib) {
            const Vec3& ca = (*tris)[ia].centroid;
            const Vec3& cb = (*tris)[ib].centroid;
            if (axis == 0) return ca.x < cb.x;
            if (axis == 1) return ca.y < cb.y;
            return ca.z < cb.z;
        };
        std::nth_element(order.begin() + l, order.begin() + m, order.begin() + r, cmp);

        node.left = buildRec(l, m);
        node.right = buildRec(m, r);
        node.box = nodes[node.left].box;
        node.box.expand(nodes[node.right].box);

        nodes[idx] = node;
        return idx;
    }

    bool rayBoxHit(const Vec3& o, const Vec3& d, const AABB& b) const {
        double tmin = 0.0;
        double tmax = std::numeric_limits<double>::infinity();

        auto axisTest = [&](double origin, double dir, double mn, double mx) -> bool {
            if (std::fabs(dir) < 1e-18) {
                return origin >= mn && origin <= mx;
            }
            double inv = 1.0 / dir;
            double t1 = (mn - origin) * inv;
            double t2 = (mx - origin) * inv;
            if (t1 > t2) {
                std::swap(t1, t2);
            }
            tmin = std::max(tmin, t1);
            tmax = std::min(tmax, t2);
            return tmin <= tmax;
        };

        if (!axisTest(o.x, d.x, b.mn.x, b.mx.x)) return false;
        if (!axisTest(o.y, d.y, b.mn.y, b.mx.y)) return false;
        if (!axisTest(o.z, d.z, b.mn.z, b.mx.z)) return false;
        return tmax >= std::max(0.0, tmin);
    }

    bool rayTriHit(const Vec3& o, const Vec3& d, const Triangle& t) const {
        const Vec3& v0 = (*verts)[t.a];
        const Vec3& v1 = (*verts)[t.b];
        const Vec3& v2 = (*verts)[t.c];

        Vec3 e1 = v1 - v0;
        Vec3 e2 = v2 - v0;
        Vec3 p = cross(d, e2);
        double det = dot(e1, p);
        if (std::fabs(det) < gClassEps) {
            return false;
        }
        double invDet = 1.0 / det;
        Vec3 s = o - v0;
        double u = dot(s, p) * invDet;
        if (u <= gClassEps || u >= 1.0 - gClassEps) {
            return false;
        }
        Vec3 q = cross(s, e1);
        double v = dot(d, q) * invDet;
        if (v <= gClassEps || u + v >= 1.0 - gClassEps) {
            return false;
        }
        double tt = dot(e2, q) * invDet;
        return tt > gClassEps;
    }

    int rayCount(const Vec3& o, const Vec3& d) const {
        if (root < 0) {
            return 0;
        }
        int hits = 0;
        std::vector<int> stack;
        stack.push_back(root);
        while (!stack.empty()) {
            int ni = stack.back();
            stack.pop_back();
            const BVHNode& node = nodes[ni];
            if (!rayBoxHit(o, d, node.box)) {
                continue;
            }
            if (node.leaf()) {
                for (int i = 0; i < node.count; ++i) {
                    const Triangle& t = (*tris)[order[node.start + i]];
                    if (rayTriHit(o, d, t)) {
                        ++hits;
                    }
                }
            } else {
                if (node.left >= 0) stack.push_back(node.left);
                if (node.right >= 0) stack.push_back(node.right);
            }
        }
        return hits;
    }
};

struct Mesh {
    vector<Vec3> verts;
    vector<Face> faces;
    vector<Triangle> tris;
    vector<Polygon> polys;
    BVH bvh;

    void buildDerived() {
        tris.clear();
        polys.clear();
        tris.reserve(faces.size());
        polys.reserve(faces.size());

        for (const Face& f : faces) {
            if (f.a < 0 || f.b < 0 || f.c < 0) {
                continue;
            }
            if (f.a >= static_cast<int>(verts.size()) ||
                f.b >= static_cast<int>(verts.size()) ||
                f.c >= static_cast<int>(verts.size())) {
                continue;
            }

            const Vec3& a = verts[f.a];
            const Vec3& b = verts[f.b];
            const Vec3& c = verts[f.c];

            Vec3 n = cross(b - a, c - a);
            if (length2(n) <= gAreaEps2) {
                continue;
            }

            Triangle t;
            t.a = f.a;
            t.b = f.b;
            t.c = f.c;
            t.centroid = (a + b + c) / 3.0;
            t.box.reset();
            t.box.expand(a);
            t.box.expand(b);
            t.box.expand(c);
            tris.push_back(t);

            Polygon p;
            p.v = {a, b, c};
            p.normal = normalized(n);
            polys.push_back(std::move(p));
        }

        bvh.build(&verts, &tris);
    }
};

static bool bvhPotentialOverlap(const BVH& a, const BVH& b) {
    if (a.root < 0 || b.root < 0) {
        return false;
    }
    if (!boxesOverlap(a.nodes[a.root].box, b.nodes[b.root].box)) {
        return false;
    }

    std::vector<std::pair<int, int>> stack;
    stack.reserve(64);
    stack.emplace_back(a.root, b.root);

    while (!stack.empty()) {
        auto [ia, ib] = stack.back();
        stack.pop_back();

        const BVHNode& na = a.nodes[ia];
        const BVHNode& nb = b.nodes[ib];
        if (!boxesOverlap(na.box, nb.box)) {
            continue;
        }

        if (na.leaf() && nb.leaf()) {
            for (int i = 0; i < na.count; ++i) {
                const Triangle& ta = (*a.tris)[a.order[na.start + i]];
                for (int j = 0; j < nb.count; ++j) {
                    const Triangle& tb = (*b.tris)[b.order[nb.start + j]];
                    if (boxesOverlap(ta.box, tb.box)) {
                        return true;
                    }
                }
            }
            continue;
        }

        if (na.leaf()) {
            stack.emplace_back(ia, nb.left);
            stack.emplace_back(ia, nb.right);
            continue;
        }

        if (nb.leaf()) {
            stack.emplace_back(na.left, ib);
            stack.emplace_back(na.right, ib);
            continue;
        }

        double va = (na.box.mx.x - na.box.mn.x) * (na.box.mx.y - na.box.mn.y) * (na.box.mx.z - na.box.mn.z);
        double vb = (nb.box.mx.x - nb.box.mn.x) * (nb.box.mx.y - nb.box.mn.y) * (nb.box.mx.z - nb.box.mn.z);

        if (va >= vb) {
            stack.emplace_back(na.left, ib);
            stack.emplace_back(na.right, ib);
        } else {
            stack.emplace_back(ia, nb.left);
            stack.emplace_back(ia, nb.right);
        }
    }
    return false;
}

static int classifyPointInMesh(const Mesh& mesh, const Vec3& p) {
    if (mesh.bvh.root < 0) {
        return 0;
    }

    const Vec3 rayDirs[3] = {
        normalized(Vec3{0.873456789, 0.421234567, 0.246813579}),
        normalized(Vec3{-0.531975308, 0.817345679, 0.219753086}),
        normalized(Vec3{0.331245679, -0.272468135, 0.903157894})
    };

    int votesInside = 0;
    int votesOutside = 0;
    for (const Vec3& d : rayDirs) {
        int hits = mesh.bvh.rayCount(p, d);
        if (hits & 1) {
            ++votesInside;
        } else {
            ++votesOutside;
        }
    }

    if (votesInside >= 2) {
        return 1;
    }
    if (votesOutside >= 2) {
        return 0;
    }
    return -1;
}

struct OutputTri {
    Vec3 a, b, c;
};

struct CellKey {
    long long x = 0, y = 0, z = 0;
    bool operator==(const CellKey& o) const noexcept {
        return x == o.x && y == o.y && z == o.z;
    }
};

struct CellHash {
    static uint64_t mix(uint64_t x) {
        x += 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL;
        x = (x ^ (x >> 30)) * 0xbf58476d1ce4e5b9ULL;
        x = (x ^ (x >> 27)) * 0x94d049bb133111ebULL;
        return x ^ (x >> 31);
    }

    size_t operator()(const CellKey& k) const noexcept {
        uint64_t h1 = mix(static_cast<uint64_t>(k.x));
        uint64_t h2 = mix(static_cast<uint64_t>(k.y) ^ 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL);
        uint64_t h3 = mix(static_cast<uint64_t>(k.z) ^ 0xbf58476d1ce4e5b9ULL);
        return static_cast<size_t>(h1 ^ (h2 << 1) ^ (h3 << 7));
    }
};

struct VertexDeduper {
    double eps = 1e-9;
    double eps2 = 1e-18;
    std::unordered_map<CellKey, vector<int>, CellHash> cells;
    vector<Vec3> verts;

    explicit VertexDeduper(double e = 1e-9) : eps(e), eps2(e * e) {}

    CellKey keyFor(const Vec3& p) const {
        return CellKey{
            static_cast<long long>(std::floor(p.x / eps)),
            static_cast<long long>(std::floor(p.y / eps)),
            static_cast<long long>(std::floor(p.z / eps))
        };
    }

    int add(const Vec3& p) {
        CellKey base = keyFor(p);
        for (long long dx = -1; dx <= 1; ++dx) {
            for (long long dy = -1; dy <= 1; ++dy) {
                for (long long dz = -1; dz <= 1; ++dz) {
                    CellKey k{base.x + dx, base.y + dy, base.z + dz};
                    auto it = cells.find(k);
                    if (it == cells.end()) {
                        continue;
                    }
                    for (int idx : it->second) {
                        if (dist2(verts[idx], p) <= eps2) {
                            return idx;
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        int id = static_cast<int>(verts.size());
        verts.push_back(p);
        cells[base].push_back(id);
        return id;
    }
};

static vector<OutputTri> emitMesh(const Mesh& mesh) {
    vector<OutputTri> out;
    out.reserve(mesh.faces.size());
    for (const Face& f : mesh.faces) {
        if (f.a < 0 || f.b < 0 || f.c < 0) {
            continue;
        }
        if (f.a >= static_cast<int>(mesh.verts.size()) ||
            f.b >= static_cast<int>(mesh.verts.size()) ||
            f.c >= static_cast<int>(mesh.verts.size())) {
            continue;
        }
        const Vec3& a = mesh.verts[f.a];
        const Vec3& b = mesh.verts[f.b];
        const Vec3& c = mesh.verts[f.c];
        if (triangleArea2(a, b, c) <= gAreaEps2) {
            continue;
        }
        out.push_back(OutputTri{a, b, c});
    }
    return out;
}

static void addPolygonTriangles(const Polygon& p, vector<OutputTri>& out) {
    if (p.v.size() < 3) {
        return;
    }
    const Vec3& a = p.v[0];
    for (size_t i = 1; i + 1 < p.v.size(); ++i) {
        const Vec3& b = p.v[i];
        const Vec3& c = p.v[i + 1];
        if (triangleArea2(a, b, c) <= gAreaEps2) {
            continue;
        }
        out.push_back(OutputTri{a, b, c});
    }
}

static void emitMeshAsPolygonTriangles(const vector<Polygon>& polys, vector<OutputTri>& out) {
    for (const Polygon& p : polys) {
        addPolygonTriangles(p, out);
    }
}

static void writeOutput(const vector<OutputTri>& tris) {
    VertexDeduper dedup(gMergeEps);
    dedup.cells.reserve(tris.size() * 2 + 8);
    dedup.verts.reserve(tris.size() * 3);
    vector<array<int, 3>> faces;
    faces.reserve(tris.size());

    for (const OutputTri& t : tris) {
        int ia = dedup.add(t.a);
        int ib = dedup.add(t.b);
        int ic = dedup.add(t.c);
        if (ia == ib || ib == ic || ia == ic) {
            continue;
        }
        faces.push_back(array<int, 3>{ia, ib, ic});
    }

    vector<int> remap(dedup.verts.size(), -1);
    vector<Vec3> verts;
    verts.reserve(dedup.verts.size());
    for (auto& f : faces) {
        for (int k = 0; k < 3; ++k) {
            int idx = f[k];
            if (remap[idx] < 0) {
                remap[idx] = static_cast<int>(verts.size());
                verts.push_back(dedup.verts[idx]);
            }
            f[k] = remap[idx];
        }
    }

    cout << verts.size() << ' ' << faces.size() << '\n';
    cout.setf(std::ios::fmtflags(0), std::ios::floatfield);
    cout << std::setprecision(17);
    for (const Vec3& v : verts) {
        cout << v.x << ' ' << v.y << ' ' << v.z << '\n';
    }
    for (const auto& f : faces) {
        cout << f[0] << ' ' << f[1] << ' ' << f[2] << '\n';
    }
}

static void writeRawMesh(const Mesh& mesh) {
    cout << mesh.verts.size() << ' ' << mesh.faces.size() << '\n';
    cout.setf(std::ios::fmtflags(0), std::ios::floatfield);
    cout << std::setprecision(17);
    for (const Vec3& v : mesh.verts) {
        cout << v.x << ' ' << v.y << ' ' << v.z << '\n';
    }
    for (const Face& f : mesh.faces) {
        cout << f.a << ' ' << f.b << ' ' << f.c << '\n';
    }
}

static void writeRawUnion(const Mesh& a, const Mesh& b) {
    cout << (a.verts.size() + b.verts.size()) << ' ' << (a.faces.size() + b.faces.size()) << '\n';
    cout.setf(std::ios::fmtflags(0), std::ios::floatfield);
    cout << std::setprecision(17);
    for (const Vec3& v : a.verts) {
        cout << v.x << ' ' << v.y << ' ' << v.z << '\n';
    }
    for (const Vec3& v : b.verts) {
        cout << v.x << ' ' << v.y << ' ' << v.z << '\n';
    }
    for (const Face& f : a.faces) {
        cout << f.a << ' ' << f.b << ' ' << f.c << '\n';
    }
    const int offset = static_cast<int>(a.verts.size());
    for (const Face& f : b.faces) {
        cout << (f.a + offset) << ' ' << (f.b + offset) << ' ' << (f.c + offset) << '\n';
    }
}

static double meshBoundingScale(const Mesh& a, const Mesh& b) {
    AABB box;
    box.reset();
    for (const Vec3& v : a.verts) {
        box.expand(v);
    }
    for (const Vec3& v : b.verts) {
        box.expand(v);
    }
    if (!box.valid()) {
        return 1.0;
    }
    Vec3 ext = box.mx - box.mn;
    double scale = std::max({std::fabs(ext.x), std::fabs(ext.y), std::fabs(ext.z), 1.0});
    return scale;
}

static Vec3 faceCentroid(const Mesh& mesh, const Face& f) {
    return (mesh.verts[f.a] + mesh.verts[f.b] + mesh.verts[f.c]) / 3.0;
}

int main() {
    std::ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    std::cin.tie(nullptr);

    int VA = 0, FA = 0, VB = 0, FB = 0;
    if (!(std::cin >> VA >> FA >> VB >> FB)) {
        return 0;
    }

    Mesh A, B;
    A.verts.resize(std::max(VA, 0));
    B.verts.resize(std::max(VB, 0));

    for (int i = 0; i < VA; ++i) {
        std::cin >> A.verts[i].x >> A.verts[i].y >> A.verts[i].z;
    }
    A.faces.clear();
    A.faces.reserve(std::max(FA, 0));
    for (int i = 0; i < FA; ++i) {
        Face f;
        std::cin >> f.a >> f.b >> f.c;
        A.faces.push_back(f);
    }

    for (int i = 0; i < VB; ++i) {
        std::cin >> B.verts[i].x >> B.verts[i].y >> B.verts[i].z;
    }
    B.faces.reserve(std::max(FB, 0));
    for (int i = 0; i < FB; ++i) {
        Face f;
        std::cin >> f.a >> f.b >> f.c;
        B.faces.push_back(f);
    }

    // Union is symmetric. Put the smaller face set first to keep the BSP work modest.
    if (A.faces.size() > B.faces.size()) {
        std::swap(A, B);
    }

    double scale = meshBoundingScale(A, B);
    gClassEps = std::max(scale * 1e-12, 1e-12);
    gMergeEps = std::max(scale * 1e-11, 1e-12);
    gAreaEps2 = std::max(scale * scale * 1e-24, 1e-30);

    A.buildDerived();
    B.buildDerived();

    if (A.tris.empty() && B.tris.empty()) {
        cout << "0 0\n";
        return 0;
    }
    if (A.tris.empty()) {
        writeRawMesh(B);
        return 0;
    }
    if (B.tris.empty()) {
        writeRawMesh(A);
        return 0;
    }

    bool potentialOverlap = bvhPotentialOverlap(A.bvh, B.bvh);

    if (!potentialOverlap) {
        int aInB = classifyPointInMesh(B, faceCentroid(A, A.faces[0]));
        int bInA = classifyPointInMesh(A, faceCentroid(B, B.faces[0]));

        if (aInB == 1 && bInA == 0) {
            writeRawMesh(B);
            return 0;
        }
        if (bInA == 1 && aInB == 0) {
            writeRawMesh(A);
            return 0;
        }
        if (aInB == 0 && bInA == 0) {
            writeRawUnion(A, B);
            return 0;
        }
        // Ambiguous boundary contact: fall through to the general boolean.
    }

    std::mt19937_64 rng(0xC0FFEEULL);
    std::shuffle(A.polys.begin(), A.polys.end(), rng);
    std::shuffle(B.polys.begin(), B.polys.end(), rng);

    BSPNode aTree;
    BSPNode bTree;
    aTree.build(A.polys);
    bTree.build(B.polys);

    aTree.clipTo(bTree);
    bTree.clipTo(aTree);
    bTree.invert();
    bTree.clipTo(aTree);
    bTree.invert();
    aTree.build(bTree.allPolygons());

    vector<Polygon> result = aTree.allPolygons();
    vector<OutputTri> out;
    out.reserve(result.size() * 2);
    emitMeshAsPolygonTriangles(result, out);
    writeOutput(out);
    return 0;
}

Test 8: Maze Solver (Rust) Score: 14.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a maze solver in Rust that must handle ANY maze complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

Your program will be tested with mazes ranging from 5x5 to 50000x50000.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Maze format:**
- 'A' = Start position
- 'B' = Goal/End position
- '#' = Wall (cannot pass through)
- ' ' (space) = Open path (can walk through)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the maze row strings (each W characters)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of steps in path)
Next N lines: x y (0-indexed coordinates from top-left)
Path should include both start (A) and end (B) positions.
Movement is only up/down/left/right (no diagonals).
Output nothing (or 0) if no path exists.

**Constraints:**
- Path must be continuous (each step adjacent to previous)
- Path must not go through walls ('#')
- Path must start at 'A' and end at 'B'
- Path must not loop even if the maze allows it.
- If multiple solutions exist, return any one.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.

Find the shortest path through a maze from a start cell to an end cell. The maze is a grid of walls and open passages, and the AI must navigate from one corner to the other using the fewest steps possible.

Subpasses increase the maze size dramatically, testing whether the AI's approach scales to very large mazes. The baseline finds the true shortest path using breadth-first search.

 1.0000
[5x5] Path found, Time: 0.1s - path length 5

Maze Solver - 5x5

View Code (5504 chars)
use std::io::{self, BufRead, Write};

#[derive(Clone)]
struct BitSet {
    data: Vec<u64>,
}

impl BitSet {
    #[inline]
    fn new(bits: usize) -> Self {
        Self {
            data: vec![0; (bits + 63) >> 6],
        }
    }

    #[inline(always)]
    fn set(&mut self, idx: usize) {
        unsafe {
            let word = self.data.get_unchecked_mut(idx >> 6);
            *word |= 1u64 << (idx & 63);
        }
    }

    #[inline(always)]
    fn take(&mut self, idx: usize) -> bool {
        unsafe {
            let word = self.data.get_unchecked_mut(idx >> 6);
            let mask = 1u64 << (idx & 63);
            let present = (*word & mask) != 0;
            *word &= !mask;
            present
        }
    }
}

#[inline(always)]
fn pack_coord(row: usize, col: usize) -> u32 {
    ((row as u32) << 16) | (col as u32)
}

#[inline(always)]
fn ordered_dirs(row: usize, col: usize, goal_row: usize, goal_col: usize) -> [u8; 4] {
    let vert = if row < goal_row { 1 } else { 0 };
    let horiz = if col < goal_col { 3 } else { 2 };
    let vert_other = vert ^ 1;
    let horiz_other = if horiz == 2 { 3 } else { 2 };
    if goal_row.abs_diff(row) >= goal_col.abs_diff(col) {
        [vert, horiz, vert_other, horiz_other]
    } else {
        [horiz, vert, horiz_other, vert_other]
    }
}

fn main() {
    let stdin = io::stdin();
    let mut reader = io::BufReader::with_capacity(1 << 20, stdin.lock());
    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());

    let mut header = Vec::new();
    if reader.read_until(b'\n', &mut header).ok().filter(|&n| n > 0).is_none() {
        let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
        return;
    }
    let header_str = match std::str::from_utf8(&header) {
        Ok(s) => s,
        Err(_) => {
            let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
            return;
        }
    };
    let mut parts = header_str.split_whitespace();
    let h: usize = match parts.next().and_then(|s| s.parse().ok()) {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => {
            let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
            return;
        }
    };
    let w: usize = match parts.next().and_then(|s| s.parse().ok()) {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => {
            let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
            return;
        }
    };

    let total = match h.checked_mul(w) {
        Some(v) => v,
        None => {
            let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
            return;
        }
    };

    if total == 0 {
        let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
        return;
    }

    let mut open = BitSet::new(total);
    let mut start_idx: Option<usize> = None;
    let mut goal_idx: Option<usize> = None;

    let mut line = Vec::new();
    for r in 0..h {
        line.clear();
        if reader.read_until(b'\n', &mut line).ok().filter(|&n| n > 0).is_none() {
            break;
        }
        if line.last() == Some(&b'\n') {
            line.pop();
            if line.last() == Some(&b'\r') {
                line.pop();
            }
        }

        let row_base = r * w;
        let row_len = line.len().min(w);
        for (c, &b) in line[..row_len].iter().enumerate() {
            if b != b'#' {
                let idx = row_base + c;
                open.set(idx);
                if b == b'A' && start_idx.is_none() {
                    start_idx = Some(idx);
                } else if b == b'B' && goal_idx.is_none() {
                    goal_idx = Some(idx);
                }
            }
        }
    }

    let (start_idx, goal_idx) = match (start_idx, goal_idx) {
        (Some(s), Some(g)) => (s, g),
        _ => {
            let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
            return;
        }
    };

    let start_row = start_idx / w;
    let start_col = start_idx % w;
    let goal_row = goal_idx / w;
    let goal_col = goal_idx % w;

    // Mark the start as already visited so DFS never loops back into it.
    let _ = open.take(start_idx);

    let mut stack_coords: Vec<u32> = Vec::new();
    let mut stack_next: Vec<u8> = Vec::new();
    stack_coords.push(pack_coord(start_row, start_col));
    stack_next.push(0);

    let mut found = false;

    while let Some(&coord) = stack_coords.last() {
        let row = (coord >> 16) as usize;
        let col = (coord & 0xFFFF) as usize;
        let idx = row * w + col;

        if idx == goal_idx {
            found = true;
            break;
        }

        let next = *stack_next.last().unwrap();
        if next == 4 {
            stack_coords.pop();
            stack_next.pop();
            continue;
        }

        let order = ordered_dirs(row, col, goal_row, goal_col);
        let dir = order[next as usize];
        *stack_next.last_mut().unwrap() = next + 1;

        let (nr, nc, nidx) = match dir {
            0 if row > 0 => (row - 1, col, idx - w),
            1 if row + 1 < h => (row + 1, col, idx + w),
            2 if col > 0 => (row, col - 1, idx - 1),
            3 if col + 1 < w => (row, col + 1, idx + 1),
            _ => continue,
        };

        if open.take(nidx) {
            stack_coords.push(pack_coord(nr, nc));
            stack_next.push(0);
            if nidx == goal_idx {
                found = true;
                break;
            }
        }
    }

    if !found {
        let _ = writeln!(out, "0");
        return;
    }

    let _ = writeln!(out, "{}", stack_coords.len());
    for &coord in &stack_coords {
        let row = (coord >> 16) as usize;
        let col = (coord & 0xFFFF) as usize;
        let _ = writeln!(out, "{} {}", col, row);
    }
}

Test 9: 1D Cutting Stock - Minimum Waste (C#) Score: 13.50 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 1D Cutting Stock Problem in C#.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N stock_length
Line 2: N cut lengths separated by spaces

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next num_stocks lines: space-separated cut indices assigned to that stock (0-indexed)

**Example:**
For cuts=[30, 40, 50] and stock_length=100:
Input:
3 100
30 40 50

Output:
2
0 1
2

**Constraints:**
- Each cut must come from a single stock piece (no gluing)
- Multiple cuts can come from the same stock piece if they fit
- No cut can exceed stock_length
- Must handle varying numbers of cuts efficiently. You can use
multiple threads, you can micro-optimise code, you can write clever algorithms,
whatever it takes to handle the problem and solve it within 5 minutes even if
there are millions of cuts.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given fixed-length stock material and a shopping list of smaller pieces to cut, use as few stock pieces as possible. Every requested piece must be cut from somewhere, but any leftover material is wasted — so packing the cuts tightly matters.

This is essentially a bin-packing problem and is NP-hard. Subpasses scale from a handful of cuts up to millions, forcing the AI to write code that is both correct and fast. The baseline uses a simple first-fit-decreasing heuristic.

 1.0000
[5 cuts, stock=100] Stocks used: 2, Waste: 20, Time: 0.1s - (waste < 1 stock, no repack check)

Cutting Stock - 5 cuts, stock=100

View Code (6033 chars)
using System;
using System.IO;
using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;

public static class Program
{
    public static void Main()
    {
        var input = new FastScanner();
        int n = input.NextInt();
        int stockLength = input.NextInt();

        if (n <= 0)
        {
            var emptyOutput = new FastWriter();
            emptyOutput.WriteInt(0);
            emptyOutput.WriteLine();
            emptyOutput.Flush();
            return;
        }

        ulong[] items = new ulong[n];
        for (int i = 0; i < n; i++)
        {
            int length = input.NextInt();
            items[i] = ((ulong)(uint)length << 32) | (uint)i;
        }

        Array.Sort(items);

        int size = 1;
        while (size < n)
        {
            size <<= 1;
        }

        int[] segmentTree = new int[size << 1];
        Array.Fill(segmentTree, -1);

        int[] head = new int[n];
        int[] next = new int[n];
        int binCount = 0;

        for (int i = n - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            ulong packed = items[i];
            int index = (int)(packed & 0xFFFFFFFFu);
            int length = (int)(packed >> 32);

            if (binCount == 0 || segmentTree[1] < length)
            {
                int bin = binCount++;
                head[bin] = index;
                next[index] = -1;
                Update(segmentTree, size, bin, stockLength - length);
            }
            else
            {
                int bin = FindFirstFit(segmentTree, size, length);
                next[index] = head[bin];
                head[bin] = index;
                int remaining = segmentTree[size + bin] - length;
                Update(segmentTree, size, bin, remaining);
            }
        }

        var output = new FastWriter();
        output.WriteInt(binCount);
        output.WriteLine();
        for (int bin = 0; bin < binCount; bin++)
        {
            int current = head[bin];
            bool first = true;
            while (current != -1)
            {
                if (!first)
                {
                    output.WriteSpace();
                }
                output.WriteInt(current);
                first = false;
                current = next[current];
            }
            output.WriteLine();
        }
        output.Flush();
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    private static int FindFirstFit(int[] segmentTree, int size, int length)
    {
        int node = 1;
        while (node < size)
        {
            int left = node << 1;
            if (segmentTree[left] >= length)
            {
                node = left;
            }
            else
            {
                node = left | 1;
            }
        }
        return node - size;
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    private static void Update(int[] segmentTree, int size, int bin, int remaining)
    {
        int node = size + bin;
        segmentTree[node] = remaining;

        for (node >>= 1; node > 0; node >>= 1)
        {
            int left = segmentTree[node << 1];
            int right = segmentTree[(node << 1) | 1];
            segmentTree[node] = left >= right ? left : right;
        }
    }
}

sealed class FastScanner
{
    private readonly Stream _stream = Console.OpenStandardInput();
    private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
    private int _length;
    private int _position;

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public int NextInt()
    {
        int c;
        do
        {
            if (_position >= _length)
            {
                _length = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _position = 0;
                if (_length == 0)
                {
                    return 0;
                }
            }
            c = _buffer[_position++];
        } while (c <= 32);

        int sign = 1;
        if (c == '-')
        {
            sign = -1;
            if (_position >= _length)
            {
                _length = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _position = 0;
            }
            c = _buffer[_position++];
        }

        int value = c - '0';
        while (true)
        {
            if (_position >= _length)
            {
                _length = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _position = 0;
                if (_length == 0)
                {
                    break;
                }
            }

            c = _buffer[_position];
            if (c < '0' || c > '9')
            {
                break;
            }

            _position++;
            value = value * 10 + (c - '0');
        }

        return value * sign;
    }
}

sealed class FastWriter
{
    private readonly Stream _stream = Console.OpenStandardOutput();
    private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 20];
    private int _position;

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteByte(byte value)
    {
        if (_position == _buffer.Length)
        {
            Flush();
        }
        _buffer[_position++] = value;
    }

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteSpace() => WriteByte((byte)' ');

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteLine() => WriteByte((byte)'\n');

    [MethodImpl(MethodImplOptions.AggressiveInlining)]
    public void WriteInt(int value)
    {
        if (value == 0)
        {
            WriteByte((byte)'0');
            return;
        }

        Span<byte> digits = stackalloc byte[11];
        int len = 0;
        uint v = (uint)value;
        do
        {
            uint q = v / 10;
            digits[len++] = (byte)('0' + (v - q * 10));
            v = q;
        } while (v != 0);

        for (int i = len - 1; i >= 0; i--)
        {
            WriteByte(digits[i]);
        }
    }

    public void Flush()
    {
        _stream.Write(_buffer, 0, _position);
        _position = 0;
    }
}

Test 10: 2D Cutting Stock - Rectangle Packing (C++) Score: 0.00 / 13
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the 2D Cutting Stock Problem (Rectangle Bin Packing) in C++.

Given the option to purchase fixed size large 2D boards, and a list of rectangles needed,
minimize the number of boards purchased by cleverly planning your cuts and minimising waste.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 4-8 rectangles, 100x100-200x150 boards
- **Ludicrous**: 500-50000 rectangles, 2000x2000-10000x10000 boards

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N board_width board_height
Next N lines: w h (rectangle dimensions)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_boards
Next N lines: board_index x y rotated (one per rectangle, rotated is 0 or 1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on problem size and board dimensions
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 30 seconds even for 50000 rectangles
3. **Quality**: Minimize number of boards used while producing valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Rectangles may be rotated 90° if it helps
- No overlapping allowed
- Rectangles must not exceed board boundaries

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

This 2D packing problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number and variety of rectangles. The baseline stacks rectangles in simple horizontal rows.

Test 11: 2D Polygon Cutting Stock (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 2D Polygon Cutting Stock Problem in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with problems ranging from 2 simple pieces to 5000+ complex polygons.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: SV NP (number of stock polygon vertices, number of pieces)
Next SV lines: x y (stock polygon vertices)
For each piece:
Line: PV (number of vertices in this piece)
Next PV lines: x y (piece polygon vertices)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: num_stocks
Next NP lines: stock_index x y rotation (one per piece)
stock_index: 0-indexed stock polygon
x y: translation to apply
rotation: angle in radians

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and complexity of pieces
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for 5000+ complex pieces
3. **Quality**: Minimize stock usage while ensuring valid placements

**Constraints:**
- Each placed piece must fit entirely within the stock polygon
- Pieces on same stock must not overlap
- Pieces may be rotated to any angle

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

The AI must handle polygon geometry (containment, intersection, rotation) and plan efficient layouts. Subpasses increase the number and complexity of the pieces. The baseline uses a simple greedy placement strategy.
