Test 12: 3D Bin Packing - Polyhedra in Box (C#) Score: 0.50 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a 3D Bin Packing problem in C#.

You must write a C# solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small containers (5x5x5), simple polyhedra
- **Medium**: Medium containers (10x10x10-20x20x20)
- **Large**: Large containers (50x50x50-100x100x100)
- **Extreme**: Massive containers (200x200x200-500x500x500)

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: V F (vertex/face count of the polyhedron)
Next V lines: x y z (polyhedron vertices)
Next F lines: a b c (polyhedron face indices)
Last line: W H D (container width, height, depth)
Container origin at (0,0,0), extends to (W,H,D).

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of polyhedra packed)
Next count lines: tx ty tz qw qx qy qz
tx,ty,tz: translation
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar part)

**Quaternion Rotation Reference:**
- Identity (no rotation): 1 0 0 0
- 90° around Z: 0.707 0 0 0.707
- To rotate a point: p' = q * p * q^(-1)

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on container size and polyhedron complexity
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive containers
3. **Quality**: Maximize packing count while ensuring valid placements

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Pack as many copies of an irregular 3D shape into a box as possible, without any overlaps or pieces poking outside. The shapes can be rotated freely, so finding the best orientations is part of the puzzle.

The AI must handle 3D collision detection and spatial reasoning. Subpasses use increasingly awkward shapes that resist neat stacking. The baseline uses a simple grid placement without rotation optimisation.

 0.5000
[2x2x2 boxes in 10x10x10 container] Packed: 27, Baseline: 64, Time: 0.2s - low (42% of baseline) .

3D Bin Packing - 2x2x2 boxes in 10x10x10 container

View Code (31903 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public static class Program
{
    public static void Main()
    {
        new Solver().Run();
    }
}

internal sealed class Solver
{
    private const double Tiny = 1e-12;
    private const double FitSlack = 0.0;
    private const double FitEps = 1e-9;
    private const double KeyScale = 100000000.0;
    private const int CandidateCap = 192;
    private const int MaxEmittedPlacements = 500000;

    private readonly FastScanner _input = new FastScanner();
    private readonly List<OrientationCandidate> _candidates = new List<OrientationCandidate>();
    private readonly Dictionary<BoxKey, BoxChoice> _memo = new Dictionary<BoxKey, BoxChoice>(1024);
    private BufferedOutput _output = null!;
    private Vec3[] _vertices = Array.Empty<Vec3>();
    private int _branchFactor;
    private int _depth;

    public void Run()
    {
        int vertexCount = _input.NextInt();
        int faceCount = _input.NextInt();
        if (vertexCount <= 0 || faceCount < 0)
        {
            using var output = new BufferedOutput();
            output.WriteInt64Line(0);
            return;
        }

        _vertices = new Vec3[vertexCount];
        for (int i = 0; i < vertexCount; i++)
        {
            _vertices[i] = new Vec3(_input.NextDouble(), _input.NextDouble(), _input.NextDouble());
        }

        FaceTriangle[] rawFaces = new FaceTriangle[faceCount];
        int minIndex = int.MaxValue;
        int maxIndex = int.MinValue;
        bool hasZero = false;
        bool hasVertexCount = false;
        for (int i = 0; i < faceCount; i++)
        {
            int a = _input.NextInt();
            int b = _input.NextInt();
            int c = _input.NextInt();
            rawFaces[i] = new FaceTriangle(a, b, c, 0.0);
            if (a < minIndex) minIndex = a;
            if (b < minIndex) minIndex = b;
            if (c < minIndex) minIndex = c;
            if (a > maxIndex) maxIndex = a;
            if (b > maxIndex) maxIndex = b;
            if (c > maxIndex) maxIndex = c;
            if (a == 0 || b == 0 || c == 0) hasZero = true;
            if (a == vertexCount || b == vertexCount || c == vertexCount) hasVertexCount = true;
        }

        double width = _input.NextDouble();
        double height = _input.NextDouble();
        double depth = _input.NextDouble();

        FaceTriangle[] faces;
        bool rawValid = TryAdjustFaces(rawFaces, vertexCount, 0, out FaceTriangle[] rawAdjusted);
        bool shiftedValid = TryAdjustFaces(rawFaces, vertexCount, -1, out FaceTriangle[] shiftedAdjusted);
        if (!rawValid && !shiftedValid)
        {
            using var output = new BufferedOutput();
            output.WriteInt64Line(0);
            return;
        }

        bool useShifted = false;
        if (rawValid && shiftedValid)
        {
            useShifted = !hasZero && (hasVertexCount || minIndex == 1);
        }
        else if (!rawValid && shiftedValid)
        {
            useShifted = true;
        }

        faces = useShifted ? shiftedAdjusted : rawAdjusted;

        for (int i = 0; i < faces.Length; i++)
        {
            FaceTriangle face = faces[i];
            Vec3 a = _vertices[face.A];
            Vec3 b = _vertices[face.B];
            Vec3 c = _vertices[face.C];
            face.Area = Vec3.Cross(b - a, c - a).Length() * 0.5;
            faces[i] = face;
        }

        Array.Sort(faces, (x, y) => y.Area.CompareTo(x.Area));

        BuildCandidates(faces, width, height, depth);
        if (_candidates.Count == 0)
        {
            using var output = new BufferedOutput();
            output.WriteInt64Line(0);
            return;
        }

        ScoreCandidates(width, height, depth);
        if (_candidates.Count > CandidateCap)
        {
            _candidates.RemoveRange(CandidateCap, _candidates.Count - CandidateCap);
        }

        ConfigureStrategy(width, height, depth);

        _output = new BufferedOutput();
        _memo.Clear();

        long fullCount = ChooseBest(width, height, depth, _depth).Count;
        long toEmit = fullCount > MaxEmittedPlacements ? MaxEmittedPlacements : fullCount;

        _output.WriteInt64Line(toEmit);
        long remaining = toEmit;
        EmitBox(0.0, 0.0, 0.0, width, height, depth, _depth, ref remaining);
        _output.Flush();
    }

    private void ConfigureStrategy(double width, double height, double depth)
    {
        double volume = width * height * depth;
        double maxDim = Math.Max(width, Math.Max(height, depth));

        if (maxDim <= 8.0 || volume <= 1000.0)
        {
            _depth = 3;
            _branchFactor = 10;
        }
        else if (maxDim <= 20.0 || volume <= 8000.0)
        {
            _depth = 2;
            _branchFactor = 8;
        }
        else if (maxDim <= 100.0 || volume <= 125000.0)
        {
            _depth = 2;
            _branchFactor = 6;
        }
        else
        {
            _depth = 1;
            _branchFactor = 4;
        }

        if (_candidates.Count > 160)
        {
            _branchFactor = Math.Max(4, _branchFactor - 1);
        }
    }

    private void BuildCandidates(FaceTriangle[] faces, double width, double height, double depth)
    {
        _candidates.Clear();
        HashSet<(long, long, long)> seen = new HashSet<(long, long, long)>();

        AddBasisPermutations(new Vec3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0), new Vec3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0), new Vec3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0), seen);
        AddPcaCandidates(seen);

        int faceLimit = faces.Length;
        double containerVolume = width * height * depth;
        if (containerVolume > 200000.0)
        {
            faceLimit = Math.Min(faceLimit, 48);
        }
        else if (containerVolume > 20000.0)
        {
            faceLimit = Math.Min(faceLimit, 96);
        }
        else if (containerVolume > 2000.0)
        {
            faceLimit = Math.Min(faceLimit, 128);
        }
        else
        {
            faceLimit = Math.Min(faceLimit, 256);
        }

        for (int i = 0; i < faceLimit; i++)
        {
            AddFaceCandidates(faces[i], seen);
        }

        if (_candidates.Count == 0)
        {
            AddBasisPermutations(new Vec3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0), new Vec3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0), new Vec3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0), seen);
        }
    }

    private void ScoreCandidates(double width, double height, double depth)
    {
        for (int i = 0; i < _candidates.Count; i++)
        {
            OrientationCandidate cand = _candidates[i];
            long cx = FitCount(width, cand.Dx);
            long cy = FitCount(height, cand.Dy);
            long cz = FitCount(depth, cand.Dz);
            cand.GlobalScore = SafeMul3(cx, cy, cz);
            _candidates[i] = cand;
        }

        _candidates.Sort((a, b) =>
        {
            int cmp = b.GlobalScore.CompareTo(a.GlobalScore);
            if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
            cmp = b.Volume.CompareTo(a.Volume);
            if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
            cmp = a.Dx.CompareTo(b.Dx);
            if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
            cmp = a.Dy.CompareTo(b.Dy);
            if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
            return a.Dz.CompareTo(b.Dz);
        });
    }

    private void AddPcaCandidates(HashSet<(long, long, long)> seen)
    {
        if (_vertices.Length < 2)
        {
            return;
        }

        Vec3 centroid = new Vec3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0);
        for (int i = 0; i < _vertices.Length; i++)
        {
            centroid += _vertices[i];
        }
        centroid /= _vertices.Length;

        double xx = 0.0, xy = 0.0, xz = 0.0, yy = 0.0, yz = 0.0, zz = 0.0;
        for (int i = 0; i < _vertices.Length; i++)
        {
            Vec3 p = _vertices[i] - centroid;
            xx += p.X * p.X;
            xy += p.X * p.Y;
            xz += p.X * p.Z;
            yy += p.Y * p.Y;
            yz += p.Y * p.Z;
            zz += p.Z * p.Z;
        }

        double n = _vertices.Length;
        xx /= n;
        xy /= n;
        xz /= n;
        yy /= n;
        yz /= n;
        zz /= n;

        double[,] a = new double[3, 3];
        a[0, 0] = xx;
        a[0, 1] = xy;
        a[0, 2] = xz;
        a[1, 0] = xy;
        a[1, 1] = yy;
        a[1, 2] = yz;
        a[2, 0] = xz;
        a[2, 1] = yz;
        a[2, 2] = zz;

        JacobiEigen(a, out Vec3 e0, out Vec3 e1, out Vec3 e2, out double l0, out double l1, out double l2);

        Vec3[] vecs = { Vec3.Normalize(e0), Vec3.Normalize(e1), Vec3.Normalize(e2) };
        double[] vals = { l0, l1, l2 };
        int[] order = { 0, 1, 2 };
        Array.Sort(order, (i, j) => vals[j].CompareTo(vals[i]));

        Vec3 b0 = Vec3.Normalize(vecs[order[0]]);
        Vec3 b1 = Vec3.Normalize(vecs[order[1]]);
        Vec3 b2 = Vec3.Normalize(vecs[order[2]]);
        if (Vec3.Dot(Vec3.Cross(b0, b1), b2) < 0.0)
        {
            b2 = -b2;
        }

        AddBasisPermutations(b0, b1, b2, seen);
    }

    private void AddFaceCandidates(FaceTriangle face, HashSet<(long, long, long)> seen)
    {
        Vec3 a = _vertices[face.A];
        Vec3 b = _vertices[face.B];
        Vec3 c = _vertices[face.C];

        Vec3 normal = Vec3.Cross(b - a, c - a);
        double normalLength = normal.Length();
        if (normalLength <= Tiny)
        {
            return;
        }
        normal /= normalLength;

        AddEdgeBasis(normal, b - a, seen);
        AddEdgeBasis(normal, c - b, seen);
        AddEdgeBasis(normal, a - c, seen);
    }

    private void AddEdgeBasis(Vec3 normal, Vec3 edge, HashSet<(long, long, long)> seen)
    {
        Vec3 u = edge - normal * Vec3.Dot(edge, normal);
        double uLen = u.Length();
        if (uLen <= Tiny)
        {
            return;
        }
        u /= uLen;
        Vec3 v = Vec3.Normalize(Vec3.Cross(normal, u));
        if (v.Length() <= Tiny)
        {
            return;
        }

        AddBasisPermutations(u, v, normal, seen);
    }

    private void AddBasisPermutations(Vec3 a, Vec3 b, Vec3 c, HashSet<(long, long, long)> seen)
    {
        AddCandidateFromRows(a, b, c, seen);
        AddCandidateFromRows(a, c, -b, seen);
        AddCandidateFromRows(b, a, -c, seen);
        AddCandidateFromRows(b, c, a, seen);
        AddCandidateFromRows(c, a, b, seen);
        AddCandidateFromRows(c, b, -a, seen);
    }

    private void AddCandidateFromRows(Vec3 r0, Vec3 r1, Vec3 r2, HashSet<(long, long, long)> seen)
    {
        double minX = double.PositiveInfinity;
        double minY = double.PositiveInfinity;
        double minZ = double.PositiveInfinity;
        double maxX = double.NegativeInfinity;
        double maxY = double.NegativeInfinity;
        double maxZ = double.NegativeInfinity;

        for (int i = 0; i < _vertices.Length; i++)
        {
            Vec3 p = _vertices[i];
            double x = Vec3.Dot(r0, p);
            double y = Vec3.Dot(r1, p);
            double z = Vec3.Dot(r2, p);

            if (x < minX) minX = x;
            if (y < minY) minY = y;
            if (z < minZ) minZ = z;
            if (x > maxX) maxX = x;
            if (y > maxY) maxY = y;
            if (z > maxZ) maxZ = z;
        }

        double dx = maxX - minX;
        double dy = maxY - minY;
        double dz = maxZ - minZ;
        if (!(dx > Tiny && dy > Tiny && dz > Tiny))
        {
            return;
        }

        if (double.IsNaN(dx) || double.IsNaN(dy) || double.IsNaN(dz) ||
            double.IsInfinity(dx) || double.IsInfinity(dy) || double.IsInfinity(dz))
        {
            return;
        }

        long kx = RoundKey(dx);
        long ky = RoundKey(dy);
        long kz = RoundKey(dz);
        if (!seen.Add((kx, ky, kz)))
        {
            return;
        }

        OrientationCandidate cand = new OrientationCandidate
        {
            Min = new Vec3(minX, minY, minZ),
            Dx = dx,
            Dy = dy,
            Dz = dz,
            Q = MatrixToQuaternion(r0, r1, r2),
            Volume = dx * dy * dz,
            GlobalScore = 0
        };
        _candidates.Add(cand);
    }

    private BoxChoice ChooseBest(double lx, double ly, double lz, int depth)
    {
        lx = NormalizeLength(lx);
        ly = NormalizeLength(ly);
        lz = NormalizeLength(lz);
        if (lx <= Tiny || ly <= Tiny || lz <= Tiny || _candidates.Count == 0)
        {
            return new BoxChoice { Index = -1, Count = 0 };
        }

        BoxKey key = new BoxKey(lx, ly, lz, depth);
        if (_memo.TryGetValue(key, out BoxChoice cached))
        {
            return cached;
        }

        int poolSize = Math.Min(32, Math.Min(_candidates.Count, _branchFactor * 3));
        Span<int> topIndex = stackalloc int[32];
        Span<long> topBase = stackalloc long[32];
        Span<long> topNx = stackalloc long[32];
        Span<long> topNy = stackalloc long[32];
        Span<long> topNz = stackalloc long[32];
        Span<double> topVol = stackalloc double[32];
        int filled = 0;

        for (int i = 0; i < _candidates.Count; i++)
        {
            OrientationCandidate cand = _candidates[i];
            long nx = FitCount(lx, cand.Dx);
            if (nx == 0) continue;
            long ny = FitCount(ly, cand.Dy);
            if (ny == 0) continue;
            long nz = FitCount(lz, cand.Dz);
            if (nz == 0) continue;

            long baseCount = SafeMul3(nx, ny, nz);
            if (baseCount == 0) continue;

            double vol = cand.Volume;
            int pos;
            if (filled < poolSize)
            {
                pos = filled;
                filled++;
            }
            else
            {
                if (BetterScore(baseCount, vol, topBase[filled - 1], topVol[filled - 1]) <= 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }
                pos = filled - 1;
            }

            while (pos > 0 && BetterScore(baseCount, vol, topBase[pos - 1], topVol[pos - 1]) > 0)
            {
                topIndex[pos] = topIndex[pos - 1];
                topBase[pos] = topBase[pos - 1];
                topNx[pos] = topNx[pos - 1];
                topNy[pos] = topNy[pos - 1];
                topNz[pos] = topNz[pos - 1];
                topVol[pos] = topVol[pos - 1];
                pos--;
            }

            topIndex[pos] = i;
            topBase[pos] = baseCount;
            topNx[pos] = nx;
            topNy[pos] = ny;
            topNz[pos] = nz;
            topVol[pos] = vol;
        }

        if (filled == 0)
        {
            BoxChoice none = new BoxChoice { Index = -1, Count = 0 };
            _memo[key] = none;
            return none;
        }

        long bestCount = 0;
        int bestIndex = -1;
        for (int k = 0; k < filled; k++)
        {
            long total = topBase[k];
            if (depth > 0)
            {
                OrientationCandidate cand = _candidates[topIndex[k]];
                double bx = topNx[k] * cand.Dx;
                double by = topNy[k] * cand.Dy;
                double bz = topNz[k] * cand.Dz;

                total = AddSaturating(total, ChooseBest(NormalizeLength(lx - bx), by, bz, depth - 1).Count);
                total = AddSaturating(total, ChooseBest(lx, NormalizeLength(ly - by), bz, depth - 1).Count);
                total = AddSaturating(total, ChooseBest(lx, ly, NormalizeLength(lz - bz), depth - 1).Count);
            }

            if (total > bestCount)
            {
                bestCount = total;
                bestIndex = topIndex[k];
            }
        }

        BoxChoice result = new BoxChoice { Index = bestIndex, Count = bestCount };
        _memo[key] = result;
        return result;
    }

    private void EmitBox(double ox, double oy, double oz, double lx, double ly, double lz, int depth, ref long remaining)
    {
        if (remaining <= 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        lx = NormalizeLength(lx);
        ly = NormalizeLength(ly);
        lz = NormalizeLength(lz);
        if (lx <= Tiny || ly <= Tiny || lz <= Tiny)
        {
            return;
        }

        BoxChoice choice = ChooseBest(lx, ly, lz, depth);
        if (choice.Index < 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        OrientationCandidate cand = _candidates[choice.Index];
        long nx = FitCount(lx, cand.Dx);
        long ny = FitCount(ly, cand.Dy);
        long nz = FitCount(lz, cand.Dz);
        if (nx == 0 || ny == 0 || nz == 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        double bx = nx * cand.Dx;
        double by = ny * cand.Dy;
        double bz = nz * cand.Dz;
        double baseTx = ox - cand.Min.X;
        double baseTy = oy - cand.Min.Y;
        double baseTz = oz - cand.Min.Z;

        for (long iz = 0; iz < nz && remaining > 0; iz++)
        {
            double tz = baseTz + iz * cand.Dz;
            for (long iy = 0; iy < ny && remaining > 0; iy++)
            {
                double ty = baseTy + iy * cand.Dy;
                for (long ix = 0; ix < nx && remaining > 0; ix++)
                {
                    double tx = baseTx + ix * cand.Dx;
                    _output.WritePlacement(tx, ty, tz, cand.Q);
                    remaining--;
                }
            }
        }

        if (remaining <= 0 || depth <= 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        EmitBox(ox + bx, oy, oz, NormalizeLength(lx - bx), by, bz, depth - 1, ref remaining);
        if (remaining <= 0) return;
        EmitBox(ox, oy + by, oz, lx, NormalizeLength(ly - by), bz, depth - 1, ref remaining);
        if (remaining <= 0) return;
        EmitBox(ox, oy, oz + bz, lx, ly, NormalizeLength(lz - bz), depth - 1, ref remaining);
    }

    private static bool TryAdjustFaces(FaceTriangle[] rawFaces, int vertexCount, int offset, out FaceTriangle[] adjusted)
    {
        adjusted = new FaceTriangle[rawFaces.Length];
        for (int i = 0; i < rawFaces.Length; i++)
        {
            FaceTriangle face = rawFaces[i];
            int a = face.A + offset;
            int b = face.B + offset;
            int c = face.C + offset;
            if ((uint)a >= (uint)vertexCount || (uint)b >= (uint)vertexCount || (uint)c >= (uint)vertexCount)
            {
                adjusted = Array.Empty<FaceTriangle>();
                return false;
            }
            adjusted[i] = new FaceTriangle(a, b, c, face.Area);
        }
        return true;
    }

    private static void JacobiEigen(double[,] a, out Vec3 v0, out Vec3 v1, out Vec3 v2, out double l0, out double l1, out double l2)
    {
        double[,] v = new double[3, 3];
        v[0, 0] = 1.0;
        v[1, 1] = 1.0;
        v[2, 2] = 1.0;

        for (int iter = 0; iter < 32; iter++)
        {
            int p = 0;
            int q = 1;
            double max = Math.Abs(a[0, 1]);
            double a02 = Math.Abs(a[0, 2]);
            if (a02 > max)
            {
                max = a02;
                p = 0;
                q = 2;
            }
            double a12 = Math.Abs(a[1, 2]);
            if (a12 > max)
            {
                max = a12;
                p = 1;
                q = 2;
            }

            if (max < 1e-15)
            {
                break;
            }

            double app = a[p, p];
            double aqq = a[q, q];
            double apq = a[p, q];
            if (Math.Abs(apq) < 1e-20)
            {
                continue;
            }

            double tau = (aqq - app) / (2.0 * apq);
            double t = 1.0 / (Math.Abs(tau) + Math.Sqrt(1.0 + tau * tau));
            if (tau < 0.0)
            {
                t = -t;
            }
            double c = 1.0 / Math.Sqrt(1.0 + t * t);
            double s = t * c;

            for (int k = 0; k < 3; k++)
            {
                if (k == p || k == q)
                {
                    continue;
                }
                double aik = a[k, p];
                double aiq = a[k, q];
                double newAik = c * aik - s * aiq;
                double newAiq = s * aik + c * aiq;
                a[k, p] = newAik;
                a[p, k] = newAik;
                a[k, q] = newAiq;
                a[q, k] = newAiq;
            }

            double newApp = c * c * app - 2.0 * s * c * apq + s * s * aqq;
            double newAqq = s * s * app + 2.0 * s * c * apq + c * c * aqq;
            a[p, p] = newApp;
            a[q, q] = newAqq;
            a[p, q] = 0.0;
            a[q, p] = 0.0;

            for (int k = 0; k < 3; k++)
            {
                double vip = v[k, p];
                double viq = v[k, q];
                v[k, p] = c * vip - s * viq;
                v[k, q] = s * vip + c * viq;
            }
        }

        l0 = a[0, 0];
        l1 = a[1, 1];
        l2 = a[2, 2];
        v0 = new Vec3(v[0, 0], v[1, 0], v[2, 0]);
        v1 = new Vec3(v[0, 1], v[1, 1], v[2, 1]);
        v2 = new Vec3(v[0, 2], v[1, 2], v[2, 2]);
    }

    private static QuaternionD MatrixToQuaternion(Vec3 r0, Vec3 r1, Vec3 r2)
    {
        double m00 = r0.X;
        double m01 = r0.Y;
        double m02 = r0.Z;
        double m10 = r1.X;
        double m11 = r1.Y;
        double m12 = r1.Z;
        double m20 = r2.X;
        double m21 = r2.Y;
        double m22 = r2.Z;

        double w;
        double x;
        double y;
        double z;
        double trace = m00 + m11 + m22;
        if (trace > 0.0)
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(trace + 1.0) * 2.0;
            w = 0.25 * s;
            x = (m21 - m12) / s;
            y = (m02 - m20) / s;
            z = (m10 - m01) / s;
        }
        else if (m00 > m11 && m00 > m22)
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(1.0 + m00 - m11 - m22) * 2.0;
            w = (m21 - m12) / s;
            x = 0.25 * s;
            y = (m01 + m10) / s;
            z = (m02 + m20) / s;
        }
        else if (m11 > m22)
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(1.0 + m11 - m00 - m22) * 2.0;
            w = (m02 - m20) / s;
            x = (m01 + m10) / s;
            y = 0.25 * s;
            z = (m12 + m21) / s;
        }
        else
        {
            double s = Math.Sqrt(1.0 + m22 - m00 - m11) * 2.0;
            w = (m10 - m01) / s;
            x = (m02 + m20) / s;
            y = (m12 + m21) / s;
            z = 0.25 * s;
        }

        double norm = Math.Sqrt(w * w + x * x + y * y + z * z);
        if (norm > 0.0)
        {
            w /= norm;
            x /= norm;
            y /= norm;
            z /= norm;
        }
        if (w < 0.0)
        {
            w = -w;
            x = -x;
            y = -y;
            z = -z;
        }

        return new QuaternionD(w, x, y, z);
    }

    private static long FitCount(double length, double step)
    {
        if (double.IsNaN(length) || double.IsNaN(step) || double.IsInfinity(length) || double.IsInfinity(step))
        {
            return 0;
        }
        if (length <= Tiny || step <= Tiny)
        {
            return 0;
        }

        double ratio = (length + FitSlack) / step;
        if (ratio <= 0.0)
        {
            return 0;
        }
        if (ratio >= long.MaxValue)
        {
            return long.MaxValue;
        }

        long n = (long)Math.Floor(ratio);
        while (n > 0 && n * step > length + FitEps)
        {
            n--;
        }
        return n < 0 ? 0 : n;
    }

    private static long SafeMul3(long a, long b, long c)
    {
        if (a <= 0 || b <= 0 || c <= 0)
        {
            return 0;
        }
        if (a > long.MaxValue / b)
        {
            return long.MaxValue;
        }
        long ab = a * b;
        if (ab > long.MaxValue / c)
        {
            return long.MaxValue;
        }
        return ab * c;
    }

    private static long AddSaturating(long a, long b)
    {
        if (b <= 0)
        {
            return a;
        }
        if (a >= long.MaxValue - b)
        {
            return long.MaxValue;
        }
        return a + b;
    }

    private static int BetterScore(long countA, double volA, long countB, double volB)
    {
        if (countA > countB) return 1;
        if (countA < countB) return -1;
        if (volA > volB) return 1;
        if (volA < volB) return -1;
        return 0;
    }

    private static double NormalizeLength(double value)
    {
        if (double.IsNaN(value) || double.IsInfinity(value))
        {
            return 0.0;
        }
        return value <= Tiny ? 0.0 : value;
    }

    private static long RoundKey(double value)
    {
        return (long)Math.Floor(value * KeyScale + 0.5);
    }

    private static bool IsValidIndexRange(int index, int vertexCount)
    {
        return (uint)index < (uint)vertexCount;
    }

    private static double Clean(double value)
    {
        return Math.Abs(value) < 1e-12 ? 0.0 : value;
    }

    private static bool IsFinite(double value)
    {
        return !double.IsNaN(value) && !double.IsInfinity(value);
    }

    private readonly struct BoxKey : IEquatable<BoxKey>
    {
        private readonly long _x;
        private readonly long _y;
        private readonly long _z;
        private readonly int _depth;

        public BoxKey(double x, double y, double z, int depth)
        {
            _x = BitConverter.DoubleToInt64Bits(NormalizeLength(x));
            _y = BitConverter.DoubleToInt64Bits(NormalizeLength(y));
            _z = BitConverter.DoubleToInt64Bits(NormalizeLength(z));
            _depth = depth;
        }

        public bool Equals(BoxKey other)
        {
            return _x == other._x && _y == other._y && _z == other._z && _depth == other._depth;
        }

        public override bool Equals(object obj)
        {
            return obj is BoxKey other && Equals(other);
        }

        public override int GetHashCode()
        {
            unchecked
            {
                int hash = (int)(_x ^ (_x >> 32));
                hash = (hash * 397) ^ (int)(_y ^ (_y >> 32));
                hash = (hash * 397) ^ (int)(_z ^ (_z >> 32));
                hash = (hash * 397) ^ _depth;
                return hash;
            }
        }
    }

    private struct BoxChoice
    {
        public int Index;
        public long Count;
    }

    private struct FaceTriangle
    {
        public int A;
        public int B;
        public int C;
        public double Area;

        public FaceTriangle(int a, int b, int c, double area)
        {
            A = a;
            B = b;
            C = c;
            Area = area;
        }
    }

    private struct OrientationCandidate
    {
        public Vec3 Min;
        public QuaternionD Q;
        public double Dx;
        public double Dy;
        public double Dz;
        public long GlobalScore;
        public double Volume;
    }

    private struct QuaternionD
    {
        public double W;
        public double X;
        public double Y;
        public double Z;

        public QuaternionD(double w, double x, double y, double z)
        {
            W = w;
            X = x;
            Y = y;
            Z = z;
        }
    }

    private struct Vec3
    {
        public double X;
        public double Y;
        public double Z;

        public Vec3(double x, double y, double z)
        {
            X = x;
            Y = y;
            Z = z;
        }

        public static Vec3 operator +(Vec3 a, Vec3 b)
        {
            return new Vec3(a.X + b.X, a.Y + b.Y, a.Z + b.Z);
        }

        public static Vec3 operator -(Vec3 a, Vec3 b)
        {
            return new Vec3(a.X - b.X, a.Y - b.Y, a.Z - b.Z);
        }

        public static Vec3 operator -(Vec3 a)
        {
            return new Vec3(-a.X, -a.Y, -a.Z);
        }

        public static Vec3 operator *(Vec3 a, double s)
        {
            return new Vec3(a.X * s, a.Y * s, a.Z * s);
        }

        public static Vec3 operator /(Vec3 a, double s)
        {
            return new Vec3(a.X / s, a.Y / s, a.Z / s);
        }

        public double Length()
        {
            return Math.Sqrt(X * X + Y * Y + Z * Z);
        }

        public static double Dot(Vec3 a, Vec3 b)
        {
            return a.X * b.X + a.Y * b.Y + a.Z * b.Z;
        }

        public static Vec3 Cross(Vec3 a, Vec3 b)
        {
            return new Vec3(
                a.Y * b.Z - a.Z * b.Y,
                a.Z * b.X - a.X * b.Z,
                a.X * b.Y - a.Y * b.X);
        }

        public static Vec3 Normalize(Vec3 v)
        {
            double len = Math.Sqrt(v.X * v.X + v.Y * v.Y + v.Z * v.Z);
            if (len <= 1e-18)
            {
                return new Vec3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0);
            }
            return new Vec3(v.X / len, v.Y / len, v.Z / len);
        }
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly string _data = Console.In.ReadToEnd();
        private int _index;

        public int NextInt()
        {
            ReadOnlySpan<char> token = NextToken();
            return int.Parse(token, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        }

        public double NextDouble()
        {
            ReadOnlySpan<char> token = NextToken();
            return double.Parse(token, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        }

        private ReadOnlySpan<char> NextToken()
        {
            while (_index < _data.Length && char.IsWhiteSpace(_data[_index]))
            {
                _index++;
            }

            int start = _index;
            while (_index < _data.Length && !char.IsWhiteSpace(_data[_index]))
            {
                _index++;
            }

            return _data.AsSpan(start, _index - start);
        }
    }

    private sealed class BufferedOutput : IDisposable
    {
        private readonly StringBuilder _builder;
        private readonly StreamWriter _writer;
        private const int FlushThreshold = 1 << 20;

        public BufferedOutput()
        {
            _builder = new StringBuilder(FlushThreshold);
            _writer = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), new UTF8Encoding(false), FlushThreshold)
            {
                AutoFlush = false
            };
        }

        public void WriteInt64Line(long value)
        {
            _builder.Append(value);
            _builder.Append('\n');
            FlushIfNeeded();
        }

        public void WritePlacement(double tx, double ty, double tz, QuaternionD q)
        {
            AppendDouble(Clean(tx));
            _builder.Append(' ');
            AppendDouble(Clean(ty));
            _builder.Append(' ');
            AppendDouble(Clean(tz));
            _builder.Append(' ');
            AppendDouble(Clean(q.W));
            _builder.Append(' ');
            AppendDouble(Clean(q.X));
            _builder.Append(' ');
            AppendDouble(Clean(q.Y));
            _builder.Append(' ');
            AppendDouble(Clean(q.Z));
            _builder.Append('\n');
            FlushIfNeeded();
        }

        private void AppendDouble(double value)
        {
            Span<char> buffer = stackalloc char[64];
            if (value.TryFormat(buffer, out int written, "G17".AsSpan(), CultureInfo.InvariantCulture))
            {
                _builder.Append(buffer.Slice(0, written));
            }
            else
            {
                _builder.Append(value.ToString("G17", CultureInfo.InvariantCulture));
            }
        }

        private void FlushIfNeeded()
        {
            if (_builder.Length >= FlushThreshold)
            {
                Flush();
            }
        }

        public void Flush()
        {
            if (_builder.Length > 0)
            {
                _writer.Write(_builder.ToString());
                _builder.Clear();
            }
            _writer.Flush();
        }

        public void Dispose()
        {
            Flush();
            _writer.Dispose();
        }
    }
}

Test 13: Longest Common Substring (C++) Score: 0.00 / 12
Typical Prompt:
You are solving the Longest Common Substring problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 short strings (10-50 chars each)
- **Medium**: 3-5 medium strings (100-500 chars each)
- **Large**: 5-10 long strings (1000-5000 chars each)
- **Extreme**: 100 very long strings (10MB+ each), massive search space

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: N (number of strings)
Next N lines: one string per line

**Output format (stdout):**
One line: the longest common substring
If no common substring exists, output an empty line.

**Example:**
Input:
3
abcdef
xbcdey
zbcdew

Output:
bcde

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on number and length of strings
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 300 seconds even for very long strings
3. **Correctness**: Must find the actual longest common substring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given several strings, find the longest chunk of text that appears identically in every one of them. For example, if the strings are “abcdef” and “xbcdey”, the answer is “bcde”.

Naïve approaches check every possible substring and become far too slow on large inputs. Subpasses increase the string lengths into the millions, so the AI must use efficient data structures or hashing. The baseline uses a simple library call that times out on the bigger cases.

 0.0000
[3 short strings (7 chars)] Invalid: Result must be string, got dict

Longest Common Substring - 3 short strings (7 chars)

Algorithm: Longest Common Substring solver with adaptive strategy: - Small/medium anchor: exact suffix automaton on the shortest string. - Large/extreme anchor: binary search on length + rolling-hash intersection.

View Code (13573 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstring>
#include <iostream>
#include <string>
#include <string_view>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

static constexpr uint64_t BASE1 = 11400714819323198485ull;
static constexpr uint64_t BASE2 = 14029467366897019727ull;
static constexpr size_t SAM_LIMIT = 300000; // shortest string length threshold

static uint64_t mix64(uint64_t x) {
    x += 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ull;
    x = (x ^ (x >> 30)) * 0xbf58476d1ce4e5b9ull;
    x = (x ^ (x >> 27)) * 0x94d049bb133111ebull;
    return x ^ (x >> 31);
}

static uint64_t pow_u64(uint64_t base, size_t exp) {
    uint64_t result = 1;
    while (exp) {
        if (exp & 1) result *= base;
        base *= base;
        exp >>= 1;
    }
    return result;
}

static size_t nextPow2(size_t x) {
    size_t p = 1;
    while (p < x) p <<= 1;
    return p;
}

static bool containsKMP(const string& text, string_view pat) {
    const size_t m = pat.size();
    if (m == 0) return true;
    if (m > text.size()) return false;

    vector<uint32_t> pi(m, 0);
    size_t j = 0;
    for (size_t i = 1; i < m; ++i) {
        while (j > 0 && pat[i] != pat[j]) j = pi[j - 1];
        if (pat[i] == pat[j]) ++j;
        pi[i] = static_cast<uint32_t>(j);
    }

    for (char ch : text) {
        while (j > 0 && ch != pat[j]) j = pi[j - 1];
        if (ch == pat[j]) ++j;
        if (j == m) return true;
    }
    return false;
}

struct HashTable {
    struct Entry {
        uint64_t h1 = 0;
        uint64_t h2 = 0;
        uint32_t pos = 0;
        uint8_t count = 0;
        uint8_t seen = 0;
        uint32_t stamp = 0;
    };

    vector<Entry> slots;
    size_t mask = 0;
    uint32_t buildId = 1;

    explicit HashTable(size_t expectedEntries = 0) {
        init(expectedEntries);
    }

    void init(size_t expectedEntries) {
        size_t cap = nextPow2(max<size_t>(8, expectedEntries));
        slots.assign(cap, Entry{});
        mask = cap - 1;
        buildId = 1;
    }

    void nextBuild() {
        ++buildId;
        if (buildId == 0) {
            for (auto& e : slots) e.stamp = 0;
            buildId = 1;
        }
    }

    size_t index(uint64_t h1, uint64_t h2) const {
        uint64_t x = h1 ^ (h2 + 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ull + (h1 << 6) + (h1 >> 2));
        return static_cast<size_t>(mix64(x) & mask);
    }

    Entry* find(uint64_t h1, uint64_t h2) {
        size_t i = index(h1, h2);
        while (true) {
            Entry& e = slots[i];
            if (e.stamp != buildId) return nullptr;
            if (e.h1 == h1 && e.h2 == h2) return &e;
            i = (i + 1) & mask;
        }
    }

    void insert(uint64_t h1, uint64_t h2, uint32_t pos) {
        size_t i = index(h1, h2);
        while (true) {
            Entry& e = slots[i];
            if (e.stamp != buildId) {
                e.stamp = buildId;
                e.h1 = h1;
                e.h2 = h2;
                e.pos = pos;
                e.count = 0;
                e.seen = 0;
                return;
            }
            if (e.h1 == h1 && e.h2 == h2) return;
            i = (i + 1) & mask;
        }
    }
};

struct SAM {
    struct State {
        int link = -1;
        int len = 0;
        int firstPos = -1;
        int next[256];

        State() {
            std::fill_n(next, 256, -1);
        }
    };

    vector<State> st;
    int last = 0;

    explicit SAM(size_t reserveStates = 0) {
        st.reserve(reserveStates);
        st.emplace_back(); // root
    }

    int newState() {
        st.emplace_back();
        return static_cast<int>(st.size() - 1);
    }

    void extend(unsigned char c, int pos) {
        int cur = newState();
        st[cur].len = st[last].len + 1;
        st[cur].firstPos = pos;

        int p = last;
        while (p != -1 && st[p].next[c] == -1) {
            st[p].next[c] = cur;
            p = st[p].link;
        }

        if (p == -1) {
            st[cur].link = 0;
        } else {
            int q = st[p].next[c];
            if (st[p].len + 1 == st[q].len) {
                st[cur].link = q;
            } else {
                int clone = newState();
                st[clone] = st[q];
                st[clone].len = st[p].len + 1;
                // firstPos from q is fine for reconstruction purposes.

                while (p != -1 && st[p].next[c] == q) {
                    st[p].next[c] = clone;
                    p = st[p].link;
                }

                st[q].link = st[cur].link = clone;
            }
        }

        last = cur;
    }
};

class Solver {
public:
    vector<string> strings;

    void readInput() {
        ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
        cin.tie(nullptr);

        int N = 0;
        if (!(cin >> N)) return;
        string dummy;
        getline(cin, dummy); // consume endline

        strings.reserve(max(0, N));
        for (int i = 0; i < N; ++i) {
            string s;
            getline(cin, s);
            if (!s.empty() && s.back() == '\r') s.pop_back();
            strings.push_back(std::move(s));
        }
    }

    bool locateCommonSubstring(size_t L, string& out) {
        if (strings.empty()) {
            out.clear();
            return true;
        }
        const string& ref = strings[0];
        if (L == 0) {
            out.clear();
            return true;
        }
        if (ref.size() < L) return false;

        if (strings.size() == 1) {
            out.assign(ref.data(), L);
            return true;
        }

        table.nextBuild();

        const uint64_t pow1 = pow_u64(BASE1, L - 1);
        const uint64_t pow2 = pow_u64(BASE2, L - 1);

        uint64_t h1 = 0, h2 = 0;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < L; ++i) {
            uint64_t x = static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i]) + 1ull;
            h1 = h1 * BASE1 + x;
            h2 = h2 * BASE2 + x;
        }
        table.insert(h1, h2, 0);

        for (size_t i = L; i < ref.size(); ++i) {
            uint64_t outc = static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i - L]) + 1ull;
            uint64_t inc = static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i]) + 1ull;
            h1 -= pow1 * outc;
            h1 = h1 * BASE1 + inc;
            h2 -= pow2 * outc;
            h2 = h2 * BASE2 + inc;
            table.insert(h1, h2, static_cast<uint32_t>(i - L + 1));
        }

        for (size_t sid = 1; sid < strings.size(); ++sid) {
            const string& s = strings[sid];
            if (s.size() < L) return false;

            uint64_t q1 = 0, q2 = 0;
            for (size_t i = 0; i < L; ++i) {
                uint64_t x = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]) + 1ull;
                q1 = q1 * BASE1 + x;
                q2 = q2 * BASE2 + x;
            }

            if (auto* e = table.find(q1, q2); e != nullptr) {
                if (e->seen != sid) {
                    e->seen = static_cast<uint8_t>(sid);
                    ++e->count;
                }
            }

            for (size_t i = L; i < s.size(); ++i) {
                uint64_t outc = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i - L]) + 1ull;
                uint64_t inc = static_cast<unsigned char>(s[i]) + 1ull;
                q1 -= pow1 * outc;
                q1 = q1 * BASE1 + inc;
                q2 -= pow2 * outc;
                q2 = q2 * BASE2 + inc;

                if (auto* e = table.find(q1, q2); e != nullptr) {
                    if (e->seen != sid) {
                        e->seen = static_cast<uint8_t>(sid);
                        ++e->count;
                    }
                }
            }
        }

        for (const auto& e : table.slots) {
            if (e.stamp != table.buildId) continue;
            if (e.count != strings.size() - 1) continue;

            auto verifyAtPos = [&](size_t pos) -> bool {
                string_view candidate(ref.data() + pos, L);
                for (size_t sid = 1; sid < strings.size(); ++sid) {
                    if (!containsKMP(strings[sid], candidate)) return false;
                }
                out.assign(candidate.data(), candidate.size());
                return true;
            };

            // Fast path: try the stored representative first.
            if (verifyAtPos(e.pos)) return true;

            // Slow path: rescan the reference and try every position with this hash pair.
            uint64_t scanH1 = 0, scanH2 = 0;
            for (size_t i = 0; i < L; ++i) {
                uint64_t x = static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i]) + 1ull;
                scanH1 = scanH1 * BASE1 + x;
                scanH2 = scanH2 * BASE2 + x;
            }
            if (scanH1 == e.h1 && scanH2 == e.h2 && verifyAtPos(0)) return true;

            for (size_t i = L; i < ref.size(); ++i) {
                uint64_t outc = static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i - L]) + 1ull;
                uint64_t inc = static_cast<unsigned char>(ref[i]) + 1ull;
                scanH1 -= pow1 * outc;
                scanH1 = scanH1 * BASE1 + inc;
                scanH2 -= pow2 * outc;
                scanH2 = scanH2 * BASE2 + inc;
                if (scanH1 == e.h1 && scanH2 == e.h2) {
                    size_t pos = i - L + 1;
                    if (pos != e.pos && verifyAtPos(pos)) return true;
                }
            }
        }

        return false;
    }

    string solveWithHash() {
        size_t minLen = strings[0].size();
        for (const auto& s : strings) minLen = min(minLen, s.size());

        if (minLen == 0) return string();

        string best;
        size_t lo = 0, hi = minLen;
        while (lo < hi) {
            size_t mid = (lo + hi + 1) >> 1;
            string cand;
            if (locateCommonSubstring(mid, cand)) {
                lo = mid;
                best = std::move(cand);
            } else {
                hi = mid - 1;
            }
        }

        if (lo == 0) return string();
        if (best.size() != lo) {
            string cand;
            if (locateCommonSubstring(lo, cand)) best = std::move(cand);
        }
        return best;
    }

    string solveWithSAM() {
        const string& base = strings[0];
        if (base.empty()) return string();
        if (strings.size() == 1) return base;

        SAM sam(base.size() * 2 + 1);
        for (size_t i = 0; i < base.size(); ++i) {
            sam.extend(static_cast<unsigned char>(base[i]), static_cast<int>(i));
        }

        const int stateCount = static_cast<int>(sam.st.size());
        vector<int> order(stateCount);
        vector<int> cnt(base.size() + 1, 0);
        for (const auto& st : sam.st) ++cnt[st.len];
        for (size_t i = 1; i < cnt.size(); ++i) cnt[i] += cnt[i - 1];
        for (int v = 0; v < stateCount; ++v) {
            order[--cnt[sam.st[v].len]] = v;
        }

        vector<int> common(stateCount);
        for (int v = 0; v < stateCount; ++v) common[v] = sam.st[v].len;
        vector<int> best(stateCount, 0);

        for (size_t sid = 1; sid < strings.size(); ++sid) {
            fill(best.begin(), best.end(), 0);

            int v = 0;
            int l = 0;
            for (unsigned char c : strings[sid]) {
                while (v != -1 && sam.st[v].next[c] == -1) {
                    v = sam.st[v].link;
                    if (v != -1) l = sam.st[v].len;
                }
                if (v == -1) {
                    v = 0;
                    l = 0;
                    continue;
                }
                v = sam.st[v].next[c];
                ++l;
                if (best[v] < l) best[v] = l;
            }

            for (int idx = stateCount - 1; idx > 0; --idx) {
                int x = order[idx];
                int p = sam.st[x].link;
                if (p >= 0) {
                    int cand = best[x];
                    int lim = sam.st[p].len;
                    if (cand > lim) cand = lim;
                    if (best[p] < cand) best[p] = cand;
                }
            }

            int curMax = 0;
            for (int v2 = 0; v2 < stateCount; ++v2) {
                if (best[v2] < common[v2]) common[v2] = best[v2];
                if (common[v2] > curMax) curMax = common[v2];
            }
            if (curMax == 0) return string();
        }

        int ansLen = 0;
        for (int v = 0; v < stateCount; ++v) ansLen = max(ansLen, common[v]);
        if (ansLen == 0) return string();

        // Use the hash-based locator to recover an actual substring.
        string out;
        if (locateCommonSubstring(static_cast<size_t>(ansLen), out)) return out;

        // Very unlikely fallback: return the base prefix of that length.
        // If a hash collision ever caused the locator to fail, the answer
        // length is still correct, but reconstruction is probabilistic.
        return base.substr(0, static_cast<size_t>(ansLen));
    }

    string solve() {
        if (strings.empty()) return string();

        sort(strings.begin(), strings.end(), [](const string& a, const string& b) {
            return a.size() < b.size();
        });

        if (strings[0].empty()) return string();
        if (strings.size() == 1) return strings[0];

        const size_t shortest = strings[0].size();

        // Keep the adaptive logic explicit: exact SAM for smaller anchors,
        // hashed binary search for larger anchors.
        table.init(max<size_t>(8, shortest * 3 / 2 + 16));

        if (shortest <= SAM_LIMIT) {
            return solveWithSAM();
        }
        return solveWithHash();
    }

private:
    HashTable table;
};

int main() {
    Solver solver;
    solver.readInput();
    string ans = solver.solve();
    cout << ans << '\n';
    return 0;
}

Test 14: Minesweeper Solver (C#) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Minesweeper solver in C# that must handle ANY board complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale.

- **Trivial**: Small boards (5x5-8x8), simple patterns, few mines
- **Extreme**: Massive boards (10000x10000), very complex constraint satisfaction problems

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: H W (height and width)
Next H lines: the board row strings (each W characters)

**Cell meanings:**
- ' ' (space): Unknown/unrevealed cell - could be mine or safe
- '0'-'8': Revealed safe cell showing count of adjacent mines (8 neighbors)
- '*': Flagged mine (known mine location)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: N (number of safe cells found)
Next N lines: x y (column row, 0-indexed)
Only output cells that are currently unknown (' ') and provably safe.
Do NOT guess - only return cells you can prove are safe.

**Example:**
For a board where cell (0,0) shows '1' and has one adjacent mine at (1,0):
Neighbors (0,1) and (0,2) are guaranteed safe.

**Constraints:**
- Return as many provably safe cells as possible
- Never return a cell that could be a mine
- Must handle varying board sizes and mine densities efficiently

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on board complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Given a partially-revealed Minesweeper board, deduce which hidden cells are safe to click and which contain mines. Each visible number tells you exactly how many of its neighbours are mined — the AI must combine these clues to make guaranteed-safe moves without guessing.

Larger boards and denser mine layouts make the logic harder. The problem is NP-complete in general, but clever constraint reasoning can solve most practical boards. The baseline uses only the simplest single-cell deduction rules.

Test 15: Tetrahedron Shadow Covering (Rust) Score: 11.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Shadow Covering problem with tetrahedrons in Rust.

**The Challenge:**
Your program will be tested with polygons ranging from simple shapes to very complex geometries.
The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales.

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: NV (number of polygon vertices)
Next NV lines: x y (polygon vertices on z=0 plane)
Last line: sx sy sz (sun direction vector, points from sun toward ground)

**Output format (stdout):**
Line 1: count (number of tetrahedrons)
Next count lines: px py pz qw qx qy qz scale
px,py,pz: center position
qw,qx,qy,qz: rotation quaternion (w is scalar)
scale: uniform scale factor

**Standard Tetrahedron Reference:**
Vertices (before transform):
[1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[-1.0, 0.0, -0.707]
[0.0, 1.0, 0.707]
[0.0, -1.0, 0.707]

**Constraints:**
- Combined shadows must completely cover target polygon
- Tetrahedrons must not intersect each other in 3D
- Maximum scale factor per tetrahedron: 10.0
- Minimize number of tetrahedrons

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Place 3D tetrahedra (triangular pyramids) above a flat surface so that their shadows, cast by a directional light, completely cover a given 2D target shape. Use as few tetrahedra as possible while covering every part of the target.

The AI must reason about 3D-to-2D projection, rotation, and coverage geometry. Subpasses use more complex target shapes and trickier light angles. The baseline uses a simple grid of tetrahedra above the target.

 1.0000
[3x3 square, sun straight down] 100% coverage with 1 tetrahedrons, Time: 0.1s

Shadow Covering - 3x3 square, sun straight down

View Code (11694 chars)
use std::f64::consts::PI;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct V2 {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy, Debug)]
struct V3 {
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

fn v2(x: f64, y: f64) -> V2 {
    V2 { x, y }
}

fn v3(x: f64, y: f64, z: f64) -> V3 {
    V3 { x, y, z }
}

fn add2(a: V2, b: V2) -> V2 {
    v2(a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y)
}

fn sub2(a: V2, b: V2) -> V2 {
    v2(a.x - b.x, a.y - b.y)
}

fn scale2(a: V2, s: f64) -> V2 {
    v2(a.x * s, a.y * s)
}

fn add3(a: V3, b: V3) -> V3 {
    v3(a.x + b.x, a.y + b.y, a.z + b.z)
}

fn scale3(a: V3, s: f64) -> V3 {
    v3(a.x * s, a.y * s, a.z * s)
}

fn dot3(a: V3, b: V3) -> f64 {
    a.x * b.x + a.y * b.y + a.z * b.z
}

fn cross3(a: V3, b: V3) -> V3 {
    v3(
        a.y * b.z - a.z * b.y,
        a.z * b.x - a.x * b.z,
        a.x * b.y - a.y * b.x,
    )
}

fn norm3(a: V3) -> f64 {
    dot3(a, a).sqrt()
}

fn normalize3(a: V3) -> V3 {
    let n = norm3(a);
    if n == 0.0 {
        v3(0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
    } else {
        scale3(a, 1.0 / n)
    }
}

fn project_to_ground(p: V3, wz: f64, wx: f64, wy: f64) -> V2 {
    v2(p.x - p.z * wx / wz, p.y - p.z * wy / wz)
}

fn solve_q(p: V2, d1: V2, d2: V2) -> V2 {
    let det = d1.x * d2.y - d1.y * d2.x;
    v2(
        (d2.y * p.x - d2.x * p.y) / det,
        (-d1.y * p.x + d1.x * p.y) / det,
    )
}

fn floor_i(v: f64) -> i64 {
    v.floor() as i64
}

fn ceil_i(v: f64) -> i64 {
    v.ceil() as i64
}

fn bbox_to_cell_range(minx: f64, maxx: f64, miny: f64, maxy: f64, eps: f64) -> (i64, i64, i64, i64, u128, u128) {
    let mut ix0 = ceil_i(minx - 0.5 - eps);
    let mut ix1 = floor_i(maxx + 0.5 + eps);
    let mut iy0 = ceil_i(miny - 0.5 - eps);
    let mut iy1 = floor_i(maxy + 0.5 + eps);

    if ix0 > ix1 {
        let mid = ((minx + maxx) * 0.5).round() as i64;
        ix0 = mid;
        ix1 = mid;
    }
    if iy0 > iy1 {
        let mid = ((miny + maxy) * 0.5).round() as i64;
        iy0 = mid;
        iy1 = mid;
    }

    let nx = (ix1 - ix0 + 1) as u128;
    let ny = (iy1 - iy0 + 1) as u128;
    (ix0, ix1, iy0, iy1, nx, ny)
}

fn point_on_segment(p: V2, a: V2, b: V2, eps: f64) -> bool {
    let abx = b.x - a.x;
    let aby = b.y - a.y;
    let apx = p.x - a.x;
    let apy = p.y - a.y;
    let cross = abx * apy - aby * apx;
    if cross.abs() > eps * (abx.abs() + aby.abs() + 1.0) {
        return false;
    }
    let dot = apx * (p.x - b.x) + apy * (p.y - b.y);
    dot <= eps
}

fn point_in_polygon(p: V2, poly: &[V2], eps: f64) -> bool {
    let n = poly.len();
    let mut inside = false;
    for i in 0..n {
        let a = poly[i];
        let b = poly[(i + 1) % n];
        if point_on_segment(p, a, b, eps) {
            return true;
        }
        let cond = (a.y > p.y) != (b.y > p.y);
        if cond {
            let x_at_y = (b.x - a.x) * (p.y - a.y) / (b.y - a.y) + a.x;
            if x_at_y >= p.x - eps {
                inside = !inside;
            }
        }
    }
    inside
}

fn clip_axis(p: f64, q: f64, t0: &mut f64, t1: &mut f64, eps: f64) -> bool {
    if p.abs() < eps {
        return q >= -eps;
    }
    let r = q / p;
    if p < 0.0 {
        if r > *t1 + eps {
            return false;
        }
        if r > *t0 {
            *t0 = r;
        }
    } else {
        if r < *t0 - eps {
            return false;
        }
        if r < *t1 {
            *t1 = r;
        }
    }
    true
}

fn segment_intersects_rect(a: V2, b: V2, minx: f64, maxx: f64, miny: f64, maxy: f64, eps: f64) -> bool {
    let mut t0 = 0.0;
    let mut t1 = 1.0;
    let dx = b.x - a.x;
    let dy = b.y - a.y;
    if !clip_axis(-dx, a.x - minx, &mut t0, &mut t1, eps) {
        return false;
    }
    if !clip_axis(dx, maxx - a.x, &mut t0, &mut t1, eps) {
        return false;
    }
    if !clip_axis(-dy, a.y - miny, &mut t0, &mut t1, eps) {
        return false;
    }
    if !clip_axis(dy, maxy - a.y, &mut t0, &mut t1, eps) {
        return false;
    }
    t0 <= t1 + eps
}

fn cell_intersects_polygon(i: i64, j: i64, poly: &[V2], eps: f64) -> bool {
    let minx = i as f64 - 0.5;
    let maxx = i as f64 + 0.5;
    let miny = j as f64 - 0.5;
    let maxy = j as f64 + 0.5;

    for &p in poly {
        if p.x >= minx - eps && p.x <= maxx + eps && p.y >= miny - eps && p.y <= maxy + eps {
            return true;
        }
    }

    let corners = [
        v2(minx, miny),
        v2(minx, maxy),
        v2(maxx, miny),
        v2(maxx, maxy),
    ];
    for &c in &corners {
        if point_in_polygon(c, poly, eps) {
            return true;
        }
    }

    let n = poly.len();
    for k in 0..n {
        let a = poly[k];
        let b = poly[(k + 1) % n];
        if segment_intersects_rect(a, b, minx, maxx, miny, maxy, eps) {
            return true;
        }
    }
    false
}

fn quat_from_matrix(m: [[f64; 3]; 3]) -> [f64; 4] {
    let trace = m[0][0] + m[1][1] + m[2][2];
    let (qw, qx, qy, qz) = if trace > 0.0 {
        let s = (trace + 1.0).sqrt() * 2.0;
        (
            0.25 * s,
            (m[2][1] - m[1][2]) / s,
            (m[0][2] - m[2][0]) / s,
            (m[1][0] - m[0][1]) / s,
        )
    } else if m[0][0] > m[1][1] && m[0][0] > m[2][2] {
        let s = (1.0 + m[0][0] - m[1][1] - m[2][2]).sqrt() * 2.0;
        (
            (m[2][1] - m[1][2]) / s,
            0.25 * s,
            (m[0][1] + m[1][0]) / s,
            (m[0][2] + m[2][0]) / s,
        )
    } else if m[1][1] > m[2][2] {
        let s = (1.0 + m[1][1] - m[0][0] - m[2][2]).sqrt() * 2.0;
        (
            (m[0][2] - m[2][0]) / s,
            (m[0][1] + m[1][0]) / s,
            0.25 * s,
            (m[1][2] + m[2][1]) / s,
        )
    } else {
        let s = (1.0 + m[2][2] - m[0][0] - m[1][1]).sqrt() * 2.0;
        (
            (m[1][0] - m[0][1]) / s,
            (m[0][2] + m[2][0]) / s,
            (m[1][2] + m[2][1]) / s,
            0.25 * s,
        )
    };
    let n = (qw * qw + qx * qx + qy * qy + qz * qz).sqrt();
    [qw / n, qx / n, qy / n, qz / n]
}

fn basis_perp_to_w(w: V3) -> (V3, V3) {
    let helper = if w.z.abs() < 0.9 {
        v3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0)
    } else {
        v3(1.0, 0.0, 0.0)
    };
    let mut a = cross3(helper, w);
    if norm3(a) < 1e-12 {
        a = cross3(v3(0.0, 1.0, 0.0), w);
    }
    a = normalize3(a);
    let b = cross3(w, a);
    (a, b)
}

fn rotate_around_w(a0: V3, b0: V3, theta: f64) -> (V3, V3) {
    let c = theta.cos();
    let s = theta.sin();
    let a = add3(scale3(a0, c), scale3(b0, s));
    let b = add3(scale3(a0, -s), scale3(b0, c));
    (a, b)
}

fn transform_poly(poly: &[V2], d1: V2, d2: V2) -> Vec<V2> {
    poly.iter()
        .map(|&p| solve_q(p, d1, d2))
        .collect()
}

fn main() {
    let mut input = String::new();
    io::stdin().read_to_string(&mut input).unwrap();
    let mut it = input.split_whitespace();

    let nv: usize = match it.next() {
        Some(s) => s.parse().unwrap(),
        None => return,
    };

    let mut poly = Vec::with_capacity(nv);
    for _ in 0..nv {
        let x: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        let y: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
        poly.push(v2(x, y));
    }

    let sx: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let sy: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();
    let sz: f64 = it.next().unwrap().parse().unwrap();

    let mut w = v3(sx, sy, sz);
    let len = norm3(w);
    if len == 0.0 {
        w = v3(0.0, 0.0, 1.0);
    } else {
        w = scale3(w, 1.0 / len);
    }

    let safe_wz = if w.z.abs() < 1e-12 {
        if w.z.is_sign_negative() {
            -1e-12
        } else {
            1e-12
        }
    } else {
        w.z
    };

    const SCALE: f64 = 10.0;
    const ANGLES: usize = 36;

    let (a0, b0) = basis_perp_to_w(w);
    let mut best_count: u128 = u128::MAX;
    let mut best_a = a0;
    let mut best_b = b0;
    let mut best_d1 = v2(0.0, 0.0);
    let mut best_d2 = v2(0.0, 0.0);

    for k in 0..ANGLES {
        let theta = (k as f64) * (PI * 0.5 / ANGLES as f64);
        let (a, b) = rotate_around_w(a0, b0, theta);
        let u = project_to_ground(a, safe_wz, w.x, w.y);
        let v = project_to_ground(b, safe_wz, w.x, w.y);
        let d1 = scale2(add2(u, v), SCALE);
        let d2 = scale2(sub2(u, v), SCALE);
        let det = d1.x * d2.y - d1.y * d2.x;
        if det.abs() < 1e-18 {
            continue;
        }

        let qpoly = transform_poly(&poly, d1, d2);
        let mut minx = f64::INFINITY;
        let mut maxx = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
        let mut miny = f64::INFINITY;
        let mut maxy = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
        for q in &qpoly {
            if q.x < minx {
                minx = q.x;
            }
            if q.x > maxx {
                maxx = q.x;
            }
            if q.y < miny {
                miny = q.y;
            }
            if q.y > maxy {
                maxy = q.y;
            }
        }

        let (_ix0, _ix1, _iy0, _iy1, nx, ny) =
            bbox_to_cell_range(minx, maxx, miny, maxy, 1e-9);
        let est = nx * ny;
        if est < best_count {
            best_count = est;
            best_a = a;
            best_b = b;
            best_d1 = d1;
            best_d2 = d2;
        }
    }

    let qpoly = transform_poly(&poly, best_d1, best_d2);
    let mut minx = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut maxx = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    let mut miny = f64::INFINITY;
    let mut maxy = f64::NEG_INFINITY;
    let mut max_abs = 0.0;
    for q in &qpoly {
        if q.x < minx {
            minx = q.x;
        }
        if q.x > maxx {
            maxx = q.x;
        }
        if q.y < miny {
            miny = q.y;
        }
        if q.y > maxy {
            maxy = q.y;
        }
        let ax = q.x.abs();
        let ay = q.y.abs();
        if ax > max_abs {
            max_abs = ax;
        }
        if ay > max_abs {
            max_abs = ay;
        }
    }
    let eps = 1e-9 * (1.0 + max_abs);

    let (ix0, ix1, iy0, iy1, nx, ny) = bbox_to_cell_range(minx, maxx, miny, maxy, eps);
    let candidate_count = nx.saturating_mul(ny);
    let exact = candidate_count.saturating_mul(nv as u128) <= 5_000_000;

    let mut cells: Vec<(i64, i64)> = Vec::new();
    if exact {
        for j in iy0..=iy1 {
            for i in ix0..=ix1 {
                if cell_intersects_polygon(i, j, &qpoly, eps) {
                    cells.push((i, j));
                }
            }
        }
        if cells.is_empty() {
            for j in iy0..=iy1 {
                for i in ix0..=ix1 {
                    cells.push((i, j));
                }
            }
        }
    } else {
        for j in iy0..=iy1 {
            for i in ix0..=ix1 {
                cells.push((i, j));
            }
        }
    }

    let quat = quat_from_matrix([
        [best_a.x, best_b.x, w.x],
        [best_a.y, best_b.y, w.y],
        [best_a.z, best_b.z, w.z],
    ]);

    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout());
    writeln!(out, "{}", cells.len()).unwrap();

    let height_base = 60.0;
    let height_step = 30.0;
    for (idx, (i, j)) in cells.iter().enumerate() {
        let gx = *i as f64 * best_d1.x + *j as f64 * best_d2.x;
        let gy = *i as f64 * best_d1.y + *j as f64 * best_d2.y;
        let h = height_base + height_step * idx as f64;
        let center_x = gx + h * w.x / safe_wz;
        let center_y = gy + h * w.y / safe_wz;
        let center_z = h;
        writeln!(
            out,
            "{:.12} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12} {:.12}",
            center_x,
            center_y,
            center_z,
            quat[0],
            quat[1],
            quat[2],
            quat[3],
            SCALE
        )
        .unwrap();
    }
}

Test 16: Job-Shop Scheduling (C++) Score: 9.00 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are solving a Job-Shop Scheduling problem in C++.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY problem size from trivial to extremely large scale:
- **Trivial**: 2-3 jobs, 2-3 machines, 5-10 tasks total
- **Extreme**: 1000+ jobs, 50+ machines, 50k+ tasks total

**Input format (stdin):**
Line 1: J M (number of jobs, number of machines)
For each job:
Line: T (number of tasks in this job)
Next T lines: machine duration

**Output format (stdout):**
For each job (J lines total):
T pairs per line: start_time machine start_time machine ... (all tasks for that job)

**Example:**
Input:
2 2
2
0 3
1 2
2
1 2
0 4

Output:
0 0 3 1
0 1 5 0

(Job 0: task 0 starts at t=0 on M0, task 1 at t=3 on M1)
(Job 1: task 0 starts at t=0 on M1, task 1 at t=5 on M0)

**Key Constraints:**
1. Each task in a job must run on its specified machine
2. Tasks within a job must execute in order (task i before task i+1)
3. A machine can only run one task at a time
4. Tasks cannot be interrupted once started

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on problem scale.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Schedule a set of jobs across several machines to finish everything as quickly as possible. Each job is a sequence of tasks that must run in order, and each task needs a specific machine — but a machine can only do one thing at a time.

This classic factory-scheduling problem is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the number of jobs and machines. The baseline greedily starts whichever task can begin earliest.

 1.0000
[3 jobs, 2 machines] Makespan: 12, Baseline: 12, Time: 0.2s - excellent (≤ baseline)

Job-Shop Scheduling - 3 jobs, 2 machines

Algorithm: Job-Shop Scheduling feasibility solver. Strategy: 1) Small/medium instances: greedy simulation that repeatedly schedules the currently earliest-startable job head. 2) Large instances: simple linear job-by-job scheduling for low overhead.

View Code (4421 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <charconv>
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdint>
#include <iostream>
#include <limits>
#include <string>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct Task {
    int machine = 0;
    long long duration = 0;
};

template <class Int>
static inline void appendInt(string& out, Int value) {
    char buf[32];
    auto result = to_chars(buf, buf + 32, value);
    out.append(buf, static_cast<size_t>(result.ptr - buf));
}

static void scheduleSerialByJob(const vector<vector<Task>>& jobs,
                                vector<vector<long long>>& starts,
                                int machineCount) {
    vector<long long> machineReady(machineCount, 0);
    for (size_t j = 0; j < jobs.size(); ++j) {
        const auto& job = jobs[j];
        starts[j].resize(job.size());
        long long jobReady = 0;
        for (size_t i = 0; i < job.size(); ++i) {
            const Task& t = job[i];
            long long s = max(jobReady, machineReady[t.machine]);
            long long f = s + t.duration;
            starts[j][i] = s;
            jobReady = f;
            machineReady[t.machine] = f;
        }
    }
}

static void scheduleGreedySmall(const vector<vector<Task>>& jobs,
                                vector<vector<long long>>& starts,
                                int machineCount,
                                size_t totalTasks) {
    vector<long long> machineReady(machineCount, 0);
    vector<long long> jobReady(jobs.size(), 0);
    vector<size_t> nextIndex(jobs.size(), 0);

    size_t remaining = totalTasks;
    while (remaining > 0) {
        int bestJob = -1;
        int bestMachine = -1;
        long long bestStart = numeric_limits<long long>::max();

        for (int j = 0; j < static_cast<int>(jobs.size()); ++j) {
            if (nextIndex[j] >= jobs[j].size()) {
                continue;
            }
            const Task& t = jobs[j][nextIndex[j]];
            long long s = max(jobReady[j], machineReady[t.machine]);
            if (bestJob == -1 || s < bestStart ||
                (s == bestStart &&
                 (t.machine < bestMachine ||
                  (t.machine == bestMachine && j < bestJob)))) {
                bestJob = j;
                bestMachine = t.machine;
                bestStart = s;
            }
        }

        if (bestJob < 0) {
            break;
        }

        const Task& t = jobs[bestJob][nextIndex[bestJob]];
        long long finish = bestStart + t.duration;
        starts[bestJob][nextIndex[bestJob]] = bestStart;
        jobReady[bestJob] = finish;
        machineReady[t.machine] = finish;
        ++nextIndex[bestJob];
        --remaining;
    }
}

int main() {
    ios::sync_with_stdio(false);
    cin.tie(nullptr);

    int jobCount = 0;
    int machineCount = 0;
    if (!(cin >> jobCount >> machineCount)) {
        return 0;
    }

    vector<vector<Task>> jobs(jobCount);
    size_t totalTasks = 0;
    for (int j = 0; j < jobCount; ++j) {
        int taskCount = 0;
        cin >> taskCount;
        jobs[j].reserve(taskCount);
        totalTasks += static_cast<size_t>(taskCount);
        for (int i = 0; i < taskCount; ++i) {
            Task t;
            cin >> t.machine >> t.duration;
            jobs[j].push_back(t);
        }
    }

    vector<vector<long long>> starts(jobCount);

    // Adaptive choice: the greedy scan is fine for small/medium instances,
    // while the linear strategy is the safest choice for very large ones.
    const unsigned long long estimatedOps =
        static_cast<unsigned long long>(totalTasks) *
        static_cast<unsigned long long>(max(jobCount, 1));

    if (estimatedOps <= 2'000'000ULL && jobCount <= 256) {
        for (int j = 0; j < jobCount; ++j) {
            starts[j].resize(jobs[j].size());
        }
        scheduleGreedySmall(jobs, starts, machineCount, totalTasks);
    } else {
        scheduleSerialByJob(jobs, starts, machineCount);
    }

    string out;
    out.reserve(totalTasks * 24 + static_cast<size_t>(jobCount) * 2);
    for (int j = 0; j < jobCount; ++j) {
        const auto& job = jobs[j];
        for (size_t i = 0; i < job.size(); ++i) {
            if (i) {
                out.push_back(' ');
            }
            appendInt(out, starts[j][i]);
            out.push_back(' ');
            appendInt(out, job[i].machine);
        }
        out.push_back('\n');
    }

    cout << out;
    return 0;
}
