Plan an open-pit mine in a 3D block model. Each block has a value (ore worth money, waste costs money to move) and you can only dig a block once the blocks above it have been removed. The goal is to maximise profit by choosing which blocks to extract and where to dump the waste.

The AI must balance revenue against haulage costs, respect slope constraints for road access, and avoid dumping waste where it blocks future digging. Subpasses increase the pit size and complexity.

 0.0000
[10×10 tiny pit (10 KB)] Ops: 0, Profit: $0, Revenue: $0 / $1,937,000 max, Gen: 0.0s, Run: 0.2s — Score: 0.000

3D Voxel Mining - 10×10 tiny pit (10 KB)

Results: Profit: $0, Revenue: $0 / $1,937,000 theoretical max, Operations: 0, Score: 0.000

View C# Code (10976 chars)
using System;
using System.Collections.Generic;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public static class Program
{
    private const int FullModeThreshold = 1_000_000;
    private const int MaxOpsFull = 12_000;
    private const int MaxOpsLocal = 5_000;
    private const int Inf = int.MaxValue / 4;

    private struct Candidate
    {
        public int Index;
        public long Score;
        public int Depth;

        public Candidate(int index, long score, int depth)
        {
            Index = index;
            Score = score;
            Depth = depth;
        }
    }

    public static void Main()
    {
        var input = new FastInput(Console.OpenStandardInput());

        string header = input.ReadLine();
        while (header.Length == 0)
        {
            header = input.ReadLine();
        }

        string[] parts = header.Split(' ', StringSplitOptions.RemoveEmptyEntries);
        int xCount = int.Parse(parts[0]);
        int yCount = int.Parse(parts[1]);
        int zCount = int.Parse(parts[2]);

        long columnsLong = (long)xCount * yCount;
        int columns = (int)columnsLong;

        byte[] topByte = new byte[columns];
        short[] topZ = new short[columns];

        for (int x = 0; x < xCount; x++)
        {
            int baseIndex = x * yCount;
            for (int y = 0; y < yCount; y++)
            {
                int idx = baseIndex + y;
                byte best = 0;
                short bestZ = 0;

                for (int z = 0; z < zCount; z++)
                {
                    int d = input.Read();
                    if (d != 0)
                    {
                        best = (byte)d;
                        bestZ = (short)z;
                    }
                }

                topByte[idx] = best;
                topZ[idx] = bestZ;
            }
        }

        bool fullMode = columnsLong <= FullModeThreshold;
        int limitX = fullMode ? xCount : Math.Min(xCount, 256);
        int limitY = fullMode ? yCount : Math.Min(yCount, 256);
        int regionCount = limitX * limitY;

        if (regionCount <= 0)
        {
            Console.WriteLine("0");
            return;
        }

        short[] walkHeights;
        if (fullMode)
        {
            walkHeights = topZ;
        }
        else
        {
            walkHeights = new short[regionCount];
            for (int x = 0; x < limitX; x++)
            {
                int fullBase = x * yCount;
                int regionBase = x * limitY;
                for (int y = 0; y < limitY; y++)
                {
                    int globalIdx = fullBase + y;
                    byte b = topByte[globalIdx];
                    walkHeights[regionBase + y] = b == 0 ? (short)-1 : topZ[globalIdx];
                }
            }
        }

        if (walkHeights[0] < 0)
        {
            walkHeights[0] = 0;
        }

        int[] dist = new int[regionCount];
        int[] parent = new int[regionCount];
        Array.Fill(dist, Inf);
        Array.Fill(parent, -1);

        var pq = new PriorityQueue<int, int>();
        dist[0] = 0;
        pq.Enqueue(0, 0);

        while (pq.TryDequeue(out int u, out int priority))
        {
            int du = dist[u];
            if (priority != du)
            {
                continue;
            }

            if (du == Inf)
            {
                continue;
            }

            int x = u / limitY;
            int y = u - x * limitY;
            int hu = walkHeights[u];

            if (x > 0)
            {
                int v = u - limitY;
                Relax(u, v, hu, walkHeights[v], du, dist, parent, pq);
            }
            if (x + 1 < limitX)
            {
                int v = u + limitY;
                Relax(u, v, hu, walkHeights[v], du, dist, parent, pq);
            }
            if (y > 0)
            {
                int v = u - 1;
                Relax(u, v, hu, walkHeights[v], du, dist, parent, pq);
            }
            if (y + 1 < limitY)
            {
                int v = u + 1;
                Relax(u, v, hu, walkHeights[v], du, dist, parent, pq);
            }
        }

        int[] firstChild = new int[regionCount];
        int[] nextSibling = new int[regionCount];
        Array.Fill(firstChild, -1);
        Array.Fill(nextSibling, -1);

        for (int v = 1; v < regionCount; v++)
        {
            int p = parent[v];
            if (p >= 0 && dist[v] < Inf)
            {
                nextSibling[v] = firstChild[p];
                firstChild[p] = v;
            }
        }

        int[] depth = new int[regionCount];
        Array.Fill(depth, -1);
        depth[0] = 0;

        int[] queue = new int[regionCount];
        int qh = 0;
        int qt = 0;
        queue[qt++] = 0;

        while (qh < qt)
        {
            int u = queue[qh++];
            for (int c = firstChild[u]; c != -1; c = nextSibling[c])
            {
                depth[c] = depth[u] + 1;
                queue[qt++] = c;
            }
        }

        var candidates = new List<Candidate>(Math.Min(regionCount, 4096));
        for (int x = 0; x < limitX; x++)
        {
            int regionBase = x * limitY;
            int fullBase = x * yCount;
            for (int y = 0; y < limitY; y++)
            {
                int regionIdx = regionBase + y;
                if (dist[regionIdx] >= Inf)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                byte voxel = topByte[fullBase + y];
                if (voxel == 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                int mass = voxel & 15;
                int value = voxel >> 4;
                if (mass <= 0 || value <= 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                int sourceHeight = walkHeights[regionIdx];
                int adjustedDist = dist[regionIdx];

                if (regionIdx != 0)
                {
                    int p = parent[regionIdx];
                    if (p < 0)
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    int parentHeight = walkHeights[p];
                    if (parentHeight == sourceHeight + 1)
                    {
                        continue;
                    }
                    if (parentHeight == sourceHeight)
                    {
                        adjustedDist += 4;
                    }
                    else if (parentHeight == sourceHeight - 1)
                    {
                        adjustedDist += 1;
                    }
                    else
                    {
                        continue;
                    }
                }

                long perTonNet = 1000L * value - adjustedDist;
                if (perTonNet <= 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                long score = perTonNet * mass;
                candidates.Add(new Candidate(regionIdx, score, depth[regionIdx]));
            }
        }

        int maxOps = fullMode ? MaxOpsFull : MaxOpsLocal;
        if (candidates.Count > maxOps)
        {
            candidates.Sort((a, b) =>
            {
                int cmp = b.Score.CompareTo(a.Score);
                if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
                cmp = b.Depth.CompareTo(a.Depth);
                if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
                return a.Index.CompareTo(b.Index);
            });
            candidates.RemoveRange(maxOps, candidates.Count - maxOps);
        }

        candidates.Sort((a, b) =>
        {
            int cmp = b.Depth.CompareTo(a.Depth);
            if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
            cmp = b.Score.CompareTo(a.Score);
            if (cmp != 0) return cmp;
            return a.Index.CompareTo(b.Index);
        });

        using var writer = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput(), new UTF8Encoding(false), 1 << 16);
        writer.NewLine = "\n";
        writer.WriteLine(candidates.Count);

        var pathNodes = new List<int>(64);
        var line = new StringBuilder(256);

        foreach (var candidate in candidates)
        {
            int idx = candidate.Index;
            int x = idx / limitY;
            int y = idx - x * limitY;
            int globalIdx = x * yCount + y;
            int z = topZ[globalIdx];

            pathNodes.Clear();
            int cur = idx;
            while (true)
            {
                pathNodes.Add(cur);
                if (cur == 0)
                {
                    break;
                }

                cur = parent[cur];
            }

            line.Clear();
            line.Append(pathNodes.Count - 1);
            line.Append(' ').Append(x);
            line.Append(' ').Append(y);
            line.Append(' ').Append(z);

            for (int i = 0; i + 1 < pathNodes.Count; i++)
            {
                int a = pathNodes[i];
                int b = pathNodes[i + 1];
                int ax = a / limitY;
                int ay = a - ax * limitY;
                int bx = b / limitY;
                int by = b - bx * limitY;
                line.Append(' ').Append(bx - ax).Append(' ').Append(by - ay);
            }

            line.Append(' ').Append(0).Append(' ').Append(0);
            writer.WriteLine(line.ToString());
        }

        writer.Flush();
    }

    private static void Relax(int u, int v, int hu, int hv, int du, int[] dist, int[] parent, PriorityQueue<int, int> pq)
    {
        if (hv < 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        int diff = hv - hu;
        if (diff < -1 || diff > 1)
        {
            return;
        }

        int w = diff > 0 ? 0 : diff == 0 ? 1 : 5;
        int nd = du + w;
        if (nd < dist[v])
        {
            dist[v] = nd;
            parent[v] = u;
            pq.Enqueue(v, nd);
        }
    }

    private sealed class FastInput
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer;
        private int _length;
        private int _position;

        public FastInput(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
            _buffer = new byte[1 << 20];
        }

        public int Read()
        {
            if (_position >= _length)
            {
                _length = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _position = 0;
                if (_length <= 0)
                {
                    return -1;
                }
            }

            return _buffer[_position++];
        }

        public string ReadLine()
        {
            var sb = new StringBuilder();
            int b;
            while ((b = Read()) != -1)
            {
                if (b == '\n')
                {
                    break;
                }

                if (b != '\r')
                {
                    sb.Append((char)b);
                }
            }

            return sb.ToString();
        }
    }
}

Test 20: Drillhole Data Validation (Rust) Score: 3.72 / 14
Typical Prompt:
You are writing a Rust program to validate drillhole assay data.


**The Challenge:**
Your Rust program will be tested with drillhole datasets ranging from small exploration projects to
massive mining surveys. The same program must work efficiently across ALL scales from 10 holes to
200,000 holes.

**Problem:**
Given drillhole data from a mining exploration project, identify samples that likely contain typos or
measurement errors. These outliers don't correlate well with spatially neighboring data points.

**Target environment specs**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler information**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

**Drillhole Data:**
- Each hole has a 3D path: start point (x,y,z) + direction vector + length
- Samples are taken at intervals along each hole
- Each sample has measurements for multiple properties

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_holes num_properties
property_name_1 property_name_2 ...
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
depth prop1 prop2 ...
depth prop1 prop2 ...
etc.
hole_id start_x start_y start_z dir_x dir_y dir_z length num_samples
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
M (number of suspect entries found)
hole_id sample_idx property_name confidence (for each suspect, sorted by confidence desc)
```

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc and only the standard rust library.
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle up to thousands of holes with thousands of samples each
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Report at least the most obvious outliers
6. Confidence score should reflect how anomalous the value is
7. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on dataset scale.

The program should be robust enough to handle edge cases like missing data, duplicate entries, and varying data distributions.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Parameters: (see typical prompt)View exact prompt
View raw AI output
View chain of thought

Given a set of mining drillholes — each a 3D path with measurements taken at intervals along its length — identify the data entries that are most likely to be errors (typos, instrument glitches, or transcription mistakes).

The AI must compare each measurement against its spatial neighbours: a reading that disagrees with nearby drillholes is suspect. Subpasses increase the number of drillholes and properties, requiring efficient spatial reasoning at scale.

 0.6247
[5 holes, 3 properties, 50 samples] Found: 19, Actual errors: 8, TP: 7, FP: 12, FN: 1, P: 0.37, R: 0.88, F1: 0.52, Time: 0.06s

Subpass 0: 5 holes, 3 properties, 50 samples

Score: 0.62

Details: [5 holes, 3 properties, 50 samples] Found: 19, Actual errors: 8, TP: 7, FP: 12, FN: 1, P: 0.37, R: 0.88, F1: 0.52, Time: 0.06s

Reasoning 
No reasoning provided
Rust Code 
use std::collections::HashMap;
use std::io::{self, Read, Write};

const MAX_HOLE_WINDOW: usize = 32;
const LOCAL_WINDOW_THRESHOLD_SMALL: usize = 20_000;
const LOCAL_WINDOW_THRESHOLD_MEDIUM: usize = 200_000;
const SMALL_MODE_THRESHOLD: usize = 50_000;

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct Stats {
    median: f64,
    scale: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone)]
struct HoleMeta {
    id: String,
    samples: Vec<usize>,
    interval_est: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct Sample {
    hole: usize,
    depth: f64,
    ordinal: usize,
    x: f64,
    y: f64,
    z: f64,
}

#[derive(Clone, Copy)]
struct Suspect {
    hole: usize,
    sample_idx: usize,
    prop: usize,
    confidence: f64,
}

struct Scanner {
    input: Vec<u8>,
    idx: usize,
}

impl Scanner {
    fn new() -> io::Result<Self> {
        let mut input = Vec::new();
        io::stdin().read_to_end(&mut input)?;
        Ok(Self { input, idx: 0 })
    }

    fn skip_ws(&mut self) {
        while self.idx < self.input.len() && self.input[self.idx].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            self.idx += 1;
        }
    }

    fn next_token(&mut self) -> Option<&[u8]> {
        self.skip_ws();
        if self.idx >= self.input.len() {
            return None;
        }
        let start = self.idx;
        while self.idx < self.input.len() && !self.input[self.idx].is_ascii_whitespace() {
            self.idx += 1;
        }
        Some(&self.input[start..self.idx])
    }

    fn next_string(&mut self) -> Option<String> {
        self.next_token().map(|tok| String::from_utf8_lossy(tok).into_owned())
    }

    fn next_usize(&mut self) -> Option<usize> {
        let tok = self.next_token()?;
        std::str::from_utf8(tok).ok()?.parse::<usize>().ok()
    }

    fn next_f64(&mut self) -> Option<f64> {
        let tok = self.next_token()?;
        parse_f64_token(tok)
    }
}

fn parse_f64_token(tok: &[u8]) -> Option<f64> {
    let s = std::str::from_utf8(tok).ok()?;
    let v = s.parse::<f64>().ok()?;
    if v.is_finite() {
        Some(v)
    } else {
        None
    }
}

fn quantile_sorted(vals: &[f64], q: f64) -> f64 {
    if vals.is_empty() {
        return 0.0;
    }
    if vals.len() == 1 {
        return vals[0];
    }
    let q = q.clamp(0.0, 1.0);
    let pos = q * (vals.len() as f64 - 1.0);
    let lo = pos.floor() as usize;
    let hi = pos.ceil() as usize;
    if lo == hi {
        vals[lo]
    } else {
        let frac = pos - lo as f64;
        vals[lo] * (1.0 - frac) + vals[hi] * frac
    }
}

fn global_stats(col: &[f64]) -> Stats {
    let mut vals: Vec<f64> = col.iter().copied().filter(|v| v.is_finite()).collect();
    if vals.is_empty() {
        return Stats {
            median: 0.0,
            scale: 1.0,
        };
    }
    vals.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.total_cmp(b));
    let median = quantile_sorted(&vals, 0.5);
    let q1 = quantile_sorted(&vals, 0.25);
    let q3 = quantile_sorted(&vals, 0.75);
    let iqr_scale = (q3 - q1).abs() / 1.349;
    let floor = (median.abs().max(1.0)) * 1e-12 + 1e-12;
    let scale = iqr_scale.max(floor);
    Stats { median, scale }
}

fn global_score(value: f64, global: Stats) -> f64 {
    (((value - global.median).abs() / global.scale).min(1e12)).max(0.0)
}

fn robust_score_small_window(
    value: f64,
    vals: &mut [f64; MAX_HOLE_WINDOW],
    count: usize,
    global: Stats,
) -> Option<f64> {
    if count < 3 {
        return None;
    }
    let slice = &mut vals[..count];
    slice.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.total_cmp(b));
    let med = quantile_sorted(slice, 0.5);
    let mut devs = [0.0_f64; MAX_HOLE_WINDOW];
    for i in 0..count {
        devs[i] = (slice[i] - med).abs();
    }
    devs[..count].sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.total_cmp(b));
    let mad = quantile_sorted(&devs[..count], 0.5) * 1.4826;
    let floor = (med.abs().max(global.median.abs()).max(1.0)) * 1e-12 + 1e-12;
    let scale = mad.max(global.scale * 0.25).max(floor);
    Some((((value - med).abs() / scale).min(1e12)).max(0.0))
}

fn robust_score_dynamic_window(value: f64, vals: &mut Vec<f64>, global: Stats) -> Option<f64> {
    if vals.len() < 3 {
        return None;
    }
    vals.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.total_cmp(b));
    let med = quantile_sorted(vals, 0.5);
    let mut devs: Vec<f64> = vals.iter().map(|v| (v - med).abs()).collect();
    devs.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.total_cmp(b));
    let mad = quantile_sorted(&devs, 0.5) * 1.4826;
    let floor = (med.abs().max(global.median.abs()).max(1.0)) * 1e-12 + 1e-12;
    let scale = mad.max(global.scale * 0.25).max(floor);
    Some((((value - med).abs() / scale).min(1e12)).max(0.0))
}

fn hole_window_radius(hole_len: usize, total_samples: usize) -> usize {
    if hole_len <= 1 {
        return 0;
    }
    let base = if total_samples < LOCAL_WINDOW_THRESHOLD_SMALL {
        4
    } else if total_samples < LOCAL_WINDOW_THRESHOLD_MEDIUM {
        3
    } else {
        2
    };
    let cap = (hole_len - 1) / 2;
    base.min(cap).max(1)
}

fn median_interval(holes: &[HoleMeta]) -> f64 {
    let mut vals: Vec<f64> = holes
        .iter()
        .map(|h| h.interval_est)
        .filter(|v| v.is_finite() && *v > 0.0)
        .collect();
    if vals.is_empty() {
        return 1.0;
    }
    vals.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| a.total_cmp(b));
    quantile_sorted(&vals, 0.5).max(1e-9)
}

fn cell_key(x: f64, y: f64, z: f64, cell: f64) -> (i64, i64, i64) {
    let inv = 1.0 / cell;
    (
        (x * inv).floor() as i64,
        (y * inv).floor() as i64,
        (z * inv).floor() as i64,
    )
}

fn build_small_mode_neighbors(
    samples: &[Sample],
    cell_size: f64,
    radius2: f64,
) -> Vec<Vec<usize>> {
    let mut grid: HashMap<(i64, i64, i64), Vec<usize>> = HashMap::new();
    for (idx, s) in samples.iter().enumerate() {
        grid.entry(cell_key(s.x, s.y, s.z, cell_size))
            .or_default()
            .push(idx);
    }

    let mut neighbors: Vec<Vec<usize>> = Vec::with_capacity(samples.len());
    for (idx, s) in samples.iter().enumerate() {
        let (cx, cy, cz) = cell_key(s.x, s.y, s.z, cell_size);
        let mut list = Vec::new();
        for dx in -1..=1 {
            for dy in -1..=1 {
                for dz in -1..=1 {
                    if let Some(bucket) = grid.get(&(cx + dx, cy + dy, cz + dz)) {
                        for &other in bucket {
                            if other == idx {
                                continue;
                            }
                            let o = samples[other];
                            let ddx = o.x - s.x;
                            let ddy = o.y - s.y;
                            let ddz = o.z - s.z;
                            let dist2 = ddx * ddx + ddy * ddy + ddz * ddz;
                            if dist2 <= radius2 {
                                list.push(other);
                            }
                        }
                    }
                }
            }
        }
        neighbors.push(list);
    }
    neighbors
}

fn main() {
    let _ = run();
}

fn run() -> io::Result<()> {
    let mut scanner = Scanner::new()?;
    let num_holes = scanner.next_usize().unwrap_or(0);
    let num_props = scanner.next_usize().unwrap_or(0);

    let mut prop_names: Vec<String> = Vec::with_capacity(num_props);
    for i in 0..num_props {
        prop_names.push(
            scanner
                .next_string()
                .unwrap_or_else(|| format!("prop_{}", i)),
        );
    }

    let mut holes: Vec<HoleMeta> = Vec::with_capacity(num_holes);
    let mut samples: Vec<Sample> = Vec::new();
    let mut props: Vec<Vec<f64>> = (0..num_props).map(|_| Vec::new()).collect();

    for _ in 0..num_holes {
        let hole_id = match scanner.next_string() {
            Some(v) => v,
            None => break,
        };
        let sx = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let sy = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let sz = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let dx = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let dy = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let dz = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let length = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
        let num_samples = scanner.next_usize().unwrap_or(0);

        let norm = (dx * dx + dy * dy + dz * dz).sqrt();
        let (ux, uy, uz) = if norm > 0.0 {
            (dx / norm, dy / norm, dz / norm)
        } else {
            (0.0, 0.0, 0.0)
        };

        for col in &mut props {
            col.reserve(num_samples);
        }
        samples.reserve(num_samples);

        let hole_idx = holes.len();
        holes.push(HoleMeta {
            id: hole_id,
            samples: Vec::with_capacity(num_samples),
            interval_est: if num_samples > 0 {
                length.abs() / num_samples as f64
            } else {
                0.0
            },
        });

        for sample_ord in 0..num_samples {
            let depth = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(0.0);
            let d = if depth.is_finite() {
                if length.is_finite() && length > 0.0 {
                    depth.max(0.0).min(length)
                } else {
                    depth.max(0.0)
                }
            } else {
                0.0
            };
            let idx = samples.len();
            let x = sx + ux * d;
            let y = sy + uy * d;
            let z = sz + uz * d;
            samples.push(Sample {
                hole: hole_idx,
                depth: d,
                ordinal: sample_ord,
                x,
                y,
                z,
            });
            holes[hole_idx].samples.push(idx);

            for p in 0..num_props {
                let val = scanner.next_f64().unwrap_or(f64::NAN);
                props[p].push(val);
            }
        }
    }

    let total_samples = samples.len();
    let small_mode = total_samples <= SMALL_MODE_THRESHOLD;

    let mut hole_order_pos = vec![0_usize; total_samples];
    for hole in &mut holes {
        hole.samples.sort_unstable_by(|&a, &b| {
            samples[a]
                .depth
                .total_cmp(&samples[b].depth)
                .then_with(|| samples[a].ordinal.cmp(&samples[b].ordinal))
        });
        for (rank, &idx) in hole.samples.iter().enumerate() {
            hole_order_pos[idx] = rank;
        }
    }

    let median_step = median_interval(&holes);
    let spatial_neighbors = if small_mode && total_samples > 0 {
        let cell_size = (if total_samples < 10_000 {
            median_step * 2.0
        } else {
            median_step * 1.5
        })
        .max(1e-9);
        let radius = median_step * if total_samples < 10_000 { 3.0 } else { 2.5 };
        Some(build_small_mode_neighbors(
            &samples,
            cell_size,
            radius * radius,
        ))
    } else {
        None
    };

    let mut globals: Vec<Stats> = Vec::with_capacity(num_props);
    for col in &props {
        globals.push(global_stats(col));
    }

    let threshold = if total_samples < 2_000 {
        3.25
    } else if total_samples < 50_000 {
        3.6
    } else {
        4.0
    };

    let mut suspects: Vec<Suspect> = Vec::new();
    let mut small_spatial_buf: Vec<f64> = Vec::new();
    let mut small_window_buf = [0.0_f64; MAX_HOLE_WINDOW];

    for p in 0..num_props {
        let global = globals[p];
        let col = &props[p];

        for idx in 0..total_samples {
            let value = col[idx];
            if !value.is_finite() {
                continue;
            }

            let hole_idx = samples[idx].hole;
            let order = &holes[hole_idx].samples;
            let radius = hole_window_radius(order.len(), total_samples);

            let mut local_count = 0usize;
            if radius > 0 {
                let rank = hole_order_pos[idx];
                let start = rank.saturating_sub(radius);
                let end = (rank + radius + 1).min(order.len());
                for &other_idx in &order[start..end] {
                    if other_idx == idx {
                        continue;
                    }
                    let ov = col[other_idx];
                    if ov.is_finite() && local_count < MAX_HOLE_WINDOW {
                        small_window_buf[local_count] = ov;
                        local_count += 1;
                    }
                }
            }

            let mut score = if let Some(s) =
                robust_score_small_window(value, &mut small_window_buf, local_count, global)
            {
                s
            } else {
                global_score(value, global)
            };

            if small_mode {
                if let Some(neighs) = spatial_neighbors.as_ref() {
                    let neigh_list = &neighs[idx];
                    if !neigh_list.is_empty() {
                        small_spatial_buf.clear();
                        small_spatial_buf.reserve(neigh_list.len());
                        for &other_idx in neigh_list {
                            let ov = col[other_idx];
                            if ov.is_finite() {
                                small_spatial_buf.push(ov);
                            }
                        }
                        if let Some(spatial_score) =
                            robust_score_dynamic_window(value, &mut small_spatial_buf, global)
                        {
                            score = score.max(spatial_score * 0.9);
                        }
                    }
                }
            }

            if score >= threshold {
                suspects.push(Suspect {
                    hole: hole_idx,
                    sample_idx: samples[idx].ordinal,
                    prop: p,
                    confidence: score,
                });
            }
        }
    }

    suspects.sort_unstable_by(|a, b| {
        b.confidence
            .total_cmp(&a.confidence)
            .then_with(|| holes[a.hole].id.cmp(&holes[b.hole].id))
            .then_with(|| a.sample_idx.cmp(&b.sample_idx))
            .then_with(|| prop_names[a.prop].cmp(&prop_names[b.prop]))
    });

    let mut out = io::BufWriter::new(io::stdout().lock());
    writeln!(out, "{}", suspects.len())?;
    for s in suspects {
        writeln!(
            out,
            "{} {} {} {:.6}",
            holes[s.hole].id, s.sample_idx, prop_names[s.prop], s.confidence
        )?;
    }
    out.flush()?;
    Ok(())
}

Test 21: N-Dimensional Snake Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play an N-Dimensional Snake Game.

You must write a C++ solver that can handle ANY dimensional complexity from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: 2D games (eg 8x8), few obstacles, basic pathfinding
- **Medium**: 3D-4D games (eg 6x6x6 to 8x8x8x8), moderate obstacles, intermediate planning
- **Large**: 6D-8D games (eg 4x4x4x4x4x4 to 6x6x6x6x6x6x6x6), many obstacles, complex pathfinding
- **Extreme**: 10D-20D games (eg 3x3x3... to 4x4x4...)

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ snake player will be tested with games ranging from simple 2D boards to massive 20D hypercubes.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL dimensionalities.

**Problem:**
Like classic Nokia snake, but generalized to arbitrary dimensions. The snake occupies a series of
connected integer grid points in N-D space, moves one step per turn along one axis (+1 or -1),
grows when eating food, and dies if it hits an obstacle or itself.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace / newline seperated):**
```
D max_turns (D = number of dimensions, max_turns = turn limit)
bounds (D integers: size of space in each dimension)
snake_length start_pos (length, then D integers for head position)
F (number of food items)
F lines: food positions (D integers each)
O (number of obstacles)
O lines: obstacle positions (D integers each)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Each turn, output: axis direction
- axis: 0 to D-1 (which dimension to move along)
- direction: -1 or +1 (which way to move)

**Game ends when:**
- Snake eats all food (win)
- Snake hits obstacle or itself (lose)
- Max turns reached

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Play the classic Snake game, but in more than two dimensions. The snake moves through an N-dimensional grid, eating food to grow while avoiding its own body and the walls. In higher dimensions the grid is enormous but self-collision becomes far trickier to reason about.

Subpasses increase the number of dimensions, making the state space explode. The AI must write a strategy that plans a safe path to food without boxing itself in.

Test 22: Tetris Game (C#) Score: 0.81 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to play Tetris optimally.

Your C# Tetris player will be tested with games ranging from short practice sessions to marathon games.

**Problem:**
Standard Tetris rules apply - variable width board (typically 10-20 columns),
pieces fall from top, clear lines by filling them completely, game over when pieces stack to the top.
The program receives pieces one at a time and must decide optimal placement.

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
```
width height num_pieces
For each piece:
Line: piece_type (I, O, T, S, Z, J, L)
Program outputs: rotation column
Then receives: result (ok, cleared_N, gameover)
```

**Output format (stdout, 1 line per piece, whitespace seperated decimal integers):**
rotation column
- rotation: 0-3 (number of 90° clockwise rotations)
- column: 0 to width-1 (leftmost column of piece)

**Piece Types:**
- I: 4x1 line piece
- O: 2x2 square piece
- T: T-shaped piece
- S: S-shaped piece (right-leaning)
- Z: Z-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- J: L-shaped piece (left-leaning)
- L: L-shaped piece (right-leaning)

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable board widths and long game sequences
4. Must place a peice within 5 seconds of it being written to STDIN.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14


Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on game complexity.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

View chain of thought

Write an AI that plays Tetris. Given the current board state and the next piece, choose where to place it to survive as long as possible and clear as many lines as you can. Good play means keeping the board flat, avoiding buried holes, and setting up multi-line clears.

Subpasses increase the board size and speed, demanding smarter lookahead and evaluation. The AI must balance short-term survival with long-term board health.

 0.0000
[20 pieces] Placed: 20/20, Lines: 0, End: completed, Time: 0.12s

Tetris AI - 20 pieces

Approach: nullable enable

View C# Code (26010 chars)
using System;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public static class Program
{
    public static void Main()
    {
        var input = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        var output = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput())
        {
            AutoFlush = true,
            NewLine = "\n"
        };

        int width = input.NextInt();
        if (width == int.MinValue)
        {
            return;
        }

        int height = input.NextInt();
        int numPieces = input.NextInt();

        if (width <= 0 || height <= 0 || numPieces < 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        var ai = new TetrisAi(width, height);

        for (int i = 0; i < numPieces; i++)
        {
            string? pieceToken = input.NextToken();
            if (pieceToken == null)
            {
                break;
            }

            PieceKind piece = ParsePiece(pieceToken[0]);
            Move move = ai.ChooseMove(piece);

            output.Write(move.Rotation);
            output.Write(' ');
            output.Write(move.Column);
            output.WriteLine();
            output.Flush();

            string? result = input.NextToken();
            if (result == null || result == "gameover")
            {
                break;
            }

            ai.CommitChosenBoard();
        }
    }

    private static PieceKind ParsePiece(char c)
    {
        return char.ToUpperInvariant(c) switch
        {
            'I' => PieceKind.I,
            'O' => PieceKind.O,
            'T' => PieceKind.T,
            'S' => PieceKind.S,
            'Z' => PieceKind.Z,
            'J' => PieceKind.J,
            'L' => PieceKind.L,
            _ => PieceKind.O
        };
    }

    private enum PieceKind
    {
        I = 0,
        O = 1,
        T = 2,
        S = 3,
        Z = 4,
        J = 5,
        L = 6
    }

    private readonly struct Move
    {
        public readonly int Rotation;
        public readonly int Column;

        public Move(int rotation, int column)
        {
            Rotation = rotation;
            Column = column;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct Cell
    {
        public readonly int X;
        public readonly int Y;

        public Cell(int x, int y)
        {
            X = x;
            Y = y;
        }
    }

    private sealed class Rotation
    {
        public readonly int Width;
        public readonly int Height;
        public readonly ulong[] RowMasks;
        public readonly int[] RowCellCounts;
        public readonly double AverageCellY;

        public Rotation(int width, int height, ulong[] rowMasks, int[] rowCellCounts, double averageCellY)
        {
            Width = width;
            Height = height;
            RowMasks = rowMasks;
            RowCellCounts = rowCellCounts;
            AverageCellY = averageCellY;
        }
    }

    private sealed class PieceDefinition
    {
        public readonly Rotation[] Rotations;

        public PieceDefinition(Rotation[] rotations)
        {
            Rotations = rotations;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct BoardStats
    {
        public readonly int AggregateHeight;
        public readonly int MaxHeight;
        public readonly int Holes;
        public readonly int Bumpiness;
        public readonly int RowTransitions;
        public readonly int ColumnTransitions;
        public readonly int WellSum;
        public readonly int FilledCells;

        public BoardStats(
            int aggregateHeight,
            int maxHeight,
            int holes,
            int bumpiness,
            int rowTransitions,
            int columnTransitions,
            int wellSum,
            int filledCells)
        {
            AggregateHeight = aggregateHeight;
            MaxHeight = maxHeight;
            Holes = holes;
            Bumpiness = bumpiness;
            RowTransitions = rowTransitions;
            ColumnTransitions = columnTransitions;
            WellSum = wellSum;
            FilledCells = filledCells;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct StrategyProfile
    {
        public readonly double FutureWeight;
        public readonly double RiskBlend;
        public readonly double LineClearWeight;
        public readonly double ErodedWeight;
        public readonly double LandingWeight;
        public readonly double AggregateHeightWeight;
        public readonly double HolesWeight;
        public readonly double BumpinessWeight;
        public readonly double RowTransitionsWeight;
        public readonly double ColumnTransitionsWeight;
        public readonly double WellsWeight;
        public readonly double MaxHeightWeight;

        public StrategyProfile(
            double futureWeight,
            double riskBlend,
            double lineClearWeight,
            double erodedWeight,
            double landingWeight,
            double aggregateHeightWeight,
            double holesWeight,
            double bumpinessWeight,
            double rowTransitionsWeight,
            double columnTransitionsWeight,
            double wellsWeight,
            double maxHeightWeight)
        {
            FutureWeight = futureWeight;
            RiskBlend = riskBlend;
            LineClearWeight = lineClearWeight;
            ErodedWeight = erodedWeight;
            LandingWeight = landingWeight;
            AggregateHeightWeight = aggregateHeightWeight;
            HolesWeight = holesWeight;
            BumpinessWeight = bumpinessWeight;
            RowTransitionsWeight = rowTransitionsWeight;
            ColumnTransitionsWeight = columnTransitionsWeight;
            WellsWeight = wellsWeight;
            MaxHeightWeight = maxHeightWeight;
        }
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _length;
        private int _position;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
        }

        private int ReadByte()
        {
            if (_position >= _length)
            {
                _length = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _position = 0;
                if (_length <= 0)
                {
                    return -1;
                }
            }

            return _buffer[_position++];
        }

        public string? NextToken()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = ReadByte();
                if (c == -1)
                {
                    return null;
                }
            }
            while (c <= 32);

            var sb = new StringBuilder(16);
            do
            {
                sb.Append((char)c);
                c = ReadByte();
            }
            while (c != -1 && c > 32);

            return sb.ToString();
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            string? token = NextToken();
            return token == null ? int.MinValue : int.Parse(token, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        }
    }

    private sealed class TetrisAi
    {
        private const double DeadScore = -1_000_000_000.0;

        private static readonly PieceDefinition[] Pieces = BuildPieces();

        private readonly int _width;
        private readonly int _height;
        private readonly ulong _fullMask;
        private readonly ulong[] _currentBoard;
        private ulong[] _pendingBoard;
        private readonly ulong[] _scratchA;
        private readonly ulong[] _scratchB;

        private static readonly StrategyProfile Aggressive = new StrategyProfile(
            futureWeight: 0.0,
            riskBlend: 0.05,
            lineClearWeight: 8.0,
            erodedWeight: 1.3,
            landingWeight: -0.16,
            aggregateHeightWeight: -0.55,
            holesWeight: -9.0,
            bumpinessWeight: -0.80,
            rowTransitionsWeight: -0.20,
            columnTransitionsWeight: -0.32,
            wellsWeight: -0.60,
            maxHeightWeight: -5.5);

        private static readonly StrategyProfile Balanced = new StrategyProfile(
            futureWeight: 0.0,
            riskBlend: 0.18,
            lineClearWeight: 10.0,
            erodedWeight: 1.6,
            landingWeight: -0.20,
            aggregateHeightWeight: -0.70,
            holesWeight: -12.0,
            bumpinessWeight: -1.00,
            rowTransitionsWeight: -0.25,
            columnTransitionsWeight: -0.40,
            wellsWeight: -0.80,
            maxHeightWeight: -7.0);

        private static readonly StrategyProfile Survival = new StrategyProfile(
            futureWeight: 0.0,
            riskBlend: 0.40,
            lineClearWeight: 11.0,
            erodedWeight: 1.1,
            landingWeight: -0.24,
            aggregateHeightWeight: -0.85,
            holesWeight: -16.0,
            bumpinessWeight: -1.20,
            rowTransitionsWeight: -0.30,
            columnTransitionsWeight: -0.48,
            wellsWeight: -1.00,
            maxHeightWeight: -9.0);

        public TetrisAi(int width, int height)
        {
            _width = width;
            _height = height;
            _fullMask = width >= 64 ? ulong.MaxValue : ((1UL << width) - 1UL);
            _currentBoard = new ulong[height];
            _pendingBoard = new ulong[height];
            _scratchA = new ulong[height];
            _scratchB = new ulong[height];
        }

        public Move ChooseMove(PieceKind pieceKind)
        {
            BoardStats currentStats = AnalyzeBoard(_currentBoard);
            StrategyProfile strategy = ChooseStrategy(currentStats);

            double bestScore = DeadScore;
            int bestRotation = 0;
            int bestColumn = 0;
            int bestCleared = -1;
            int bestLandingY = int.MaxValue;
            bool found = false;

            Rotation[] rotations = Pieces[(int)pieceKind].Rotations;

            for (int rotationIndex = 0; rotationIndex < rotations.Length; rotationIndex++)
            {
                Rotation rotation = rotations[rotationIndex];
                int maxColumn = _width - rotation.Width;
                if (maxColumn < 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                for (int column = 0; column <= maxColumn; column++)
                {
                    if (!TrySimulateMove(_currentBoard, rotation, column, _scratchA, out int landingY, out int clearedLines, out int erodedCells))
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    double score = ScoreImmediate(_scratchA, rotation, landingY, clearedLines, erodedCells, strategy);
                    if (strategy.FutureWeight != 0.0)
                    {
                        score += strategy.FutureWeight * EvaluateFutureScore(_scratchA, strategy, _scratchB);
                    }

                    if (!found ||
                        score > bestScore + 1e-9 ||
                        (Math.Abs(score - bestScore) <= 1e-9 &&
                         (clearedLines > bestCleared ||
                          (clearedLines == bestCleared && landingY < bestLandingY) ||
                          (clearedLines == bestCleared && landingY == bestLandingY && column < bestColumn))))
                    {
                        bestScore = score;
                        bestRotation = rotationIndex;
                        bestColumn = column;
                        bestCleared = clearedLines;
                        bestLandingY = landingY;
                        CopyBoard(_scratchA, _pendingBoard);
                        found = true;
                    }
                }
            }

            if (!found)
            {
                CopyBoard(_currentBoard, _pendingBoard);
                return new Move(0, 0);
            }

            return new Move(bestRotation, bestColumn);
        }

        public void CommitChosenBoard()
        {
            Array.Copy(_pendingBoard, _currentBoard, _height);
        }

        private StrategyProfile ChooseStrategy(BoardStats stats)
        {
            double area = (double)_width * _height;
            double maxHeightRatio = _height > 0 ? stats.MaxHeight / (double)_height : 0.0;
            double holeRatio = area > 0 ? stats.Holes / area : 0.0;
            double roughRatio = _height > 0 && _width > 1
                ? stats.Bumpiness / ((double)(_width - 1) * _height)
                : 0.0;
            double fillRatio = area > 0 ? stats.FilledCells / area : 0.0;

            double danger = maxHeightRatio * 0.60 + holeRatio * 20.0 + roughRatio * 2.0 + fillRatio * 0.15;

            if (stats.MaxHeight >= _height - 3 || holeRatio > 0.12 || stats.Holes >= _width * 2 || danger >= 0.85)
            {
                return Survival;
            }

            if (danger >= 0.35)
            {
                return Balanced;
            }

            return Aggressive;
        }

        private double EvaluateFutureScore(ulong[] board, StrategyProfile strategy, ulong[] work)
        {
            double sum = 0.0;
            double min = double.PositiveInfinity;

            for (int pieceIndex = 0; pieceIndex < Pieces.Length; pieceIndex++)
            {
                double score = EvaluateBestImmediate(board, (PieceKind)pieceIndex, strategy, work);
                sum += score;
                if (score < min)
                {
                    min = score;
                }
            }

            double avg = sum / Pieces.Length;
            return avg * (1.0 - strategy.RiskBlend) + min * strategy.RiskBlend;
        }

        private double EvaluateBestImmediate(ulong[] board, PieceKind pieceKind, StrategyProfile strategy, ulong[] work)
        {
            double best = DeadScore;
            Rotation[] rotations = Pieces[(int)pieceKind].Rotations;

            for (int rotationIndex = 0; rotationIndex < rotations.Length; rotationIndex++)
            {
                Rotation rotation = rotations[rotationIndex];
                int maxColumn = _width - rotation.Width;
                if (maxColumn < 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                for (int column = 0; column <= maxColumn; column++)
                {
                    if (!TrySimulateMove(board, rotation, column, work, out int landingY, out int clearedLines, out int erodedCells))
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    double score = ScoreImmediate(work, rotation, landingY, clearedLines, erodedCells, strategy);
                    if (score > best)
                    {
                        best = score;
                    }
                }
            }

            return best;
        }

        private double ScoreImmediate(
            ulong[] boardAfterMove,
            Rotation rotation,
            int landingY,
            int clearedLines,
            int erodedCells,
            StrategyProfile strategy)
        {
            BoardStats stats = AnalyzeBoard(boardAfterMove);

            double avgHeight = stats.AggregateHeight / (double)_width;
            double avgHoles = stats.Holes / (double)_width;
            double avgBumpiness = _width > 1 ? stats.Bumpiness / (double)(_width - 1) : 0.0;
            double avgRowTransitions = stats.RowTransitions / (double)Math.Max(1, _height);
            double avgColumnTransitions = stats.ColumnTransitions / (double)Math.Max(1, _width);
            double avgWells = stats.WellSum / (double)_width;
            double maxRatio = _height > 0 ? stats.MaxHeight / (double)_height : 0.0;
            double landingRatio = _height > 1 ? (landingY + rotation.AverageCellY) / (double)(_height - 1) : 0.0;

            double score =
                strategy.LineClearWeight * clearedLines +
                strategy.ErodedWeight * (erodedCells * clearedLines) +
                strategy.LandingWeight * landingRatio +
                strategy.AggregateHeightWeight * avgHeight +
                strategy.HolesWeight * avgHoles +
                strategy.BumpinessWeight * avgBumpiness +
                strategy.RowTransitionsWeight * avgRowTransitions +
                strategy.ColumnTransitionsWeight * avgColumnTransitions +
                strategy.WellsWeight * avgWells +
                strategy.MaxHeightWeight * maxRatio;

            return score;
        }

        private BoardStats AnalyzeBoard(ulong[] board)
        {
            Span<int> heights = stackalloc int[64];
            int aggregateHeight = 0;
            int maxHeight = 0;
            int holes = 0;
            int filledCells = 0;

            for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
            {
                bool seenBlock = false;
                int h = 0;
                int columnHoles = 0;

                for (int y = _height - 1; y >= 0; y--)
                {
                    bool filled = ((board[y] >> x) & 1UL) != 0;
                    if (filled)
                    {
                        seenBlock = true;
                        if (h == 0)
                        {
                            h = y + 1;
                        }

                        filledCells++;
                    }
                    else if (seenBlock)
                    {
                        columnHoles++;
                    }
                }

                heights[x] = h;
                holes += columnHoles;
                aggregateHeight += h;
                if (h > maxHeight)
                {
                    maxHeight = h;
                }
            }

            int bumpiness = 0;
            for (int x = 0; x + 1 < _width; x++)
            {
                bumpiness += Math.Abs(heights[x] - heights[x + 1]);
            }

            int rowTransitions = 0;
            for (int y = 0; y < _height; y++)
            {
                ulong row = board[y];
                bool prevFilled = true;
                for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
                {
                    bool filled = ((row >> x) & 1UL) != 0;
                    if (filled != prevFilled)
                    {
                        rowTransitions++;
                    }

                    prevFilled = filled;
                }

                if (!prevFilled)
                {
                    rowTransitions++;
                }
            }

            int columnTransitions = 0;
            for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
            {
                bool prevFilled = true;
                for (int y = _height - 1; y >= 0; y--)
                {
                    bool filled = ((board[y] >> x) & 1UL) != 0;
                    if (filled != prevFilled)
                    {
                        columnTransitions++;
                    }

                    prevFilled = filled;
                }

                if (!prevFilled)
                {
                    columnTransitions++;
                }
            }

            int wellSum = 0;
            for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
            {
                int left = x == 0 ? _height : heights[x - 1];
                int right = x == _width - 1 ? _height : heights[x + 1];
                int minNeighbor = left < right ? left : right;
                if (minNeighbor > heights[x])
                {
                    int depth = minNeighbor - heights[x];
                    wellSum += depth * (depth + 1) / 2;
                }
            }

            return new BoardStats(
                aggregateHeight,
                maxHeight,
                holes,
                bumpiness,
                rowTransitions,
                columnTransitions,
                wellSum,
                filledCells);
        }

        private bool TrySimulateMove(
            ulong[] source,
            Rotation rotation,
            int column,
            ulong[] destination,
            out int landingY,
            out int clearedLines,
            out int erodedCells)
        {
            landingY = 0;
            clearedLines = 0;
            erodedCells = 0;

            if (column < 0 || column + rotation.Width > _width)
            {
                return false;
            }

            int y = _height;
            while (y > 0 && !Collides(source, rotation, column, y - 1))
            {
                y--;
            }

            if (y + rotation.Height > _height)
            {
                return false;
            }

            landingY = y;

            Array.Copy(source, destination, _height);
            for (int row = 0; row < rotation.Height; row++)
            {
                destination[landingY + row] |= rotation.RowMasks[row] << column;
            }

            for (int row = 0; row < rotation.Height; row++)
            {
                int boardRow = landingY + row;
                if ((destination[boardRow] & _fullMask) == _fullMask)
                {
                    clearedLines++;
                    erodedCells += rotation.RowCellCounts[row];
                }
            }

            if (clearedLines > 0)
            {
                int write = 0;
                for (int read = 0; read < _height; read++)
                {
                    ulong rowMask = destination[read];
                    if ((rowMask & _fullMask) != _fullMask)
                    {
                        destination[write++] = rowMask;
                    }
                }

                while (write < _height)
                {
                    destination[write++] = 0UL;
                }
            }

            return true;
        }

        private bool Collides(ulong[] board, Rotation rotation, int column, int y)
        {
            for (int row = 0; row < rotation.Height; row++)
            {
                int boardY = y + row;
                if (boardY < 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                if (boardY >= _height)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                ulong mask = rotation.RowMasks[row] << column;
                if ((board[boardY] & mask) != 0)
                {
                    return true;
                }
            }

            return false;
        }

        private void CopyBoard(ulong[] source, ulong[] destination)
        {
            Array.Copy(source, destination, _height);
        }

        private static PieceDefinition[] BuildPieces()
        {
            return new[]
            {
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(3, 0)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(0, 1), new Cell(1, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(1, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(1, 1), new Cell(2, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(0, 1), new Cell(1, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(0, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(2, 1)
                }))
            };
        }

        private static Rotation[] BuildRotations(Cell[] baseCells)
        {
            var rotations = new Rotation[4];
            Cell[] current = baseCells;

            for (int r = 0; r < 4; r++)
            {
                int minX = int.MaxValue;
                int minY = int.MaxValue;
                int maxX = int.MinValue;
                int maxY = int.MinValue;

                for (int i = 0; i < current.Length; i++)
                {
                    Cell cell = current[i];
                    if (cell.X < minX) minX = cell.X;
                    if (cell.Y < minY) minY = cell.Y;
                    if (cell.X > maxX) maxX = cell.X;
                    if (cell.Y > maxY) maxY = cell.Y;
                }

                int width = maxX - minX + 1;
                int height = maxY - minY + 1;
                ulong[] rowMasks = new ulong[height];
                int[] rowCellCounts = new int[height];
                double sumY = 0.0;

                for (int i = 0; i < current.Length; i++)
                {
                    int x = current[i].X - minX;
                    int y = current[i].Y - minY;
                    rowMasks[y] |= 1UL << x;
                    rowCellCounts[y]++;
                    sumY += y;
                }

                rotations[r] = new Rotation(width, height, rowMasks, rowCellCounts, sumY / current.Length);
                current = RotateClockwise(current);
            }

            return rotations;
        }

        private static Cell[] RotateClockwise(Cell[] cells)
        {
            int maxX = 0;
            int maxY = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < cells.Length; i++)
            {
                if (cells[i].X > maxX) maxX = cells[i].X;
                if (cells[i].Y > maxY) maxY = cells[i].Y;
            }

            int width = maxX + 1;
            var rotated = new Cell[cells.Length];
            for (int i = 0; i < cells.Length; i++)
            {
                rotated[i] = new Cell(cells[i].Y, width - 1 - cells[i].X);
            }

            return rotated;
        }
    }
}
