Tetris AI - 5000 pieces

Approach: nullable enable

View C# Code (26010 chars)
using System;
using System.Globalization;
using System.IO;
using System.Text;

public static class Program
{
    public static void Main()
    {
        var input = new FastScanner(Console.OpenStandardInput());
        var output = new StreamWriter(Console.OpenStandardOutput())
        {
            AutoFlush = true,
            NewLine = "\n"
        };

        int width = input.NextInt();
        if (width == int.MinValue)
        {
            return;
        }

        int height = input.NextInt();
        int numPieces = input.NextInt();

        if (width <= 0 || height <= 0 || numPieces < 0)
        {
            return;
        }

        var ai = new TetrisAi(width, height);

        for (int i = 0; i < numPieces; i++)
        {
            string? pieceToken = input.NextToken();
            if (pieceToken == null)
            {
                break;
            }

            PieceKind piece = ParsePiece(pieceToken[0]);
            Move move = ai.ChooseMove(piece);

            output.Write(move.Rotation);
            output.Write(' ');
            output.Write(move.Column);
            output.WriteLine();
            output.Flush();

            string? result = input.NextToken();
            if (result == null || result == "gameover")
            {
                break;
            }

            ai.CommitChosenBoard();
        }
    }

    private static PieceKind ParsePiece(char c)
    {
        return char.ToUpperInvariant(c) switch
        {
            'I' => PieceKind.I,
            'O' => PieceKind.O,
            'T' => PieceKind.T,
            'S' => PieceKind.S,
            'Z' => PieceKind.Z,
            'J' => PieceKind.J,
            'L' => PieceKind.L,
            _ => PieceKind.O
        };
    }

    private enum PieceKind
    {
        I = 0,
        O = 1,
        T = 2,
        S = 3,
        Z = 4,
        J = 5,
        L = 6
    }

    private readonly struct Move
    {
        public readonly int Rotation;
        public readonly int Column;

        public Move(int rotation, int column)
        {
            Rotation = rotation;
            Column = column;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct Cell
    {
        public readonly int X;
        public readonly int Y;

        public Cell(int x, int y)
        {
            X = x;
            Y = y;
        }
    }

    private sealed class Rotation
    {
        public readonly int Width;
        public readonly int Height;
        public readonly ulong[] RowMasks;
        public readonly int[] RowCellCounts;
        public readonly double AverageCellY;

        public Rotation(int width, int height, ulong[] rowMasks, int[] rowCellCounts, double averageCellY)
        {
            Width = width;
            Height = height;
            RowMasks = rowMasks;
            RowCellCounts = rowCellCounts;
            AverageCellY = averageCellY;
        }
    }

    private sealed class PieceDefinition
    {
        public readonly Rotation[] Rotations;

        public PieceDefinition(Rotation[] rotations)
        {
            Rotations = rotations;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct BoardStats
    {
        public readonly int AggregateHeight;
        public readonly int MaxHeight;
        public readonly int Holes;
        public readonly int Bumpiness;
        public readonly int RowTransitions;
        public readonly int ColumnTransitions;
        public readonly int WellSum;
        public readonly int FilledCells;

        public BoardStats(
            int aggregateHeight,
            int maxHeight,
            int holes,
            int bumpiness,
            int rowTransitions,
            int columnTransitions,
            int wellSum,
            int filledCells)
        {
            AggregateHeight = aggregateHeight;
            MaxHeight = maxHeight;
            Holes = holes;
            Bumpiness = bumpiness;
            RowTransitions = rowTransitions;
            ColumnTransitions = columnTransitions;
            WellSum = wellSum;
            FilledCells = filledCells;
        }
    }

    private readonly struct StrategyProfile
    {
        public readonly double FutureWeight;
        public readonly double RiskBlend;
        public readonly double LineClearWeight;
        public readonly double ErodedWeight;
        public readonly double LandingWeight;
        public readonly double AggregateHeightWeight;
        public readonly double HolesWeight;
        public readonly double BumpinessWeight;
        public readonly double RowTransitionsWeight;
        public readonly double ColumnTransitionsWeight;
        public readonly double WellsWeight;
        public readonly double MaxHeightWeight;

        public StrategyProfile(
            double futureWeight,
            double riskBlend,
            double lineClearWeight,
            double erodedWeight,
            double landingWeight,
            double aggregateHeightWeight,
            double holesWeight,
            double bumpinessWeight,
            double rowTransitionsWeight,
            double columnTransitionsWeight,
            double wellsWeight,
            double maxHeightWeight)
        {
            FutureWeight = futureWeight;
            RiskBlend = riskBlend;
            LineClearWeight = lineClearWeight;
            ErodedWeight = erodedWeight;
            LandingWeight = landingWeight;
            AggregateHeightWeight = aggregateHeightWeight;
            HolesWeight = holesWeight;
            BumpinessWeight = bumpinessWeight;
            RowTransitionsWeight = rowTransitionsWeight;
            ColumnTransitionsWeight = columnTransitionsWeight;
            WellsWeight = wellsWeight;
            MaxHeightWeight = maxHeightWeight;
        }
    }

    private sealed class FastScanner
    {
        private readonly Stream _stream;
        private readonly byte[] _buffer = new byte[1 << 16];
        private int _length;
        private int _position;

        public FastScanner(Stream stream)
        {
            _stream = stream;
        }

        private int ReadByte()
        {
            if (_position >= _length)
            {
                _length = _stream.Read(_buffer, 0, _buffer.Length);
                _position = 0;
                if (_length <= 0)
                {
                    return -1;
                }
            }

            return _buffer[_position++];
        }

        public string? NextToken()
        {
            int c;
            do
            {
                c = ReadByte();
                if (c == -1)
                {
                    return null;
                }
            }
            while (c <= 32);

            var sb = new StringBuilder(16);
            do
            {
                sb.Append((char)c);
                c = ReadByte();
            }
            while (c != -1 && c > 32);

            return sb.ToString();
        }

        public int NextInt()
        {
            string? token = NextToken();
            return token == null ? int.MinValue : int.Parse(token, CultureInfo.InvariantCulture);
        }
    }

    private sealed class TetrisAi
    {
        private const double DeadScore = -1_000_000_000.0;

        private static readonly PieceDefinition[] Pieces = BuildPieces();

        private readonly int _width;
        private readonly int _height;
        private readonly ulong _fullMask;
        private readonly ulong[] _currentBoard;
        private ulong[] _pendingBoard;
        private readonly ulong[] _scratchA;
        private readonly ulong[] _scratchB;

        private static readonly StrategyProfile Aggressive = new StrategyProfile(
            futureWeight: 0.0,
            riskBlend: 0.05,
            lineClearWeight: 8.0,
            erodedWeight: 1.3,
            landingWeight: -0.16,
            aggregateHeightWeight: -0.55,
            holesWeight: -9.0,
            bumpinessWeight: -0.80,
            rowTransitionsWeight: -0.20,
            columnTransitionsWeight: -0.32,
            wellsWeight: -0.60,
            maxHeightWeight: -5.5);

        private static readonly StrategyProfile Balanced = new StrategyProfile(
            futureWeight: 0.0,
            riskBlend: 0.18,
            lineClearWeight: 10.0,
            erodedWeight: 1.6,
            landingWeight: -0.20,
            aggregateHeightWeight: -0.70,
            holesWeight: -12.0,
            bumpinessWeight: -1.00,
            rowTransitionsWeight: -0.25,
            columnTransitionsWeight: -0.40,
            wellsWeight: -0.80,
            maxHeightWeight: -7.0);

        private static readonly StrategyProfile Survival = new StrategyProfile(
            futureWeight: 0.0,
            riskBlend: 0.40,
            lineClearWeight: 11.0,
            erodedWeight: 1.1,
            landingWeight: -0.24,
            aggregateHeightWeight: -0.85,
            holesWeight: -16.0,
            bumpinessWeight: -1.20,
            rowTransitionsWeight: -0.30,
            columnTransitionsWeight: -0.48,
            wellsWeight: -1.00,
            maxHeightWeight: -9.0);

        public TetrisAi(int width, int height)
        {
            _width = width;
            _height = height;
            _fullMask = width >= 64 ? ulong.MaxValue : ((1UL << width) - 1UL);
            _currentBoard = new ulong[height];
            _pendingBoard = new ulong[height];
            _scratchA = new ulong[height];
            _scratchB = new ulong[height];
        }

        public Move ChooseMove(PieceKind pieceKind)
        {
            BoardStats currentStats = AnalyzeBoard(_currentBoard);
            StrategyProfile strategy = ChooseStrategy(currentStats);

            double bestScore = DeadScore;
            int bestRotation = 0;
            int bestColumn = 0;
            int bestCleared = -1;
            int bestLandingY = int.MaxValue;
            bool found = false;

            Rotation[] rotations = Pieces[(int)pieceKind].Rotations;

            for (int rotationIndex = 0; rotationIndex < rotations.Length; rotationIndex++)
            {
                Rotation rotation = rotations[rotationIndex];
                int maxColumn = _width - rotation.Width;
                if (maxColumn < 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                for (int column = 0; column <= maxColumn; column++)
                {
                    if (!TrySimulateMove(_currentBoard, rotation, column, _scratchA, out int landingY, out int clearedLines, out int erodedCells))
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    double score = ScoreImmediate(_scratchA, rotation, landingY, clearedLines, erodedCells, strategy);
                    if (strategy.FutureWeight != 0.0)
                    {
                        score += strategy.FutureWeight * EvaluateFutureScore(_scratchA, strategy, _scratchB);
                    }

                    if (!found ||
                        score > bestScore + 1e-9 ||
                        (Math.Abs(score - bestScore) <= 1e-9 &&
                         (clearedLines > bestCleared ||
                          (clearedLines == bestCleared && landingY < bestLandingY) ||
                          (clearedLines == bestCleared && landingY == bestLandingY && column < bestColumn))))
                    {
                        bestScore = score;
                        bestRotation = rotationIndex;
                        bestColumn = column;
                        bestCleared = clearedLines;
                        bestLandingY = landingY;
                        CopyBoard(_scratchA, _pendingBoard);
                        found = true;
                    }
                }
            }

            if (!found)
            {
                CopyBoard(_currentBoard, _pendingBoard);
                return new Move(0, 0);
            }

            return new Move(bestRotation, bestColumn);
        }

        public void CommitChosenBoard()
        {
            Array.Copy(_pendingBoard, _currentBoard, _height);
        }

        private StrategyProfile ChooseStrategy(BoardStats stats)
        {
            double area = (double)_width * _height;
            double maxHeightRatio = _height > 0 ? stats.MaxHeight / (double)_height : 0.0;
            double holeRatio = area > 0 ? stats.Holes / area : 0.0;
            double roughRatio = _height > 0 && _width > 1
                ? stats.Bumpiness / ((double)(_width - 1) * _height)
                : 0.0;
            double fillRatio = area > 0 ? stats.FilledCells / area : 0.0;

            double danger = maxHeightRatio * 0.60 + holeRatio * 20.0 + roughRatio * 2.0 + fillRatio * 0.15;

            if (stats.MaxHeight >= _height - 3 || holeRatio > 0.12 || stats.Holes >= _width * 2 || danger >= 0.85)
            {
                return Survival;
            }

            if (danger >= 0.35)
            {
                return Balanced;
            }

            return Aggressive;
        }

        private double EvaluateFutureScore(ulong[] board, StrategyProfile strategy, ulong[] work)
        {
            double sum = 0.0;
            double min = double.PositiveInfinity;

            for (int pieceIndex = 0; pieceIndex < Pieces.Length; pieceIndex++)
            {
                double score = EvaluateBestImmediate(board, (PieceKind)pieceIndex, strategy, work);
                sum += score;
                if (score < min)
                {
                    min = score;
                }
            }

            double avg = sum / Pieces.Length;
            return avg * (1.0 - strategy.RiskBlend) + min * strategy.RiskBlend;
        }

        private double EvaluateBestImmediate(ulong[] board, PieceKind pieceKind, StrategyProfile strategy, ulong[] work)
        {
            double best = DeadScore;
            Rotation[] rotations = Pieces[(int)pieceKind].Rotations;

            for (int rotationIndex = 0; rotationIndex < rotations.Length; rotationIndex++)
            {
                Rotation rotation = rotations[rotationIndex];
                int maxColumn = _width - rotation.Width;
                if (maxColumn < 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                for (int column = 0; column <= maxColumn; column++)
                {
                    if (!TrySimulateMove(board, rotation, column, work, out int landingY, out int clearedLines, out int erodedCells))
                    {
                        continue;
                    }

                    double score = ScoreImmediate(work, rotation, landingY, clearedLines, erodedCells, strategy);
                    if (score > best)
                    {
                        best = score;
                    }
                }
            }

            return best;
        }

        private double ScoreImmediate(
            ulong[] boardAfterMove,
            Rotation rotation,
            int landingY,
            int clearedLines,
            int erodedCells,
            StrategyProfile strategy)
        {
            BoardStats stats = AnalyzeBoard(boardAfterMove);

            double avgHeight = stats.AggregateHeight / (double)_width;
            double avgHoles = stats.Holes / (double)_width;
            double avgBumpiness = _width > 1 ? stats.Bumpiness / (double)(_width - 1) : 0.0;
            double avgRowTransitions = stats.RowTransitions / (double)Math.Max(1, _height);
            double avgColumnTransitions = stats.ColumnTransitions / (double)Math.Max(1, _width);
            double avgWells = stats.WellSum / (double)_width;
            double maxRatio = _height > 0 ? stats.MaxHeight / (double)_height : 0.0;
            double landingRatio = _height > 1 ? (landingY + rotation.AverageCellY) / (double)(_height - 1) : 0.0;

            double score =
                strategy.LineClearWeight * clearedLines +
                strategy.ErodedWeight * (erodedCells * clearedLines) +
                strategy.LandingWeight * landingRatio +
                strategy.AggregateHeightWeight * avgHeight +
                strategy.HolesWeight * avgHoles +
                strategy.BumpinessWeight * avgBumpiness +
                strategy.RowTransitionsWeight * avgRowTransitions +
                strategy.ColumnTransitionsWeight * avgColumnTransitions +
                strategy.WellsWeight * avgWells +
                strategy.MaxHeightWeight * maxRatio;

            return score;
        }

        private BoardStats AnalyzeBoard(ulong[] board)
        {
            Span<int> heights = stackalloc int[64];
            int aggregateHeight = 0;
            int maxHeight = 0;
            int holes = 0;
            int filledCells = 0;

            for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
            {
                bool seenBlock = false;
                int h = 0;
                int columnHoles = 0;

                for (int y = _height - 1; y >= 0; y--)
                {
                    bool filled = ((board[y] >> x) & 1UL) != 0;
                    if (filled)
                    {
                        seenBlock = true;
                        if (h == 0)
                        {
                            h = y + 1;
                        }

                        filledCells++;
                    }
                    else if (seenBlock)
                    {
                        columnHoles++;
                    }
                }

                heights[x] = h;
                holes += columnHoles;
                aggregateHeight += h;
                if (h > maxHeight)
                {
                    maxHeight = h;
                }
            }

            int bumpiness = 0;
            for (int x = 0; x + 1 < _width; x++)
            {
                bumpiness += Math.Abs(heights[x] - heights[x + 1]);
            }

            int rowTransitions = 0;
            for (int y = 0; y < _height; y++)
            {
                ulong row = board[y];
                bool prevFilled = true;
                for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
                {
                    bool filled = ((row >> x) & 1UL) != 0;
                    if (filled != prevFilled)
                    {
                        rowTransitions++;
                    }

                    prevFilled = filled;
                }

                if (!prevFilled)
                {
                    rowTransitions++;
                }
            }

            int columnTransitions = 0;
            for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
            {
                bool prevFilled = true;
                for (int y = _height - 1; y >= 0; y--)
                {
                    bool filled = ((board[y] >> x) & 1UL) != 0;
                    if (filled != prevFilled)
                    {
                        columnTransitions++;
                    }

                    prevFilled = filled;
                }

                if (!prevFilled)
                {
                    columnTransitions++;
                }
            }

            int wellSum = 0;
            for (int x = 0; x < _width; x++)
            {
                int left = x == 0 ? _height : heights[x - 1];
                int right = x == _width - 1 ? _height : heights[x + 1];
                int minNeighbor = left < right ? left : right;
                if (minNeighbor > heights[x])
                {
                    int depth = minNeighbor - heights[x];
                    wellSum += depth * (depth + 1) / 2;
                }
            }

            return new BoardStats(
                aggregateHeight,
                maxHeight,
                holes,
                bumpiness,
                rowTransitions,
                columnTransitions,
                wellSum,
                filledCells);
        }

        private bool TrySimulateMove(
            ulong[] source,
            Rotation rotation,
            int column,
            ulong[] destination,
            out int landingY,
            out int clearedLines,
            out int erodedCells)
        {
            landingY = 0;
            clearedLines = 0;
            erodedCells = 0;

            if (column < 0 || column + rotation.Width > _width)
            {
                return false;
            }

            int y = _height;
            while (y > 0 && !Collides(source, rotation, column, y - 1))
            {
                y--;
            }

            if (y + rotation.Height > _height)
            {
                return false;
            }

            landingY = y;

            Array.Copy(source, destination, _height);
            for (int row = 0; row < rotation.Height; row++)
            {
                destination[landingY + row] |= rotation.RowMasks[row] << column;
            }

            for (int row = 0; row < rotation.Height; row++)
            {
                int boardRow = landingY + row;
                if ((destination[boardRow] & _fullMask) == _fullMask)
                {
                    clearedLines++;
                    erodedCells += rotation.RowCellCounts[row];
                }
            }

            if (clearedLines > 0)
            {
                int write = 0;
                for (int read = 0; read < _height; read++)
                {
                    ulong rowMask = destination[read];
                    if ((rowMask & _fullMask) != _fullMask)
                    {
                        destination[write++] = rowMask;
                    }
                }

                while (write < _height)
                {
                    destination[write++] = 0UL;
                }
            }

            return true;
        }

        private bool Collides(ulong[] board, Rotation rotation, int column, int y)
        {
            for (int row = 0; row < rotation.Height; row++)
            {
                int boardY = y + row;
                if (boardY < 0)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                if (boardY >= _height)
                {
                    continue;
                }

                ulong mask = rotation.RowMasks[row] << column;
                if ((board[boardY] & mask) != 0)
                {
                    return true;
                }
            }

            return false;
        }

        private void CopyBoard(ulong[] source, ulong[] destination)
        {
            Array.Copy(source, destination, _height);
        }

        private static PieceDefinition[] BuildPieces()
        {
            return new[]
            {
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(3, 0)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(0, 1), new Cell(1, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(1, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(1, 1), new Cell(2, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(0, 1), new Cell(1, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(0, 1)
                })),
                new PieceDefinition(BuildRotations(new[]
                {
                    new Cell(0, 0), new Cell(1, 0), new Cell(2, 0), new Cell(2, 1)
                }))
            };
        }

        private static Rotation[] BuildRotations(Cell[] baseCells)
        {
            var rotations = new Rotation[4];
            Cell[] current = baseCells;

            for (int r = 0; r < 4; r++)
            {
                int minX = int.MaxValue;
                int minY = int.MaxValue;
                int maxX = int.MinValue;
                int maxY = int.MinValue;

                for (int i = 0; i < current.Length; i++)
                {
                    Cell cell = current[i];
                    if (cell.X < minX) minX = cell.X;
                    if (cell.Y < minY) minY = cell.Y;
                    if (cell.X > maxX) maxX = cell.X;
                    if (cell.Y > maxY) maxY = cell.Y;
                }

                int width = maxX - minX + 1;
                int height = maxY - minY + 1;
                ulong[] rowMasks = new ulong[height];
                int[] rowCellCounts = new int[height];
                double sumY = 0.0;

                for (int i = 0; i < current.Length; i++)
                {
                    int x = current[i].X - minX;
                    int y = current[i].Y - minY;
                    rowMasks[y] |= 1UL << x;
                    rowCellCounts[y]++;
                    sumY += y;
                }

                rotations[r] = new Rotation(width, height, rowMasks, rowCellCounts, sumY / current.Length);
                current = RotateClockwise(current);
            }

            return rotations;
        }

        private static Cell[] RotateClockwise(Cell[] cells)
        {
            int maxX = 0;
            int maxY = 0;
            for (int i = 0; i < cells.Length; i++)
            {
                if (cells[i].X > maxX) maxX = cells[i].X;
                if (cells[i].Y > maxY) maxY = cells[i].Y;
            }

            int width = maxX + 1;
            var rotated = new Cell[cells.Length];
            for (int i = 0; i < cells.Length; i++)
            {
                rotated[i] = new Cell(cells[i].Y, width - 1 - cells[i].X);
            }

            return rotated;
        }
    }
}

Test 23: Lunar Lander Game Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to play Lunar Lander in real-time.

Your Rust lunar lander controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from simple
training environments to very complex landing sites.

Control a lunar lander to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a 2D voxel map and continuous state updates, and must output thrust/turn
commands to land safely on the target.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time. If your code
doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the io buffer fills up, the ship will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes that
async code doesn't have. Multithreaded / asyncio code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Physics:**
- Gravity pulls lander down constantly
- Thrust applies force in lander's facing direction
- Fuel is limited
- Must land softly on target (low velocity)

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal numbers):**
Initial map:
```
width height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y target_x target_y
map rows (. = empty, # = obstacle, S = start, T = target, L = landing zone)
```

Then you recieve continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds):
```
x y vx vy angle angular_vel fuel_remaining
```
x and y are in meters, from the worlds top left, and y increases downward.
vx and vy are in m/s.
angle is in radians, 0 is facing up.
angular_vel is in radians per second. + is clockwise, - is counterclockwise.
fuel_remaining is seconds at max_thrust.

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following instructions at a frequency of at least 20hz:
```
thrust turn
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max thrust)
- turn: -1.0 to 1.0 (turning rate, negative = left, positive = right)

To be crystal clear: that's two decimal numbers seperated by a space, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (so it misses 2 consecutive updates), the engine
will automatically cut power. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

Map size can vary from 100x50 to multi-gb maps, so be efficient with memory and processing.

Thrust and gravity are in m/s/s. 100% engine burn burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

Lander size is approximatly 1x1m, and the 2D map grid size is 1m x 1m.

Turn is in radians per second. The lander can not rotate fully around and will clamp rotation to
facing down if attempted.

Y increases downward. Positive vy means moving downward. Gravity is +g.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 2m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries
- Complete landing within time limit

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into obstacle or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function. Assume only the standard library is available.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Subpasses vary the starting altitude, velocity, and terrain. The AI must handle 2D physics, fuel management, and real-time control to achieve a safe landing.

Test 24: 3D Lunar Lander Game (C++) Score: 0.00 / 10
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to play a 3D Lunar Lander game in real-time.

Your C++ controller will be tested with scenarios ranging from tiny (100m) to huge (100km).
The same code must work for all scales.

Control a lunar lander in full 3D space to safely land on a target pad.
The program receives a terrain file path and continuous state updates via stdin, and must
output thrust/rotation commands to stdout in real-time.

Data is streamed to stdin in real time, and you must write to stdout in real time.
If your code doesn't read from stdin fast enough and the IO buffer fills up, the ship
will likely crash.

Be very careful if writing single threaded / blocking code, this has many failure modes
that async code doesn't have. Multithreaded code is HIGHLY recommended.

**Coordinate System:**
- X: horizontal (East), Y: horizontal (North), Z: UP.
- Gravity acts in the -Z direction.
- All positions are in meters. Velocities in m/s. Angles in radians.

**Physics:**
- Gravity = 1.62 m/s² (lunar) in -Z direction.
- Thrust is along the lander's local "up" axis. When pitch=0 and yaw=0, thrust is
straight up (+Z). Tilting via pitch/yaw redirects thrust.
- Pitch: tilts the lander. +pitch tilts forward, redirecting thrust partially into -X.
Pitch is clamped to [-pi/2, pi/2].
- Yaw: rotates heading around Z.
- Roll: rolls around the thrust axis (cosmetic only, does not affect thrust direction).
- Rotation dynamics: commands apply angular acceleration (2 rad/s² per unit).
Angular rates are damped by 0.95 per tick.
- Fuel: 100% thrust burns 1 unit of fuel per second.

**Input format (stdin):**
Initial header (whitespace separated decimal numbers, except terrain path):
```
width depth height gravity max_thrust fuel
start_x start_y start_z target_x target_y target_z
terrain_file_path
STATE
```

The terrain_file_path is on its own line (the path may contain spaces).
Read the entire line with getline() or equivalent.

**Terrain file format (binary):**
The terrain file defines the 3D world as a binary file. It is a sequence of
uint16_t triplets (little-endian), one per (x, y) cell, stored in row-major order:

```cpp
for (int x = 0; x < width; x++) {
for (int y = 0; y < depth; y++) {
uint16_t a = read_uint16_le(); // ground rock top
uint16_t b = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock bottom
uint16_t c = read_uint16_le(); // floating rock top
}
}
```

For each column (x, y), the vertical structure is:
- z < a: SOLID ROCK (ground)
- a <= z < b: OPEN AIR
- b <= z < c: SOLID ROCK (floating terrain feature, overhang, arch, etc.)
- c <= z: OPEN AIR

If b == c, there is no floating rock in that column (just ground + open sky).
The file size is exactly width * depth * 6 bytes.

You have a startup grace period to parse this file before state updates begin.
The grace period is 5 seconds plus 10 seconds per gigabyte of terrain file.
For a 100x100 map this is ~5s. For a 100000x10000 map (~6GB) this is ~65s.
You may continue parsing in a background thread after the simulation begins,
but you MUST be reading stdin and writing stdout by then to avoid a crash.

Then continuous state updates (every 0.1 seconds, whitespace separated):
```
x y z vx vy vz pitch yaw roll pitch_rate yaw_rate roll_rate fuel_remaining
```

**Output format (stdout):**
Your code needs to output the following at a frequency of at least 10hz:
```
thrust pitch_cmd yaw_cmd roll_cmd
```
- thrust: 0.0 to 1.0 (fraction of max_thrust)
- pitch_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (pitch angular acceleration command)
- yaw_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (yaw angular acceleration command)
- roll_cmd: -1.0 to 1.0 (roll angular acceleration command)

To be crystal clear: four decimal numbers separated by spaces, followed by a newline.

If your code stalls / freezes for 100ms or more (misses 2 consecutive updates), the
engine will cut thrust automatically. A long freeze and eventually the lander will crash.

**Landing:**
- The target is at (target_x, target_y, target_z) which is just above the terrain.
- You must land within a small radius of the target with total speed < 3 m/s.
- Landing zone radius is approximately max(5, min(width,depth)*0.02) meters.

**Terrain collisions:**
- You collide if you enter any solid cell as defined by the terrain file.
- Out of bounds (x<0, x>=width, y<0, y>=depth, z<0, z>=height) is also a collision.

**Success Conditions:**
- Land on target with |velocity| < 3 m/s
- Stay within landing zone boundaries

**Failure Conditions:**
- Crash into terrain or ground too hard
- Run out of fuel
- Exceed simulation time limit

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)

Land a spacecraft on a 3D lunar surface with full attitude control. Unlike the 2D version, the lander can pitch, yaw, and roll, and the terrain is a detailed 3D heightmap with floating rock obstacles. The AI must parse a binary terrain file and navigate to a safe touchdown.

Subpasses vary the terrain complexity, starting conditions, and obstacle density. The AI must handle 6-degree-of-freedom physics and real-time control in three dimensions.

Test 25: Asteroid Interception (C#) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to calculate an asteroid interception trajectory.

You must write a C# solver that can handle an interception and redirection scenarios, from
short warning times (1-2 years), large impactors, generous delta-v, simple trajectories to
very long warning times (30-50 years), very small impactors, very tight delta-v,
advanced trajectory optimization with multi-flyby maneuvers and gravitational assist sequences required.

**Problem:**
Calculate a spacecraft trajectory to intercept and deflect an asteroid on collision course
with Earth using kinetic impact.

**The solver must output:**
1. Launch window and initial trajectory
2. Mid-course correction burn(s)
3. Final approach for kinetic impact

**Input format (stdin, whitespace seperated decimal/scientific notation numbers):**
```
num_bodies warning_days impactor_mass delta_v_budget asteroid_mass t0_unix_utc
body_name mass x y z vx vy vz (for each body)
de421.bsp_file_path
```

All values are in SI units unless otherwise specified. Eg mass of earth is 5.972e24 kg.
All positions + velocities are relative to solar system barycenter.
Delta_v budget is in m/s.

The body name for the asteriod is "Asteroid". The body name for where your spacecraft originates
and you must redirect the asteroid to avoid is "Earth".

**Output format (stdout):**
```
num_burns
launch_time burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
correction_time1 burn_dv_x burn_dv_y burn_dv_z
...
impact_time impact_dv_x impact_dv_y impact_dv_z
```

**Time units:**
- All times are in **days since scenario start (t=0 at the provided initial state)**.
- The **launch window is [0, warning_days]** (inclusive).
- **t0_unix_utc** is the UTC time of the provided initial state (t=0).

The simulation uses the JPL DE421 model of the solar system with Sun, Earth, Moon, and major planets,
and a KDK integrator to calculate trajectories. The path to the DE421 model is provided in the input
so your code can precisely match the simulation at extreme precision.

**Success Criteria:**
- Spacecraft intercepts asteroid before Earth impact (within 1000km)
- Kinetic impact transfers enough momentum to deflect asteroid sufficiently to miss Earth
- Total delta-v usage stays within budget
- Launch occurs within specified window

**Simulation host environment**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler**

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with C# .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable warning times and delta-v budgets
4. Complete analysis within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying mission complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C# code with a static void Main method.

Test 26: 3D Point Clustering (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 11
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to cluster 3D points into exactly N clusters.

You must write a Rust solver that can handle ANY clustering scenario from trivial to ludicrous scale:
- **Trivial**: Small datasets (1M points, 10 clusters), basic k-means, memory-friendly
- **Medium**: Moderate datasets (10M points, 100 clusters), optimized k-means, streaming
- **Large**: Large datasets (100M points, 1000 clusters), mini-batch k-means, memory constraints
- **Extreme**: Massive datasets (1B-10B points, 10000 clusters), streaming algorithms, very tight memory

**The Challenge:**
Your Rust clustering algorithm will be tested with datasets ranging from millions to billions of 3D points. The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL scales while respecting memory constraints.

**Problem:**
Given millions to billions of 3D points, partition them into exactly N clusters minimizing within-cluster variance (k-means objective). Points are provided as binary float32 triples for efficiency.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_points num_clusters
[x y z] (as binary float32 triples, num_points total)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
[cluster_index] (as binary uint32, one per point)
```

**Critical Requirements:**
1. **Scalability**: Your algorithm must adapt based on point count and cluster count
2. **Performance**: Must complete within 5 minutes even for massive datasets
3. **Memory**: Must handle streaming input for datasets that don't fit in memory
4. **Quality**: Minimize within-cluster variance while producing exactly N clusters

**Algorithm Strategy Recommendations:**
- **Small datasets (≤10M points)**: Can use standard k-means with full data in memory
- **Medium datasets (10M-100M points)**: Mini-batch k-means, streaming updates
- **Large datasets (100M-1B points)**: Streaming k-means with limited memory buffers
- **Very Large datasets (>1B points)**: Very fast streaming, approximate clustering

**Key Techniques:**
- **Streaming algorithms**: Process points in batches without storing all data
- **Mini-batch k-means**: Update centroids incrementally
- **Memory management**: Use fixed-size buffers for large datasets
- **Binary I/O**: Efficient reading/writing of binary data
- **Convergence detection**: Monitor when clustering stabilizes

**Implementation Hints:**
- Detect dataset size and choose appropriate clustering algorithm
- Use efficient binary I/O (std::io::BufReader/BufWriter)
- Implement streaming updates for large datasets
- For very large datasets, use approximation algorithms
- Handle edge cases: empty clusters, convergence issues
- Use fast random access for centroid updates

**Binary Format Details:**
- Input: Points as little-endian f32 triples (12 bytes per point)
- Output: Cluster indices as little-endian u32 (4 bytes per point)
- Total input size: num_points × 12 bytes
- Total output size: num_points × 4 bytes

**Memory Constraints:**
- Small datasets: Can load all points into memory
- Medium datasets: Use streaming with moderate buffers
- Large datasets: Streaming with minimal memory footprint
- Very Large datasets: Extremely memory-efficient streaming

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with rustc (edition 2021)
2. Read binary input from stdin, write binary output to stdout
3. Handle variable point counts and cluster numbers
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying dataset sizes efficiently
6. Produce exactly N clusters

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on clustering complexity.

The AI must decide how many clusters exist and assign every point to one. Subpasses increase the number of points and make the clusters harder to separate. Scoring is based on how well the AI’s clusters match the true groupings.

Test 27: Graph Coloring (C++) Score: 0.00 / 15
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C++ code to solve the Graph Coloring problem.

**The Challenge:**
Your C++ graph colorer will be tested with graphs ranging from simple 50 nodes
to massive million-node graphs requiring 60 colours.

**Problem:**
Color a graph with exactly k colours such that no two adjacent vertices share the same colour.
This is NP-Complete and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges num_colors
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
color_0 color_1 ... color_(n-1) (color assignment for each vertex)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices assigned valid colors (0 to k-1)
- No adjacent vertices share the same color
- All vertices are colored (complete coloring)

**Failure Criteria:**
- Cannot find valid coloring within time limit
- Invalid color assignment (adjacent vertices same color)
- Incomplete coloring

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C++ Compiler:**
MSVC cl 19.50.35728 | std=c++20 (/std:c++20)

Be sure that any deviation from the C++ standard library is supported by the given compiler,
as referencing the wrong intrinsics or non-standard header like 'bits/stdc++.h' could fail your submission.

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with g++ or MSVC (C++17)
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and color counts
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Write complete, compilable C++ code with a main() function.
Include adaptive logic that chooses different strategies based on graph complexity.

View chain of thought

Colour the nodes of a graph using as few colours as possible, with the rule that no two connected nodes may share the same colour. This is the same idea as colouring a map so that no neighbouring countries are the same colour.

Finding the minimum number of colours is NP-hard. Subpasses increase the graph size and connectivity. The AI’s colouring must be valid (no clashes) and use as few colours as possible.

 0.0000
Runtime error:

Graph Coloring - 20 vertices, 4 colors

View C++ Code (25202 chars)
#include <algorithm>
#include <array>
#include <chrono>
#include <cstdint>
#include <cstddef>
#include <cstdio>
#include <cstring>
#include <limits>
#include <numeric>
#include <queue>
#include <random>
#include <string>
#include <utility>
#include <vector>

using namespace std;

struct FastScanner {
    static constexpr size_t BUFSIZE = 1 << 20;
    char buf[BUFSIZE];
    size_t idx = 0, size = 0;

    int read() {
        if (idx >= size) {
            size = fread(buf, 1, BUFSIZE, stdin);
            idx = 0;
            if (size == 0) return 0;
        }
        return static_cast<unsigned char>(buf[idx++]);
    }

    template <class T>
    bool nextInt(T& out) {
        int c = read();
        while (c != 0 && c <= ' ') c = read();
        if (c == 0) return false;
        bool neg = false;
        if (c == '-') {
            neg = true;
            c = read();
        }
        T val = 0;
        while (c > ' ') {
            val = val * 10 + (c - '0');
            c = read();
        }
        out = neg ? -val : val;
        return true;
    }
};

static inline void appendInt(string& s, int x) {
    if (x == 0) {
        s.push_back('0');
        return;
    }
    char buf[16];
    int len = 0;
    while (x > 0) {
        buf[len++] = static_cast<char>('0' + (x % 10));
        x /= 10;
    }
    while (len--) s.push_back(buf[len]);
}

struct MoveCandidate {
    int v = -1;
    int color = -1;
    int score = numeric_limits<int>::max();
    long long gain = numeric_limits<long long>::min();
};

struct GraphColoringSolver {
    int n = 0;
    int m = 0;
    int k = 0;
    vector<int> eu;
    vector<int> ev;
    vector<int> deg;
    vector<int> offset;
    vector<int> adj;
    int maxDegree = 0;
    bool hasSelfLoop = false;
    uint64_t seedBase = 0;
    chrono::steady_clock::time_point deadline;

    GraphColoringSolver(int n_, int m_, int k_)
        : n(n_), m(m_), k(k_), eu(m_), ev(m_), deg(n_, 0) {
        seedBase = 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL
            ^ (static_cast<uint64_t>(n) * 0xbf58476d1ce4e5b9ULL)
            ^ (static_cast<uint64_t>(m) * 0x94d049bb133111ebULL)
            ^ static_cast<uint64_t>(k);
    }

    void readEdges(FastScanner& fs) {
        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
            int u = 0, v = 0;
            fs.nextInt(u);
            fs.nextInt(v);
            eu[i] = u;
            ev[i] = v;
            if (u == v) hasSelfLoop = true;
            ++deg[u];
            ++deg[v];
            maxDegree = max(maxDegree, deg[u]);
            maxDegree = max(maxDegree, deg[v]);
        }
    }

    void buildCSR() {
        offset.assign(n + 1, 0);
        for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) offset[i + 1] = offset[i] + deg[i];
        adj.assign(static_cast<size_t>(2) * static_cast<size_t>(m), 0);
        vector<int> cursor = offset;
        for (int i = 0; i < m; ++i) {
            int u = eu[i];
            int v = ev[i];
            adj[cursor[u]++] = v;
            adj[cursor[v]++] = u;
        }
        vector<int>().swap(eu);
        vector<int>().swap(ev);
    }

    void shuffleEqualDegreeRuns(vector<int>& order, mt19937_64& rng) const {
        size_t start = 0;
        while (start < order.size()) {
            size_t end = start + 1;
            while (end < order.size() && deg[order[end]] == deg[order[start]]) {
                ++end;
            }
            if (end - start > 1) {
                auto first = order.begin() + static_cast<vector<int>::difference_type>(start);
                auto last = order.begin() + static_cast<vector<int>::difference_type>(end);
                shuffle(first, last, rng);
            }
            start = end;
        }
    }

    bool solveBipartite(vector<int>& answer) const {
        answer.assign(n, -1);
        vector<int> q;
        q.reserve(n);
        for (int s = 0; s < n; ++s) {
            if (answer[s] != -1) continue;
            answer[s] = 0;
            q.push_back(s);
            for (size_t head = 0; head < q.size(); ++head) {
                int v = q[head];
                for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                    int u = adj[ei];
                    if (answer[u] == -1) {
                        answer[u] = answer[v] ^ 1;
                        q.push_back(u);
                    } else if (answer[u] == answer[v]) {
                        answer.assign(n, 0);
                        return false;
                    }
                }
            }
            q.clear();
        }
        return true;
    }

    bool validateColoring(const vector<int>& color) const {
        if (static_cast<int>(color.size()) != n) return false;
        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            int cv = color[v];
            if (cv < 0 || cv >= k) return false;
            for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                int u = adj[ei];
                if (color[u] == cv) return false;
            }
        }
        return true;
    }

    bool greedyColoring(const vector<int>& order, vector<int>& color, mt19937_64& rng, bool& usedFallback) const {
        color.assign(n, -1);
        usedFallback = false;
        vector<int> classSize(k, 0);
        array<int, 64> seen{};
        array<int, 64> candidates{};

        for (int v : order) {
            seen.fill(0);
            uint64_t forbidden = 0;
            for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                int u = adj[ei];
                int cu = color[u];
                if (cu >= 0) {
                    forbidden |= (1ULL << cu);
                    ++seen[cu];
                }
            }

            int chosen = -1;
            int candCount = 0;

            bool hasFree = false;
            for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                if (((forbidden >> c) & 1ULL) == 0ULL) {
                    hasFree = true;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (hasFree) {
                int bestClass = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                    if (((forbidden >> c) & 1ULL) != 0ULL) continue;
                    int score = classSize[c];
                    if (score < bestClass) {
                        bestClass = score;
                        candCount = 0;
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    } else if (score == bestClass) {
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    }
                }
            } else {
                usedFallback = true;
                int bestSeen = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                int bestClass = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                    int s = seen[c];
                    int cl = classSize[c];
                    if (s < bestSeen || (s == bestSeen && cl < bestClass)) {
                        bestSeen = s;
                        bestClass = cl;
                        candCount = 0;
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    } else if (s == bestSeen && cl == bestClass) {
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    }
                }
            }

            chosen = candidates[static_cast<size_t>(rng() % static_cast<uint64_t>(candCount))];
            color[v] = chosen;
            ++classSize[chosen];
        }
        return true;
    }

    bool dsaturInitialColoring(vector<int>& color, mt19937_64& rng, bool& usedFallback) const {
        color.assign(n, -1);
        usedFallback = false;

        struct Node {
            int sat;
            int deg;
            int v;
            int ver;
        };

        struct NodeCmp {
            bool operator()(const Node& a, const Node& b) const {
                if (a.sat != b.sat) return a.sat < b.sat;
                if (a.deg != b.deg) return a.deg < b.deg;
                return a.v > b.v;
            }
        };

        priority_queue<Node, vector<Node>, NodeCmp> pq;
        vector<uint64_t> satMask(n, 0);
        vector<int> satCount(n, 0);
        vector<int> ver(n, 0);
        vector<int> classSize(k, 0);
        array<int, 64> seen{};
        array<int, 64> candidates{};

        for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
            pq.push(Node{0, deg[v], v, 0});
        }

        int colored = 0;
        while (colored < n) {
            Node cur;
            do {
                if (pq.empty()) return false;
                cur = pq.top();
                pq.pop();
            } while (color[cur.v] != -1 || cur.ver != ver[cur.v] || cur.sat != satCount[cur.v]);

            int v = cur.v;
            uint64_t mask = satMask[v];
            int chosen = -1;
            int candCount = 0;

            bool hasFree = false;
            for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                if (((mask >> c) & 1ULL) == 0ULL) {
                    hasFree = true;
                    break;
                }
            }

            if (hasFree) {
                int bestClass = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                    if (((mask >> c) & 1ULL) != 0ULL) continue;
                    int score = classSize[c];
                    if (score < bestClass) {
                        bestClass = score;
                        candCount = 0;
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    } else if (score == bestClass) {
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    }
                }
            } else {
                usedFallback = true;
                seen.fill(0);
                for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                    int u = adj[ei];
                    int cu = color[u];
                    if (cu >= 0) ++seen[cu];
                }
                int bestSeen = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                int bestClass = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                    int s = seen[c];
                    int cl = classSize[c];
                    if (s < bestSeen || (s == bestSeen && cl < bestClass)) {
                        bestSeen = s;
                        bestClass = cl;
                        candCount = 0;
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    } else if (s == bestSeen && cl == bestClass) {
                        candidates[candCount++] = c;
                    }
                }
            }

            chosen = candidates[static_cast<size_t>(rng() % static_cast<uint64_t>(candCount))];
            color[v] = chosen;
            ++classSize[chosen];
            ++colored;

            uint64_t bit = 1ULL << chosen;
            for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                int u = adj[ei];
                if (color[u] != -1) continue;
                if ((satMask[u] & bit) == 0ULL) {
                    satMask[u] |= bit;
                    ++satCount[u];
                    ++ver[u];
                    pq.push(Node{satCount[u], deg[u], u, ver[u]});
                }
            }
        }
        return true;
    }

    long long runLocalSearch(vector<int>& color, mt19937_64& rng) const {
        vector<int> counts(static_cast<size_t>(n) * static_cast<size_t>(k), 0);
        vector<int> conflictCount(n, 0);
        vector<int> conflictList;
        vector<int> posInConflict(n, -1);
        vector<int> classSize(k, 0);
        vector<int> tabuColor(n, -1);
        vector<int> tabuExpire(n, 0);
        conflictList.reserve(n);

        auto addConflict = [&](int v) {
            if (posInConflict[v] != -1) return;
            posInConflict[v] = static_cast<int>(conflictList.size());
            conflictList.push_back(v);
        };

        auto removeConflict = [&](int v) {
            int idx = posInConflict[v];
            if (idx == -1) return;
            int back = conflictList.back();
            conflictList[idx] = back;
            posInConflict[back] = idx;
            conflictList.pop_back();
            posInConflict[v] = -1;
        };

        auto buildCounts = [&]() {
            fill(counts.begin(), counts.end(), 0);
            fill(conflictCount.begin(), conflictCount.end(), 0);
            fill(posInConflict.begin(), posInConflict.end(), -1);
            conflictList.clear();
            fill(classSize.begin(), classSize.end(), 0);
            fill(tabuColor.begin(), tabuColor.end(), -1);
            fill(tabuExpire.begin(), tabuExpire.end(), 0);

            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                ++classSize[color[v]];
            }

            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                int cv = color[v];
                for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                    int u = adj[ei];
                    counts[static_cast<size_t>(u) * static_cast<size_t>(k) + static_cast<size_t>(cv)]++;
                }
            }

            long long totalConflicts = 0;
            for (int v = 0; v < n; ++v) {
                conflictCount[v] = counts[static_cast<size_t>(v) * static_cast<size_t>(k) + static_cast<size_t>(color[v])];
                totalConflicts += conflictCount[v];
                if (conflictCount[v] > 0) addConflict(v);
            }
            return totalConflicts / 2;
        };

        long long totalConflicts = buildCounts();
        if (totalConflicts == 0) return 0;

        auto applyMove = [&](int v, int newColor, int step) {
            int oldColor = color[v];
            if (oldColor == newColor) return;
            size_t baseV = static_cast<size_t>(v) * static_cast<size_t>(k);
            int oldConf = conflictCount[v];
            int newConf = counts[baseV + static_cast<size_t>(newColor)];
            totalConflicts += static_cast<long long>(newConf) - static_cast<long long>(oldConf);

            color[v] = newColor;
            --classSize[oldColor];
            ++classSize[newColor];
            tabuColor[v] = oldColor;
            tabuExpire[v] = step + 7 + (deg[v] % 11);

            for (int ei = offset[v]; ei < offset[v + 1]; ++ei) {
                int u = adj[ei];
                size_t baseU = static_cast<size_t>(u) * static_cast<size_t>(k);
                int before = conflictCount[u];
                counts[baseU + static_cast<size_t>(oldColor)]--;
                counts[baseU + static_cast<size_t>(newColor)]++;
                if (color[u] == oldColor) {
                    --conflictCount[u];
                } else if (color[u] == newColor) {
                    ++conflictCount[u];
                }
                if (before == 0 && conflictCount[u] > 0) addConflict(u);
                else if (before > 0 && conflictCount[u] == 0) removeConflict(u);
            }

            int beforeV = conflictCount[v];
            conflictCount[v] = newConf;
            if (beforeV == 0 && newConf > 0) addConflict(v);
            else if (beforeV > 0 && newConf == 0) removeConflict(v);
        };

        auto evaluateVertex = [&](int v, int step) -> MoveCandidate {
            MoveCandidate best;
            int cur = color[v];
            int curConf = conflictCount[v];
            size_t base = static_cast<size_t>(v) * static_cast<size_t>(k);

            int bestColor = -1;
            int bestActual = numeric_limits<int>::max();
            int bestPriority = numeric_limits<int>::max();
            int bestTie = numeric_limits<int>::max();

            int bestOtherColor = -1;
            int bestOtherActual = numeric_limits<int>::max();
            int bestOtherPriority = numeric_limits<int>::max();
            int bestOtherTie = numeric_limits<int>::max();

            for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                int actual = counts[base + static_cast<size_t>(c)];
                int priority = actual;
                if (step < tabuExpire[v] && c == tabuColor[v]) priority += 2;
                int tie = classSize[c];

                if (bestColor == -1 || priority < bestPriority ||
                    (priority == bestPriority && (tie < bestTie || (tie == bestTie && c < bestColor)))) {
                    bestColor = c;
                    bestActual = actual;
                    bestPriority = priority;
                    bestTie = tie;
                }

                if (c != cur && (bestOtherColor == -1 || priority < bestOtherPriority ||
                                 (priority == bestOtherPriority && (tie < bestOtherTie || (tie == bestOtherTie && c < bestOtherColor))))) {
                    bestOtherColor = c;
                    bestOtherActual = actual;
                    bestOtherPriority = priority;
                    bestOtherTie = tie;
                }
            }

            int chosenColor = bestColor;
            int chosenActual = bestActual;
            if (curConf > 0 && chosenColor == cur && bestOtherColor != -1) {
                chosenColor = bestOtherColor;
                chosenActual = bestOtherActual;
            }

            best.v = v;
            best.color = chosenColor;
            best.score = chosenActual;
            best.gain = static_cast<long long>(curConf) - static_cast<long long>(chosenActual);
            return best;
        };

        auto pickMove = [&](int step) -> MoveCandidate {
            MoveCandidate best;
            int listSize = static_cast<int>(conflictList.size());
            if (listSize == 0) return best;
            int sampleSize = 0;
            if (n <= 2000) sampleSize = 8;
            else if (n <= 100000) sampleSize = 16;
            else sampleSize = 24;
            sampleSize = min(sampleSize, listSize);

            auto consider = [&](int v) {
                MoveCandidate cand = evaluateVertex(v, step);
                if (cand.v < 0 || cand.color < 0) return;
                if (best.v < 0 ||
                    cand.gain > best.gain ||
                    (cand.gain == best.gain && (cand.score < best.score ||
                                                (cand.score == best.score && deg[cand.v] > deg[best.v])))) {
                    best = cand;
                }
            };

            if (sampleSize == listSize) {
                for (int v : conflictList) consider(v);
            } else {
                for (int i = 0; i < sampleSize; ++i) {
                    int v = conflictList[static_cast<size_t>(rng() % static_cast<uint64_t>(listSize))];
                    consider(v);
                }
            }
            return best;
        };

        auto performKick = [&](int step) -> int {
            if (conflictList.empty()) return 0;
            int kickCount = 1 + static_cast<int>(rng() % 3ULL);
            for (int i = 0; i < kickCount && !conflictList.empty(); ++i) {
                int v = conflictList[static_cast<size_t>(rng() % static_cast<uint64_t>(conflictList.size()))];
                int cur = color[v];
                size_t base = static_cast<size_t>(v) * static_cast<size_t>(k);
                int bestColor = -1;
                int bestScore = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                int bestTie = numeric_limits<int>::max();
                for (int c = 0; c < k; ++c) {
                    if (c == cur) continue;
                    int score = counts[base + static_cast<size_t>(c)];
                    int tie = classSize[c];
                    if (bestColor == -1 || score < bestScore ||
                        (score == bestScore && (tie < bestTie || (tie == bestTie && c < bestColor)))) {
                        bestColor = c;
                        bestScore = score;
                        bestTie = tie;
                    }
                }
                if (bestColor != -1) applyMove(v, bestColor, step + i + 1);
            }
            return kickCount;
        };

        int moveLimit = static_cast<int>(min<long long>(3000000LL, max<long long>(200000LL, 10LL * n)));
        int kickThreshold = max(2000, n / 60);
        int stagnant = 0;
        int moves = 0;

        while (totalConflicts > 0 && moves < moveLimit) {
            if ((moves & 1023) == 0 && chrono::steady_clock::now() >= deadline) break;
            if (conflictList.empty()) break;

            MoveCandidate cand = pickMove(moves);
            if (cand.v < 0 || cand.color < 0) break;
            long long before = totalConflicts;
            applyMove(cand.v, cand.color, moves);
            ++moves;

            if (totalConflicts < before) stagnant = 0;
            else ++stagnant;

            if (stagnant >= kickThreshold) {
                moves += performKick(moves);
                stagnant = 0;
            }
        }

        return totalConflicts;
    }

    bool solveSmall(vector<int>& answer) const {
        int attempts = 8;
        vector<int> bestColoring;
        long long bestConf = numeric_limits<long long>::max();

        for (int attempt = 0; attempt < attempts; ++attempt) {
            if (chrono::steady_clock::now() >= deadline) break;
            mt19937_64 rng(seedBase + 0x9e3779b97f4a7c15ULL * static_cast<uint64_t>(attempt + 1));
            vector<int> color;
            bool usedFallback = false;
            dsaturInitialColoring(color, rng, usedFallback);
            if (!usedFallback) {
                answer = move(color);
                return true;
            }
            long long conf = runLocalSearch(color, rng);
            if (conf == 0) {
                if (validateColoring(color)) {
                    answer = move(color);
                    return true;
                }
            } else if (conf < bestConf) {
                bestConf = conf;
                bestColoring = color;
            }
        }

        if (!bestColoring.empty()) answer = move(bestColoring);
        else answer.assign(n, 0);
        return false;
    }

    bool solveGeneral(vector<int>& answer) const {
        vector<int> baseOrder(n);
        iota(baseOrder.begin(), baseOrder.end(), 0);
        sort(baseOrder.begin(), baseOrder.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
            if (deg[a] != deg[b]) return deg[a] > deg[b];
            return a < b;
        });

        int attempts = (n <= 100000 ? 6 : 4);
        vector<int> bestColoring;
        long long bestConf = numeric_limits<long long>::max();

        for (int attempt = 0; attempt < attempts; ++attempt) {
            if (chrono::steady_clock::now() >= deadline) break;
            mt19937_64 rng(seedBase + 0x517cc1b727220a95ULL * static_cast<uint64_t>(attempt + 1));
            vector<int> order = baseOrder;
            if (attempt > 0 && n <= 200000) shuffleEqualDegreeRuns(order, rng);

            vector<int> color;
            bool usedFallback = false;
            greedyColoring(order, color, rng, usedFallback);
            if (!usedFallback) {
                answer = move(color);
                return true;
            }

            long long conf = runLocalSearch(color, rng);
            if (conf == 0) {
                if (validateColoring(color)) {
                    answer = move(color);
                    return true;
                }
            } else if (conf < bestConf) {
                bestConf = conf;
                bestColoring = color;
            }
        }

        if (!bestColoring.empty()) answer = move(bestColoring);
        else answer.assign(n, 0);
        return false;
    }

    bool solve(vector<int>& answer) const {
        answer.assign(n, 0);
        if (n == 0) {
            return true;
        }
        if (k <= 0) {
            return false;
        }
        if (hasSelfLoop) {
            return false;
        }
        if (m == 0) {
            return true;
        }
        if (n <= k) {
            answer.resize(n);
            for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) answer[i] = i;
            return true;
        }
        if (k == 1) {
            return false;
        }

        if (maxDegree < k) {
            vector<int> order(n);
            iota(order.begin(), order.end(), 0);
            sort(order.begin(), order.end(), [&](int a, int b) {
                if (deg[a] != deg[b]) return deg[a] > deg[b];
                return a < b;
            });
            mt19937_64 rng(seedBase ^ 0x243f6a8885a308d3ULL);
            bool usedFallback = false;
            greedyColoring(order, answer, rng, usedFallback);
            return true;
        }

        if (solveBipartite(answer)) {
            return true;
        }

        if (n <= 8000) {
            return solveSmall(answer);
        }
        return solveGeneral(answer);
    }
};

int main() {
    FastScanner fs;
    int n = 0, m = 0, k = 0;
    if (!fs.nextInt(n)) return 0;
    fs.nextInt(m);
    fs.nextInt(k);

    GraphColoringSolver solver(n, m, k);
    solver.readEdges(fs);
    solver.buildCSR();
    solver.deadline = chrono::steady_clock::now() + chrono::seconds(265);

    vector<int> answer;
    solver.solve(answer);
    if (static_cast<int>(answer.size()) != n) answer.assign(n, 0);

    string out;
    out.reserve(static_cast<size_t>(n) * 4 + 2);
    for (int i = 0; i < n; ++i) {
        if (i) out.push_back(' ');
        appendInt(out, answer[i]);
    }
    out.push_back('\n');
    fwrite(out.data(), 1, out.size(), stdout);
    return 0;
}

Test 28: Minimum Vertex Cover (Rust) Score: 0.00 / 17
Typical Prompt:
You are writing Rust code to solve the Minimum Vertex Cover problem.

Your code may see graphs with up to 2M vertices and 15M edges.

**Problem:**
Find the minimum set of vertices such that every edge has at least one endpoint in the set.
This is NP-Hard and requires sophisticated algorithms for larger instances.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
vertex_count
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the cover, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All edges are covered by selected vertices
- Vertex set size is minimized (or near-minimized for large graphs)
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Not all edges are covered
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**Rust Compiler:**
rustc 1.92.0 (ded5c06cf 2025-12-08) | edition=2024 | host: x86_64-pc-windows-msvc

Be aware that default warnings are enabled and will cause a compilation failure,
so ensure that you write warning-free code.

Write complete, compilable Rust code with a main() function.

Test 29: Maximum Clique (C#) Score: 18.00 / 18
Typical Prompt:
You are writing C# code to solve the Maximum Clique problem, which is NP Hard.

Your C# maximum clique finder will be tested with graphs ranging from sparse to very dense instances.
The same algorithm must work efficiently across ALL graph complexities, from 20 vertices to 1 million.

**Problem:**
Find the largest complete subgraph (clique) in an undirected graph.
A clique is a set of vertices where every pair of vertices is connected by an edge.

**Input format (stdin):**
```
num_vertices num_edges
u v (for each edge, 0-indexed)
```

**Output format (stdout):**
```
clique_size
vertex_1 vertex_2 ... vertex_k (vertices in the maximum clique, space-separated)
```

**Success Criteria:**
- All vertices in output form a complete subgraph
- No larger clique exists in the graph
- Output format is correct

**Failure Criteria:**
- Output vertices don't form a clique
- Larger clique exists
- Timeout without solution
- Invalid output format

**Requirements:**
1. Program must compile with .NET/csc
2. Read from stdin, write to stdout
3. Handle variable graph sizes and densities
4. Complete within 5 minutes
5. Must handle varying graph complexities efficiently

Hints:
- You may wish to drop to a hueristic if time is running out for partial score.
- Use threads to process in parallel.

**Environment:**
Platform: Windows 11 (build 10.0.26200)
Architecture: AMD64
CPU: Intel64 Family 6 Model 158 Stepping 10, GenuineIntel
Core count: 12
Memory: 31.93 GB
SIMD: AVX, AVX2, SSE, SSE2, SSE3, SSE4.1, SSE4.2, SSSE3
SIMD detail: AVX=yes, AVX-512F=no, AVX2=yes, SSE=yes, SSE2=yes, SSE3=yes, SSE4.1=yes, SSE4.2=yes, SSSE3=yes

**C# Compiler:**
C# (.NET SDK 10.0.201; SDKs 10.0.201) | csc 5.3.0-2.26153.122 (4d3023de605a78ba3e59e50c657eed70f125c68a) | max LangVersion 14

Write complete, compilable C# code with a Main method.
 Prompt Changes:
Prompt parameters change between subpasses (increasing difficulty)
Subpass 0
